You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_36.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 36: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[36]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_36.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 36: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Evidence Contract Gate enforcement (I-ecg-002).
2	
3	Given a (contract, pool, report) triple, evaluate whether the report
4	addresses every contract expectation. Pure function; no I/O.
5	
6	`assert_generation_has_contract` is the IMPORT-time guarantee: callers
7	that try to run generation without a contract get a structured refusal.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
13	
14	from polaris_graph.evidence_contract.schema import EvidenceContract, Jurisdiction
15	from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import extract_tokens
16	from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import VerifiedReport
17	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool, Source, SourceTier
18	
19	JURISDICTION_DOMAINS: dict[Jurisdiction, frozenset[str]] = {
20	    Jurisdiction.CA: frozenset({"canada.ca", "gc.ca", "cochrane.org", "hc-sc.gc.ca"}),
21	    Jurisdiction.US: frozenset({"fda.gov", "nih.gov", "cdc.gov", "nlm.nih.gov"}),
22	    Jurisdiction.EU: frozenset({"ema.europa.eu", "efsa.europa.eu", "europa.eu"}),
23	    Jurisdiction.UK: frozenset({"nice.org.uk", "gov.uk", "mhra.gov.uk"}),
24	    Jurisdiction.GLOBAL: frozenset({"who.int", "cochrane.org"}),
25	}
26	
27	
28	class ContractRequiredError(Exception):
29	    """Raised when generation is invoked without an EvidenceContract."""
30	
31	
32	class GateVerdict(BaseModel):
33	    passed: bool
34	    failures: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
35	    contract_id: str
36	    report_id: str
37	
38	
39	def assert_generation_has_contract(
40	    contract: EvidenceContract | None, *, report_id: str | None = None
41	) -> None:
42	    if contract is None:
43	        raise ContractRequiredError(
44	            f"Evidence Contract required: generation cannot proceed without a contract"
45	            f"{f' (report_id={report_id!r})' if report_id else ''}"
46	        )
47	
48	
49	def _kept_sentence_texts(report: VerifiedReport) -> list[str]:
50	    out: list[str] = []
51	    for section in report.sections:
52	        if section.section_status == "dropped":
53	            continue
54	        for sentence in section.verified_sentences:
55	            if sentence.verifier_pass:
56	                out.append(sentence.sentence_text.lower())
57	    return out
58	
59	
60	def _sentence_cited_ids(sentence) -> set[str]:
61	    ids: set[str] = set()
62	    for tok in extract_tokens(sentence.sentence_text):
63	        ids.add(tok.source_id)
64	    for raw in sentence.provenance_tokens:
65	        for tok in extract_tokens(raw):
66	            ids.add(tok.source_id)
67	    return ids
68	
69	
70	def _cited_sources(report: VerifiedReport, pool: EvidencePool) -> list[Source]:
71	    cited_ids: set[str] = set()
72	    for section in report.sections:
73	        if section.section_status == "dropped":
74	            continue
75	        for sentence in section.verified_sentences:
76	            if not sentence.verifier_pass:
77	                continue
78	            cited_ids |= _sentence_cited_ids(sentence)
79	    return [s for s in pool.sources if s.source_id in cited_ids]
80	
81	
82	def _claim_covering_sources(
83	    report: VerifiedReport, pool: EvidencePool, claim_statement_lower: str
84	) -> list[Source]:
85	    """Sources cited by sentences whose text contains the claim statement substring."""
86	    cited_ids: set[str] = set()
87	    for section in report.sections:
88	        if section.section_status == "dropped":
89	            continue
90	        for sentence in section.verified_sentences:
91	            if not sentence.verifier_pass:
92	                continue
93	            if claim_statement_lower in sentence.sentence_text.lower():
94	                cited_ids |= _sentence_cited_ids(sentence)
95	    return [s for s in pool.sources if s.source_id in cited_ids]
96	
97	
98	def _domain_matches_jurisdiction(domain: str, jurisdiction: Jurisdiction) -> bool:
99	    domain_lower = domain.lower()
100	    return any(
101	        domain_lower == d or domain_lower.endswith("." + d)
102	        for d in JURISDICTION_DOMAINS.get(jurisdiction, frozenset())
103	    )
104	
105	
106	def evaluate_contract(
107	    contract: EvidenceContract, pool: EvidencePool, report: VerifiedReport
108	) -> GateVerdict:
109	    failures: list[str] = []
110	    sentence_texts = _kept_sentence_texts(report)
111	    blob = " ".join(sentence_texts)
112	    cited = _cited_sources(report, pool)
113	
114	    for entity in contract.expected_entities:
115	        names = [entity.name.lower()] + [a.lower() for a in entity.aliases if a.strip()]
116	        if not any(n in blob for n in names if n):
117	            failures.append(f"entity_not_covered:{entity.name}")
118	
119	    for claim in contract.expected_claims:
120	        statement_lower = claim.statement.lower()
121	        if not any(statement_lower in t for t in sentence_texts):
122	            failures.append(f"claim_not_covered:{claim.claim_id}")
123	        claim_sources = _claim_covering_sources(report, pool, statement_lower)
124	        for jurisdiction in claim.required_jurisdictions:
125	            if not any(_domain_matches_jurisdiction(s.domain, jurisdiction) for s in claim_sources):
126	                failures.append(
127	                    f"jurisdiction_not_covered:{claim.claim_id}:{jurisdiction.value}"
128	                )
129	
130	    cov = contract.expected_source_coverage
131	    counts = {SourceTier.T1: 0, SourceTier.T2: 0, SourceTier.T3: 0}
132	    for s in cited:
133	        counts[s.tier] += 1
134	    if counts[SourceTier.T1] < cov.tier_t1_min:
135	        failures.append(f"insufficient_t1_sources:{counts[SourceTier.T1]}<{cov.tier_t1_min}")
136	    if counts[SourceTier.T2] < cov.tier_t2_min:
137	        failures.append(f"insufficient_t2_sources:{counts[SourceTier.T2]}<{cov.tier_t2_min}")
138	    if counts[SourceTier.T3] < cov.tier_t3_min:
139	        failures.append(f"insufficient_t3_sources:{counts[SourceTier.T3]}<{cov.tier_t3_min}")
140	
141	    return GateVerdict(
142	        passed=not failures,
143	        failures=failures,
144	        contract_id=contract.contract_id,
145	        report_id=report.report_id,
146	    )
147

1	"""Evidence Contract version migration registry (I-ecg-004).
2	
3	When v2 lands, register `("1.0", "2.0"): _v1_to_v2` here. The migration
4	path walker chains entries; identity is short-circuited.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from typing import Callable
10	
11	
12	class ContractMigrationError(Exception):
13	    """Raised when no migration path exists between two contract versions."""
14	
15	
16	MIGRATIONS: dict[tuple[str, str], Callable[[dict], dict]] = {}
17	
18	
19	def migrate_contract(raw: dict, target_version: str = "1.0") -> dict:
20	    """Migrate a contract dict from its declared version to target_version."""
21	    source = raw.get("contract_version")
22	    if not isinstance(source, str):
23	        raise ContractMigrationError(
24	            f"contract_version missing or non-string: {source!r}"
25	        )
26	    if source == target_version:
27	        return dict(raw)
28	    seen = {source}
29	    current = dict(raw)
30	    while current.get("contract_version") != target_version:
31	        cur_version = current.get("contract_version")
32	        next_step = next(
33	            (
34	                (frm, to, fn)
35	                for (frm, to), fn in MIGRATIONS.items()
36	                if frm == cur_version and frm != to and to not in seen
37	            ),
38	            None,
39	        )
40	        if next_step is None:
41	            raise ContractMigrationError(
42	                f"no migration path from {cur_version!r} to {target_version!r}"
43	            )
44	        _, to, fn = next_step
45	        current = fn(current)
46	        current["contract_version"] = to
47	        seen.add(to)
48	    return current
49

1	"""Pre-generation evidence-expectation contract (I-ecg-001).
2	
3	Operator declares, BEFORE a research run starts, what entities + claims
4	+ jurisdictions + source-coverage the eventual report MUST address.
5	I-ecg-002 enforces (gates generation against this contract).
6	
7	DO NOT confuse with `polaris_v6.schemas.evidence_contract.EvidenceContract`
8	which is the post-run artifact (run output). Different concepts; same
9	class name acceptable since import paths disambiguate.
10	
11	Jurisdiction is geographic (CA / US / EU / UK / GLOBAL). The mapping
12	from regulatory-agency codes (FDA / EMA / NICE / HC / MHRA / TGA) to
13	these jurisdictions is owned by I-ecg-002 (gate enforcement layer).
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import uuid
19	from datetime import datetime, timezone
20	from enum import Enum
21	from typing import Literal
22	
23	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, model_validator
24	
25	
26	class Jurisdiction(str, Enum):
27	    CA = "CA"
28	    US = "US"
29	    EU = "EU"
30	    UK = "UK"
31	    GLOBAL = "GLOBAL"
32	
33	
34	EntityType = Literal["drug", "condition", "intervention", "population", "outcome"]
35	
36	
37	class ExpectedEntity(BaseModel):
38	    """A named entity (drug, condition, intervention, etc.) the report MUST address."""
39	
40	    name: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
41	    aliases: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, max_length=20)
42	    entity_type: EntityType
43	
44	
45	class ExpectedClaim(BaseModel):
46	    """A claim the report MUST make a verdict on."""
47	
48	    claim_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)
49	    statement: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=1000)
50	    expected_entities: list[str] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=20)
51	    required_jurisdictions: list[Jurisdiction] = Field(min_length=1)
52	
53	
54	class ExpectedSourceCoverage(BaseModel):
55	    """Minimum source counts per evidence-tier the report MUST cite."""
56	
57	    tier_t1_min: int = Field(ge=0, le=100)
58	    tier_t2_min: int = Field(ge=0, le=100)
59	    tier_t3_min: int = Field(ge=0, le=100)
60	
61	
62	class EvidenceContract(BaseModel):
63	    """Top-level expectation contract bound to one research question."""
64	
65	    contract_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
66	    contract_version: Literal["1.0"] = "1.0"
67	    research_question: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=2000)
68	    expected_entities: list[ExpectedEntity] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=50)
69	    expected_claims: list[ExpectedClaim] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=50)
70	    expected_source_coverage: ExpectedSourceCoverage
71	    jurisdictions: list[Jurisdiction] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=10)
72	    created_at_utc: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
73	    created_by: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
74	
75	    @model_validator(mode="after")
76	    def _internal_consistency(self) -> "EvidenceContract":
77	        names = [e.name for e in self.expected_entities]
78	        if len(set(names)) != len(names):
79	            dups = sorted({n for n in names if names.count(n) > 1})
80	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate entity name(s): {dups}")
81	        ids = [c.claim_id for c in self.expected_claims]
82	        if len(set(ids)) != len(ids):
83	            dups = sorted({i for i in ids if ids.count(i) > 1})
84	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate claim_id(s): {dups}")
85	        declared = set(names)
86	        contract_jurs = set(self.jurisdictions)
87	        for claim in self.expected_claims:
88	            for ref in claim.expected_entities:
89	                if ref not in declared:
90	                    raise ValueError(
91	                        f"claim {claim.claim_id!r} references undeclared entity {ref!r}"
92	                    )
93	            extra = set(claim.required_jurisdictions) - contract_jurs
94	            if extra:
95	                raise ValueError(
96	                    f"claim {claim.claim_id!r} required_jurisdictions {sorted(j.value for j in extra)} "
97	                    f"not in contract jurisdictions"
98	                )
99	        return self
100	
101	
102	class EvidenceContractError(BaseModel):
103	    """Returned when a contract fails validation (parallel to other slice errors)."""
104	
105	    error: bool = True
106	    code: str
107	    message: str
108	    contract_id: str | None = None
109

1	"""
2	DOCX exporter for polaris graph research reports.
3	
4	Converts a ResearchState result dict (as stored in outputs/polaris_graph/*.json)
5	into a professionally styled Microsoft Word (.docx) document with corporate
6	typography, inline superscript citations, bibliography, quality summary,
7	and audit certificate.
8	
9	Requires: python-docx >= 1.1.0
10	
11	LAW VI: All configurable parameters sourced from environment variables.
12	LAW V:  snake_case throughout, one-responsibility module.
13	"""
14	
15	import base64
16	import hashlib
17	import io
18	import json
19	import logging
20	import os
21	import re
22	from datetime import datetime, timezone
23	from pathlib import Path
24	from typing import Optional
25	
26	from docx import Document
27	from docx.enum.section import WD_ORIENT
28	from docx.enum.style import WD_STYLE_TYPE
29	from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT
30	from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
31	from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
32	from docx.oxml.ns import qn
33	from docx.shared import Cm, Inches, Pt, RGBColor
34	
35	from dotenv import load_dotenv
36	
37	load_dotenv()
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	# Configuration from environment (LAW VI)
44	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
45	
46	DOCX_BODY_FONT = os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_FONT", "Calibri")
47	DOCX_BODY_FONT_SIZE_PT = int(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_FONT_SIZE", "11"))
48	DOCX_HEADING_FONT = os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_HEADING_FONT", "Calibri")
49	DOCX_HEADING1_SIZE_PT = int(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_HEADING1_SIZE", "18"))
50	DOCX_HEADING2_SIZE_PT = int(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_HEADING2_SIZE", "14"))
51	DOCX_HEADING3_SIZE_PT = int(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_HEADING3_SIZE", "12"))
52	DOCX_TITLE_SIZE_PT = int(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_TITLE_SIZE", "26"))
53	DOCX_MARGIN_CM = float(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_MARGIN_CM", "2.54"))
54	DOCX_ACCENT_COLOR = os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_ACCENT_COLOR", "1F4E79")
55	DOCX_PIPELINE_VERSION = os.getenv("POLARIS_PIPELINE_VERSION", "polaris-graph-v1")
56	DOCX_LINE_SPACING = float(os.getenv("POLARIS_DOCX_LINE_SPACING", "1.15"))
57	
58	
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	# Markdown inline patterns
61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	# Matches **bold** text
64	_BOLD_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\*\*(.+?)\*\*")
65	# Matches *italic* text (but not **bold**)
66	_ITALIC_PATTERN = re.compile(r"(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(.+?)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*)")
67	# Matches inline citations like [1], [23], [1][2]
68	_CITATION_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
69	# Matches markdown headings at start of line
70	_HEADING_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^(#{1,3})\s+(.+)$", re.MULTILINE)
71	# Matches markdown bullet list items
72	_BULLET_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^\s*[-*+]\s+(.+)$", re.MULTILINE)
73	# Matches markdown numbered list items
74	_NUMBERED_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\.\s+(.+)$", re.MULTILINE)
75	# Matches markdown table separator row (e.g., | --- | --- |)
76	_TABLE_SEP_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^\s*\|[\s\-:]+(\|[\s\-:]+)+\|?\s*$")
77	# Matches markdown table row (e.g., | cell1 | cell2 |)
78	_TABLE_ROW_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^\s*\|(.+)\|\s*$")
79	# Matches base64 image in markdown (e.g., ![alt](data:image/png;base64,...))
80	_BASE64_IMG_PATTERN = re.compile(
81	    r"!\[([^\]]*)\]\(data:image/(\w+);base64,([A-Za-z0-9+/=\s]+)\)"
82	)
83	# Matches figure caption (e.g., *Figure 1: description*)
84	_FIGURE_CAPTION_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^\s*\*(.+)\*\s*$")
85	
86	
87	def _parse_accent_color(hex_str: str) -> RGBColor:
88	    """Parse a 6-char hex string into an RGBColor. Falls back to dark blue."""
89	    try:
90	        hex_str = hex_str.lstrip("#")
91	        if len(hex_str) != 6:
92	            raise ValueError(f"Invalid hex color length: {hex_str}")
93	        return RGBColor(
94	            int(hex_str[0:2], 16),
95	            int(hex_str[2:4], 16),
96	            int(hex_str[4:6], 16),
97	        )
98	    except (ValueError, IndexError):
99	        logger.warning(
100	            "Invalid POLARIS_DOCX_ACCENT_COLOR '%s', falling back to #1F4E79",
101	            hex_str,
102	        )
103	        return RGBColor(0x1F, 0x4E, 0x79)
104	
105	
106	class DocxExporter:
107	    """
108	    Generates professionally styled Microsoft Word documents from
109	    POLARIS research pipeline output.
110	
111	    Usage::
112	
113	        exporter = DocxExporter()
114	        output_path = exporter.export(report_data, Path("outputs/report.docx"))
115	    """
116	
117	    def __init__(self) -> None:
118	        self._accent_color = _parse_accent_color(DOCX_ACCENT_COLOR)
119	        self._body_font = DOCX_BODY_FONT
120	        self._body_size = Pt(DOCX_BODY_FONT_SIZE_PT)
121	        self._heading_font = DOCX_HEADING_FONT
122	        self._line_spacing = DOCX_LINE_SPACING
123	        self._margin = Cm(DOCX_MARGIN_CM)
124	
125	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
126	    # Public API
127	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
128	
129	    def export(self, report_data: dict, output_path: Path) -> Path:
130	        """
131	        Export a POLARIS research result dict to a styled .docx file.
132	
133	        Args:
134	            report_data: The full ResearchState dict (deserialized JSON).
135	                         Expected keys: vector_id, original_query (or query),
136	                         status, final_report, bibliography, quality_metrics,
137	                         sections, evidence (or evidence_count),
138	                         iteration_count, timestamps.
139	            output_path:  Destination path for the .docx file. Parent
140	                         directory is created if it does not exist.
141	
142	        Returns:
143	            The resolved Path where the document was written.
144	
145	        Raises:
146	            ValueError: If report_data is missing critical keys.
147	            OSError:    If the file cannot be written.
148	        """
149	        output_path = Path(output_path)
150	        self._validate_report_data(report_data)
151	
152	        doc = Document()
153	        self._configure_styles(doc)
154	        self._set_margins(doc)
155	
156	        # --- Title Page ---
157	        self._add_title_page(doc, report_data)
158	        doc.add_page_break()
159	
160	        # --- Table of Contents ---
161	        self._add_table_of_contents(doc)
162	        doc.add_page_break()
163	
164	        # --- Report Body ---
165	        self._add_report_body(doc, report_data)
166	        doc.add_page_break()
167	
168	        # --- Bibliography ---
169	        self._add_bibliography(doc, report_data)
170	        doc.add_page_break()
171	
172	        # --- Quality Summary ---
173	        self._add_quality_summary(doc, report_data)
174	        doc.add_page_break()
175	
176	        # --- Audit Certificate ---
177	        self._add_audit_certificate(doc, report_data)
178	
179	        # Write to disk
180	        output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
181	        doc.save(str(output_path))
182	        logger.info(
183	            "DOCX exported: %s (%d bytes)",
184	            output_path,
185	            output_path.stat().st_size,
186	        )
187	        return output_path
188	
189	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
190	    # Validation
191	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
192	
193	    @staticmethod
194	    def _validate_report_data(report_data: dict) -> None:
195	        """Ensure minimum required keys are present."""
196	        required_keys = {"vector_id", "status"}
197	        missing = required_keys - set(report_data.keys())
198	        if missing:
199	            raise ValueError(
200	                f"report_data missing required keys: {missing}"
201	            )
202	        # At least one of these must be present for the report body
203	        has_body = bool(
204	            report_data.get("final_report")
205	            or report_data.get("sections")
206	        )
207	        if not has_body:
208	            raise ValueError(
209	                "report_data must contain 'final_report' or 'sections'"
210	            )
211	
212	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
213	    # Style configuration
214	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
215	
216	    def _configure_styles(self, doc: Document) -> None:
217	        """Set up corporate typography for all built-in styles."""
218	        style = doc.styles["Normal"]
219	        font = style.font
220	        font.name = self._body_font
221	        font.size = self._body_size
222	        font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x33, 0x33, 0x33)
223	
224	        paragraph_format = style.paragraph_format
225	        paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(6)
226	        paragraph_format.line_spacing = self._line_spacing
227	
228	        # Heading styles
229	        heading_configs = [
230	            ("Heading 1", DOCX_HEADING1_SIZE_PT, True),
231	            ("Heading 2", DOCX_HEADING2_SIZE_PT, True),
232	            ("Heading 3", DOCX_HEADING3_SIZE_PT, False),
233	        ]
234	        for style_name, size_pt, bold in heading_configs:
235	            if style_name in doc.styles:
236	                h_style = doc.styles[style_name]
237	            else:
238	                h_style = doc.styles.add_style(
239	                    style_name, WD_STYLE_TYPE.PARAGRAPH
240	                )
241	            h_font = h_style.font
242	            h_font.name = self._heading_font
243	            h_font.size = Pt(size_pt)
244	            h_font.bold = bold
245	            h_font.color.rgb = self._accent_color
246	            h_pf = h_style.paragraph_format
247	            h_pf.space_before = Pt(18)
248	            h_pf.space_after = Pt(8)
249	            h_pf.keep_with_next = True
250	
251	        # Title style
252	        if "Title" in doc.styles:
253	            t_style = doc.styles["Title"]
254	            t_font = t_style.font
255	            t_font.name = self._heading_font
256	            t_font.size = Pt(DOCX_TITLE_SIZE_PT)
257	            t_font.bold = True
258	            t_font.color.rgb = self._accent_color
259	
260	    def _set_margins(self, doc: Document) -> None:
261	        """Apply uniform margins to all sections."""
262	        for section in doc.sections:
263	            section.top_margin = self._margin
264	            section.bottom_margin = self._margin
265	            section.left_margin = self._margin
266	            section.right_margin = self._margin
267	
268	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
269	    # Title Page
270	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
271	
272	    def _add_title_page(self, doc: Document, report_data: dict) -> None:
273	        """Build a title page with query, timestamp, and key metrics."""
274	        # Spacer
275	        for _ in range(4):
276	            doc.add_paragraph("")
277	
278	        # Title
279	        query = report_data.get("original_query") or report_data.get(
280	            "query", "Untitled Research"
281	        )
282	        title_para = doc.add_paragraph()
283	        title_para.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
284	        title_run = title_para.add_run(query)
285	        title_run.font.name = self._heading_font
286	        title_run.font.size = Pt(DOCX_TITLE_SIZE_PT)
287	        title_run.font.bold = True
288	        title_run.font.color.rgb = self._accent_color
289	
290	        # Subtitle line
291	        doc.add_paragraph("")
292	        subtitle = doc.add_paragraph()
293	        subtitle.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
294	        sub_run = subtitle.add_run("POLARIS Research Report")
295	        sub_run.font.name = self._heading_font
296	        sub_run.font.size = Pt(16)
297	        sub_run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x66, 0x66, 0x66)
298	
299	        # Horizontal rule (thin line)
300	        doc.add_paragraph("")
301	        hr_para = doc.add_paragraph()
302	        hr_para.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
303	        hr_run = hr_para.add_run("_" * 60)
304	        hr_run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0xCC, 0xCC, 0xCC)
305	        hr_run.font.size = Pt(8)
306	
307	        doc.add_paragraph("")
308	
309	        # Metadata block
310	        timestamps = report_data.get("timestamps", {})
311	        created_raw = timestamps.get("created", "")
312	        generation_ts = self._format_timestamp(created_raw)
313	
314	        quality_metrics = report_data.get("quality_metrics") or {}
315	        faithfulness = quality_metrics.get(
316	            "faithfulness_score",
317	            report_data.get("faithfulness_score", 0.0),
318	        )
319	        evidence_count = self._resolve_evidence_count(report_data)
320	        source_count = quality_metrics.get(
321	            "unique_sources",
322	            self._count_unique_sources(report_data),
323	        )
324	
325	        meta_lines = [
326	            ("Generated", generation_ts),
327	            ("Vector ID", report_data.get("vector_id", "N/A")),
328	            ("Faithfulness", f"{faithfulness * 100:.1f}%"),
329	            ("Evidence Pieces", str(evidence_count)),
330	            ("Unique Sources", str(source_count)),
331	        ]
332	
333	        for label, value in meta_lines:
334	            meta_para = doc.add_paragraph()
335	            meta_para.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
336	            label_run = meta_para.add_run(f"{label}: ")
337	            label_run.font.name = self._body_font
338	            label_run.font.size = Pt(12)
339	            label_run.font.bold = True
340	            label_run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x55, 0x55, 0x55)
341	            value_run = meta_para.add_run(value)
342	            value_run.font.name = self._body_font
343	            value_run.font.size = Pt(12)
344	            value_run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x33, 0x33, 0x33)
345	
346	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
347	    # Table of Contents (Word field code)
348	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
349	
350	    @staticmethod
351	    def _add_table_of_contents(doc: Document) -> None:
352	        """
353	        Insert a Table of Contents placeholder using Word field codes.
354	
355	        The TOC is not populated by python-docx itself; the user must
356	        right-click the field in Word and select 'Update Field' (or
357	        press Ctrl+A then F9) to generate page numbers.
358	        """
359	        toc_heading = doc.add_heading("Table of Contents", level=1)
360	        toc_heading.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT
361	
362	        paragraph = doc.add_paragraph()
363	        run = paragraph.add_run()
364	        fld_char_begin = OxmlElement("w:fldChar")
365	        fld_char_begin.set(qn("w:fldCharType"), "begin")
366	        run._r.append(fld_char_begin)
367	
368	        instr_text = OxmlElement("w:instrText")
369	        instr_text.set(qn("xml:space"), "preserve")
370	        instr_text.text = r' TOC \o "1-3" \h \z \u '
371	        run._r.append(instr_text)
372	
373	        fld_char_separate = OxmlElement("w:fldChar")
374	        fld_char_separate.set(qn("w:fldCharType"), "separate")
375	        run._r.append(fld_char_separate)
376	
377	        # Placeholder text visible before user updates the field
378	        placeholder_run = paragraph.add_run(
379	            "Right-click here and select 'Update Field' to generate "
380	            "Table of Contents."
381	        )
382	        placeholder_run.font.italic = True
383	        placeholder_run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x99, 0x99, 0x99)
384	
385	        fld_char_end_run = paragraph.add_run()
386	        fld_char_end = OxmlElement("w:fldChar")
387	        fld_char_end.set(qn("w:fldCharType"), "end")
388	        fld_char_end_run._r.append(fld_char_end)
389	
390	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
391	    # Report Body
392	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
393	
394	    def _add_report_body(self, doc: Document, report_data: dict) -> None:
395	        """
396	        Render the report body into the document.
397	
398	        Strategy:
399	        1. If structured ``sections`` are available, render each with
400	           its heading and parsed content.
401	        2. Otherwise, parse the ``final_report`` markdown string.
402	        """
403	        sections = report_data.get("sections")
404	        if sections and isinstance(sections, list) and len(sections) > 0:
405	            self._render_structured_sections(doc, sections)
406	        else:
407	            final_report = report_data.get("final_report", "")
408	            if final_report:
409	                self._render_markdown(doc, final_report)
410	
411	    def _render_structured_sections(
412	        self, doc: Document, sections: list[dict]
413	    ) -> None:
414	        """Render pre-structured section dicts with heading + content."""
415	        for section in sections:
416	            title = section.get("title", "Untitled Section")
417	            content = section.get("content", "")
418	            doc.add_heading(title, level=2)
419	            self._render_markdown(doc, content)
420	
421	    def _render_markdown(self, doc: Document, text: str) -> None:
422	        """
423	        Parse a markdown string and append paragraphs to the document.
424	
425	        Handles: headings (# ## ###), bold (**), italic (*),
426	        bullet lists (- * +), numbered lists (1.), inline citations [N].
427	        """
428	        lines = text.split("\n")
429	        i = 0
430	        while i < len(lines):
431	            line = lines[i]
432	            stripped = line.strip()
433	
434	            # Skip empty lines
435	            if not stripped:
436	                i += 1
437	                continue
438	
439	            # Heading
440	            heading_match = re.match(r"^(#{1,3})\s+(.+)$", stripped)
441	            if heading_match:
442	                level = len(heading_match.group(1))
443	                heading_text = heading_match.group(2).strip()
444	                doc.add_heading(heading_text, level=level)
445	                i += 1
446	                continue
447	
448	            # Bullet list item
449	            bullet_match = re.match(r"^\s*[-*+]\s+(.+)$", stripped)
450	            if bullet_match:
451	                item_text = bullet_match.group(1)
452	                para = doc.add_paragraph(style="List Bullet")
453	                self._add_rich_text(para, item_text)
454	                i += 1
455	                continue
456	
457	            # Numbered list item
458	            num_match = re.match(r"^\s*\d+\.\s+(.+)$", stripped)
459	            if num_match:
460	                item_text = num_match.group(1)
461	                para = doc.add_paragraph(style="List Number")
462	                self._add_rich_text(para, item_text)
463	                i += 1
464	                continue
465	
466	            # Markdown table detection: look ahead for separator row
467	            if _TABLE_ROW_PATTERN.match(stripped):
468	                # Check if next line is a separator row (header | --- | --- |)
469	                if i + 1 < len(lines) and _TABLE_SEP_PATTERN.match(
470	                    lines[i + 1].strip()
471	                ):
472	                    # Collect all table rows
473	                    table_lines = [stripped]
474	                    i += 1
475	                    while i < len(lines):
476	                        row_stripped = lines[i].strip()
477	                        if not row_stripped:
478	                            break
479	                        if not _TABLE_ROW_PATTERN.match(
480	                            row_stripped
481	                        ) and not _TABLE_SEP_PATTERN.match(row_stripped):
482	                            break
483	                        table_lines.append(row_stripped)
484	                        i += 1
485	                    self._render_markdown_table(doc, table_lines)
486	                    continue
487	
488	            # Base64 image detection
489	            img_match = _BASE64_IMG_PATTERN.search(stripped)
490	            if img_match:
491	                self._render_base64_image(
492	                    doc,
493	                    alt_text=img_match.group(1),
494	                    img_format=img_match.group(2),
495	                    b64_data=img_match.group(3),
496	                )
497	                i += 1
498	                continue
499	
500	            # Key Findings block detection (MUST be before figure caption
501	            # because **Key Findings:** matches the *...* caption pattern)
502	            # FIX-7: Also detect ## Key Findings and ### Key Findings headings
503	            _is_key_findings = (
504	                stripped in ("**Key Findings:**", "**Key Findings**")
505	                or stripped.startswith("**Key Findings")
506	                or bool(re.match(r'^#{2,4}\s+Key Findings', stripped, re.IGNORECASE))
507	            )
508	            if _is_key_findings:
509	                # Render as styled heading-like block, then collect bullets
510	                para = doc.add_paragraph()
511	                para.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(12)
512	                para.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(4)
513	                para.paragraph_format.left_indent = Cm(0.5)
514	                run = para.add_run("Key Findings")
515	                run.font.name = self._heading_font
516	                run.font.size = Pt(DOCX_HEADING3_SIZE_PT)
517	                run.font.bold = True
518	                run.font.color.rgb = self._accent_color
519	                # Add left border via XML (accent color bar)
520	                self._add_left_border(para, self._accent_color)
521	                i += 1
522	                # Collect following bullet items as Key Findings
523	                while i < len(lines):
524	                    kf_line = lines[i].strip()
525	                    if not kf_line:
526	                        i += 1
527	                        continue
528	                    kf_bullet = re.match(r"^\s*[-*+]\s+(.+)$", kf_line)
529	                    if kf_bullet:
530	                        kf_para = doc.add_paragraph(style="List Bullet")
531	                        kf_para.paragraph_format.left_indent = Cm(1.0)
532	                        self._add_rich_text(kf_para, kf_bullet.group(1))
533	                        i += 1
534	                    else:
535	                        break
536	                continue
537	
538	            # Figure caption (italic line like *Figure 1: ...*  or *Table 1: ...*)
539	            caption_match = _FIGURE_CAPTION_PATTERN.match(stripped)
540	            if caption_match:
541	                caption_text = caption_match.group(1)
542	                para = doc.add_paragraph()
543	                para.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
544	                para.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(0)
545	                para.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(12)
546	                run = para.add_run(caption_text)
547	                run.font.name = self._body_font
548	                run.font.size = Pt(max(DOCX_BODY_FONT_SIZE_PT - 2, 8))
549	                run.font.italic = True
550	                run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x66, 0x66, 0x66)
551	                i += 1
552	                continue
553	
554	            # Regular paragraph -- accumulate continuation lines
555	            paragraph_lines = [stripped]
556	            i += 1
557	            while i < len(lines):
558	                next_line = lines[i].strip()
559	                if not next_line:
560	                    break
561	                # Stop if next line is a heading, bullet, numbered item,
562	                # table row, or image
563	                if re.match(r"^#{1,3}\s+", next_line):
564	                    break
565	                if re.match(r"^\s*[-*+]\s+", next_line):
566	                    break
567	                if re.match(r"^\s*\d+\.\s+", next_line):
568	                    break
569	                if _TABLE_ROW_PATTERN.match(next_line):
570	                    break
571	                if _BASE64_IMG_PATTERN.search(next_line):
572	                    break
573	                paragraph_lines.append(next_line)
574	                i += 1
575	
576	            full_text = " ".join(paragraph_lines)
577	            para = doc.add_paragraph()
578	            self._add_rich_text(para, full_text)
579	
580	    def _add_rich_text(self, paragraph, text: str) -> None:
581	        """
582	        Parse inline markdown (bold, italic, citations) and append
583	        styled runs to the given paragraph.
584	
585	        Citations like [1] are rendered as superscript.
586	        **bold** is rendered bold. *italic* is rendered italic.
587	        """
588	        # Tokenize the text into segments: plain, bold, italic, citation
589	        # We use a combined regex that matches any of the three patterns.
590	        combined_pattern = re.compile(
591	            r"(\*\*(.+?)\*\*)"      # group 1=full bold, group 2=bold content
592	            r"|"
593	            r"((?<!\*)\*(?!\*)(.+?)(?<!\*)\*(?!\*))"  # group 3=full italic, group 4=italic content
594	            r"|"
595	            r"(\[(\d+)\])"          # group 5=full citation, group 6=number
596	        )
597	
598	        last_end = 0
599	        for match in combined_pattern.finditer(text):
600	            # Add any plain text before this match
601	            start = match.start()
602	            if start > last_end:
603	                plain = text[last_end:start]
604	                if plain:
605	                    run = paragraph.add_run(plain)
606	                    run.font.name = self._body_font
607	                    run.font.size = self._body_size
608	
609	            if match.group(2) is not None:
610	                # Bold
611	                run = paragraph.add_run(match.group(2))
612	                run.font.name = self._body_font
613	                run.font.size = self._body_size
614	                run.font.bold = True
615	            elif match.group(4) is not None:
616	                # Italic
617	                run = paragraph.add_run(match.group(4))
618	                run.font.name = self._body_font
619	                run.font.size = self._body_size
620	                run.font.italic = True
621	            elif match.group(6) is not None:
622	                # Citation as superscript
623	                run = paragraph.add_run(f"[{match.group(6)}]")
624	                run.font.name = self._body_font
625	                run.font.size = Pt(max(DOCX_BODY_FONT_SIZE_PT - 3, 7))
626	                run.font.superscript = True
627	                run.font.color.rgb = self._accent_color
628	
629	            last_end = match.end()
630	
631	        # Trailing plain text
632	        if last_end < len(text):
633	            remaining = text[last_end:]
634	            if remaining:
635	                run = paragraph.add_run(remaining)
636	                run.font.name = self._body_font
637	                run.font.size = self._body_size
638	
639	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
640	    # Markdown Table Rendering
641	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
642	
643	    def _render_markdown_table(
644	        self, doc: Document, table_lines: list[str]
645	    ) -> None:
646	        """
647	        Parse pipe-delimited markdown table lines into a python-docx Table
648	        with alternating row shading.
649	
650	        Expected format:
651	            | Header1 | Header2 |
652	            | ------- | ------- |
653	            | Cell1   | Cell2   |
654	        """
655	        # Parse rows, skipping separator lines
656	        parsed_rows: list[list[str]] = []
657	        for line in table_lines:
658	            if _TABLE_SEP_PATTERN.match(line):
659	                continue
660	            cells = [
661	                c.strip()
662	                for c in line.strip().strip("|").split("|")
663	            ]
664	            parsed_rows.append(cells)
665	
666	        if len(parsed_rows) < 2:
667	            # Need at least header + 1 data row
668	            return
669	
670	        headers = parsed_rows[0]
671	        data_rows = parsed_rows[1:]
672	        col_count = len(headers)
673	
674	        table = doc.add_table(
675	            rows=1 + len(data_rows), cols=col_count
676	        )
677	        table.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
678	
679	        # Header row
680	        for c_idx, header_text in enumerate(headers):
681	            cell = table.cell(0, c_idx)
682	            cell.text = ""
683	            para = cell.paragraphs[0]
684	            run = para.add_run(header_text)
685	            run.font.name = self._heading_font
686	            run.font.size = self._body_size
687	            run.font.bold = True
688	            run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)
689	            self._shade_cell(cell, self._accent_color)
690	
691	        # Data rows with alternating shading
692	        stripe_color = RGBColor(0xF2, 0xF2, 0xF2)
693	        for r_idx, row_cells in enumerate(data_rows):
694	            for c_idx in range(col_count):
695	                cell_text = (
696	                    row_cells[c_idx] if c_idx < len(row_cells) else ""
697	                )
698	                cell = table.cell(r_idx + 1, c_idx)
699	                cell.text = ""
700	                para = cell.paragraphs[0]
701	                self._add_rich_text(para, cell_text)
702	                # Alternating row shading (even data rows)
703	                if r_idx % 2 == 1:
704	                    self._shade_cell(cell, stripe_color)
705	
706	        # Add spacing after table
707	        doc.add_paragraph("")
708	
709	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
710	    # Base64 Image Rendering
711	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
712	
713	    def _render_base64_image(
714	        self,
715	        doc: Document,
716	        alt_text: str,
717	        img_format: str,
718	        b64_data: str,
719	    ) -> None:
720	        """
721	        Decode a base64-encoded image and embed it inline in the document.
722	
723	        Args:
724	            doc: The python-docx Document.
725	            alt_text: Alt text from the markdown image tag.
726	            img_format: Image format (png, jpg, etc.).
727	            b64_data: Base64-encoded image data.
728	        """
729	        try:
730	            # Strip whitespace from base64 data
731	            clean_b64 = b64_data.replace("\n", "").replace(" ", "")
732	            image_bytes = base64.b64decode(clean_b64)
733	            image_stream = io.BytesIO(image_bytes)
734	
735	            # Add image paragraph (centered)
736	            para = doc.add_paragraph()
737	            para.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
738	            para.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(12)
739	            para.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(4)
740	
741	            run = para.add_run()
742	            run.add_picture(image_stream, width=Inches(5.5))
743	
744	            logger.debug(
745	                "DOCX: embedded base64 image '%s' (%d bytes)",
746	                alt_text,
747	                len(image_bytes),
748	            )
749	
750	        except Exception as exc:
751	            logger.warning(
752	                "DOCX: failed to embed base64 image '%s': %s",
753	                alt_text,
754	                str(exc)[:200],
755	            )
756	            # Fallback: add placeholder text
757	            para = doc.add_paragraph()
758	            para.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
759	            run = para.add_run(f"[Image: {alt_text}]")
760	            run.font.italic = True
761	            run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x99, 0x99, 0x99)

768	    def _add_left_border(
769	        paragraph, color: RGBColor, width_pt: int = 4
770	    ) -> None:
771	        """Add a left border (accent bar) to a paragraph via XML."""
772	        pPr = paragraph._p.get_or_add_pPr()
773	        pBdr = OxmlElement("w:pBdr")
774	        left = OxmlElement("w:left")
775	        left.set(qn("w:val"), "single")
776	        left.set(qn("w:sz"), str(width_pt * 2))  # half-points
777	        left.set(qn("w:space"), "4")
778	        left.set(qn("w:color"), f"{color}")
779	        pBdr.append(left)
780	        pPr.append(pBdr)

979	        )

995	            ),

997	
998	        # Render as a clean two-column table
999	        table = doc.add_table(rows=len(certificate_fields), cols=2)
1000	        table.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
1001	
1002	        for row_idx, (label, value) in enumerate(certificate_fields):
1003	            cell_label = table.cell(row_idx, 0)

1005	
1006	            label_para = cell_label.paragraphs[0]

1010	            label_run.font.bold = True
1011	
1012	            value_para = cell_value.paragraphs[0]
1013	            # SHA-256 in monospace
1014	            if "SHA-256" in label:
1015	                value_run = value_para.add_run(value)
1016	                value_run.font.name = "Consolas"
1017	                value_run.font.size = Pt(9)
1018	                value_run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(0x55, 0x55, 0x55)
1019	            else:
1020	                value_run = value_para.add_run(value)

1028	        cert_run = cert_para.add_run(

1030	            "pipeline. The SHA-256 hash above can be used to verify the "
1031	            "integrity of the report content."
1032	        )
1033	        cert_run.font.name = self._body_font

1038	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1039	    # Helpers
1040	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
1041	
1042	    @staticmethod
1043	    def _shade_cell(cell, color: RGBColor) -> None:
1044	        """Apply a background shading color to a table cell."""
1045	        shading_elm = OxmlElement("w:shd")
1046	        shading_elm.set(qn("w:fill"), f"{color}")
1047	        shading_elm.set(qn("w:val"), "clear")
1048	        cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr().append(shading_elm)
1049	
1050	    @staticmethod
1051	    def _format_timestamp(raw: str) -> str:
1052	        """
1053	        Format an ISO timestamp string into a human-readable form.
1054	        Returns 'N/A' if the string is empty or unparseable.
1055	        """
1056	        if not raw:
1057	            return "N/A"
1058	        try:
1059	            dt = datetime.fromisoformat(raw)
1060	            return dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")
1061	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
1062	            return raw
1063	
1064	    @staticmethod
1065	    def _resolve_evidence_count(report_data: dict) -> int:
1066	        """
1067	        Determine the evidence count from the best available source.
1068	
1069	        Priority: quality_metrics.total_evidence > evidence_count key >
1070	                  len(evidence list).
1071	        """
1072	        qm = report_data.get("quality_metrics") or {}
1073	        if qm.get("total_evidence"):
1074	            return int(qm["total_evidence"])
1075	        if report_data.get("evidence_count"):
1076	            return int(report_data["evidence_count"])
1077	        evidence = report_data.get("evidence", [])
1078	        if isinstance(evidence, list):
1079	            return len(evidence)
1080	        return 0
1081	
1082	    @staticmethod
1083	    def _count_unique_sources(report_data: dict) -> int:
1084	        """
1085	        Count unique sources from bibliography or evidence list.
1086	        """
1087	        bibliography = report_data.get("bibliography", [])
1088	        if bibliography:
1089	            return len(bibliography)
1090	        evidence = report_data.get("evidence", [])
1091	        if isinstance(evidence, list):
1092	            urls = {
1093	                e.get("source_url", "")
1094	                for e in evidence
1095	                if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("source_url")
1096	            }
1097	            return len(urls)
1098	        return 0
1099

1	"""F11 follow-up agent substrate (I-f11-001).
2	
3	Phase-1 standalone agent module per CLAUDE.md §9.4. Production wiring
4	(graph_v4 calling FollowUpAgent on follow-up questions, UI surface,
5	scope-gate routing) is follow-up I-f11-001b.
6	"""
7

1	"""Follow-up agent — preserves parent-run context (I-f11-001).
2	
3	Pure deterministic substrate: no LLM call. LLM-augmented follow-up
4	disambiguation is follow-up I-f11-002.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
10	
11	
12	@dataclass(frozen=True)
13	class ParentRunContext:
14	    """Snapshot of the parent run that a follow-up will inherit from."""
15	
16	    parent_run_id: str
17	    template: str
18	    parent_question: str
19	    known_evidence_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
20	    parent_summary: str | None = None
21	
22	
23	@dataclass(frozen=True)
24	class ComposedQuery:
25	    """A follow-up question composed with parent-run context."""
26	
27	    effective_question: str
28	    inherited_template: str
29	    parent_run_id: str
30	    inherited_evidence_ids: list[str]
31	
32	
33	def _dedup_preserve_order(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
34	    """Return a fresh list with duplicates removed, preserving first-seen order."""
35	    seen: set[str] = set()
36	    out: list[str] = []
37	    for item in items:
38	        if item in seen:
39	            continue
40	        seen.add(item)
41	        out.append(item)
42	    return out
43	
44	
45	class FollowUpAgent:
46	    """Compose a follow-up question with parent-run context preserved."""
47	
48	    def compose(
49	        self, parent: ParentRunContext, follow_up: str
50	    ) -> ComposedQuery:
51	        if not follow_up or not follow_up.strip():
52	            raise ValueError("follow_up question must be non-blank")
53	        effective = (
54	            f"Follow-up to '{parent.parent_question}': {follow_up.strip()}"
55	        )
56	        inherited = _dedup_preserve_order(parent.known_evidence_ids)
57	        return ComposedQuery(
58	            effective_question=effective,
59	            inherited_template=parent.template,
60	            parent_run_id=parent.parent_run_id,
61	            inherited_evidence_ids=inherited,
62	        )
63

1	"""Evidence Contract inheritance for follow-up runs (I-f11-003).
2	
3	Pure deterministic functions. NO retrieval / network calls — the parent
4	run's accepted source pool is reused via deep copy, satisfying the
5	acceptance "no re-retrieval of parent" guarantee at the module level
6	(verified by tests).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from polaris_graph.followup.agent import (
12	    ComposedQuery,
13	    FollowUpAgent,
14	    ParentRunContext,
15	)
16	from polaris_graph.followup.refusal import RefusalDecision, compose_or_refuse
17	from polaris_v6.schemas.evidence_contract import EvidenceContract, SourceSpan
18	
19	
20	def inherit_evidence_pool(parent_contract: EvidenceContract) -> list[SourceSpan]:
21	    """Return a fresh list of the parent run's accepted source spans."""
22	    return list(parent_contract.evidence_pool)
23	
24	
25	def compose_with_inheritance(
26	    agent: FollowUpAgent,
27	    parent_contract: EvidenceContract,
28	    follow_up: str,
29	) -> tuple[ComposedQuery, list[SourceSpan]]:
30	    """Compose a follow-up question that inherits the parent's evidence pool.
31	
32	    Returns the ComposedQuery (with evidence_id list inherited from the
33	    parent's evidence_pool) and a fresh copy of the parent's source spans
34	    suitable for direct passthrough to the merger.
35	    """
36	    parent = ParentRunContext(
37	        parent_run_id=parent_contract.run_id,
38	        template=parent_contract.template,
39	        parent_question=parent_contract.question,
40	        known_evidence_ids=[s.evidence_id for s in parent_contract.evidence_pool],
41	        parent_summary=None,
42	    )
43	    composed = agent.compose(parent, follow_up)
44	    inherited = inherit_evidence_pool(parent_contract)
45	    return composed, inherited
46	
47	
48	def compose_with_inheritance_or_refuse(
49	    agent: FollowUpAgent,
50	    parent_contract: EvidenceContract,
51	    follow_up: str,
52	) -> tuple[ComposedQuery | RefusalDecision, list[SourceSpan]]:
53	    """Inheritance-aware compose that routes through out-of-scope refusal.
54	
55	    Returns (RefusalDecision, []) when the follow-up is out-of-scope, or
56	    (ComposedQuery, inherited_spans) when in-scope.
57	    """
58	    parent = ParentRunContext(
59	        parent_run_id=parent_contract.run_id,
60	        template=parent_contract.template,
61	        parent_question=parent_contract.question,
62	        known_evidence_ids=[s.evidence_id for s in parent_contract.evidence_pool],
63	        parent_summary=None,
64	    )
65	    decision = compose_or_refuse(agent, parent, follow_up)
66	    if isinstance(decision, RefusalDecision):
67	        return decision, []
68	    inherited = inherit_evidence_pool(parent_contract)
69	    return decision, inherited
70

1	"""Multi-turn follow-up driver (I-f11-005).
2	
3	Runs N follow-ups against the SAME parent contract. Each turn flows
4	through `compose_with_inheritance_or_refuse`: out-of-scope → typed
5	RefusalDecision; in-scope → ComposedQuery + inherited spans. Refusal
6	does NOT short-circuit; subsequent turns still execute. Chained-context
7	follow-ups (turn-2 inheriting from turn-1) are post-MVP — call
8	run_multi_turn again with a new contract to chain.
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from dataclasses import dataclass
14	
15	from polaris_graph.followup.agent import ComposedQuery, FollowUpAgent
16	from polaris_graph.followup.inheritance import compose_with_inheritance_or_refuse
17	from polaris_graph.followup.refusal import RefusalDecision
18	from polaris_v6.schemas.evidence_contract import EvidenceContract, SourceSpan
19	
20	
21	@dataclass(frozen=True)
22	class TurnResult:
23	    turn_index: int
24	    follow_up: str
25	    composed: ComposedQuery | None
26	    refusal: RefusalDecision | None
27	    inherited_spans: list[SourceSpan]
28	
29	
30	def run_multi_turn(
31	    agent: FollowUpAgent,
32	    parent_contract: EvidenceContract,
33	    follow_ups: list[str],
34	) -> list[TurnResult]:
35	    results: list[TurnResult] = []
36	    for i, fu in enumerate(follow_ups):
37	        decision, spans = compose_with_inheritance_or_refuse(
38	            agent, parent_contract, fu
39	        )
40	        if isinstance(decision, RefusalDecision):
41	            results.append(
42	                TurnResult(
43	                    turn_index=i, follow_up=fu, composed=None,
44	                    refusal=decision, inherited_spans=spans,
45	                )
46	            )
47	        else:
48	            results.append(
49	                TurnResult(
50	                    turn_index=i, follow_up=fu, composed=decision,
51	                    refusal=None, inherited_spans=spans,
52	                )
53	            )
54	    return results
55

1	"""Out-of-scope refusal handling for follow-up runs (I-f11-004).
2	
3	Heuristic: a follow-up is out-of-scope when zero of its tokens
4	(case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped) appear in the parent
5	template's `_`-separated keyword list. MVP debt: single-token templates
6	over-refuse; LLM-augmented intent matching is post-MVP.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import re
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	
14	from polaris_graph.followup.agent import (
15	    ComposedQuery,
16	    FollowUpAgent,
17	    ParentRunContext,
18	)
19	
20	
21	_PUNCT_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9 ]+")
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class RefusalDecision:
26	    is_refused: bool
27	    reason: str | None
28	    template_keywords: list[str]
29	    question_overlap: list[str]
30	
31	
32	def detect_out_of_scope(
33	    parent_template: str, follow_up: str, *, min_overlap: int = 1
34	) -> RefusalDecision:
35	    keywords = [t for t in parent_template.lower().split("_") if t]
36	    if parent_template == "general":
37	        return RefusalDecision(False, None, keywords, [])
38	    words = _PUNCT_RE.sub(" ", follow_up.lower()).split()
39	    kw_set = set(keywords)
40	    overlap = [w for w in words if w in kw_set]
41	    if len(overlap) < min_overlap:
42	        reason = (
43	            f"follow-up has {len(overlap)} shared keyword(s) with parent "
44	            f"template '{parent_template}' (need >= {min_overlap}); out-of-scope"
45	        )
46	        return RefusalDecision(True, reason, keywords, overlap)
47	    return RefusalDecision(False, None, keywords, overlap)
48	
49	
50	def compose_or_refuse(
51	    agent: FollowUpAgent, parent: ParentRunContext, follow_up: str
52	) -> ComposedQuery | RefusalDecision:
53	    decision = detect_out_of_scope(parent.template, follow_up)
54	    if decision.is_refused:
55	        return decision
56	    return agent.compose(parent, follow_up)
57
