1	"""POLARIS honest-rebuild generator package (Phase 4)."""
2

1	"""Abstract / executive-summary (front) + Conclusion (end) synthesis layer, drafted LAST
2	from the ALREADY-strict_verify-PASSED body (I-arch-011 PR-d #1268).
3	
4	This is the LAST composition step (composition_design_plan §THE FLOW step 4): it runs AFTER
5	the verified body (``SectionResult.verified_text``) is assembled, and draws ONLY from already-
6	verified body sentences — NEVER from ``evidence_pool`` / the raw corpus.
7	
8	FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT (clinical-lethal). Every abstract/conclusion sentence is a VERBATIM
9	RE-PRESENTATION of an already-verified body sentence (carrying the body's own ``[N]`` citation).
10	It is faithful BY IDENTITY: it is byte-equal to a sentence that already passed ``strict_verify``
11	in the body (the proven Key-Findings extractive contract). Because it carries the body sentence's
12	exact prose, the post-gate ``reconcile_report_against_verdicts`` redactor removes the abstract/
13	conclusion copy in the SAME multi-occurrence loop it redacts the body copy if the 4-role seam flips
14	the claim to non-VERIFIED (no orphan, no fail-closed abort). Empty verified body -> a disclosed
15	"insufficient verified evidence" line, NEVER fabricated filler (§-1.3).
16	
17	WHY VERBATIM-ONLY (cross-claim author-summary synthesis was DESIGNED then CUT, 2026-06-18):
18	The brief's P1 required that a LABELED author-summary which "reuses the body's entities+numbers but
19	asserts a NEW relation/comparative/causal/safety conclusion" be REJECTED by a DETERMINISTIC gate
20	(no new hot-path judge). A first build added a ``check_claim_atom_grounding`` bag-of-atoms gate, but
21	the PR-d replay harness proved it UNSOUND for exactly that class: a recombination of the body's own
22	atoms ("X causes Y" when the body separately contains X, Y, and the word "causes") has every atom
23	present BY CONSTRUCTION, so presence-checking can never reject it. Catching atom-RECOMBINATION
24	requires entailment of the synthesis sentence against the SPECIFIC cited claim — a judge call the
25	brief told us to keep off the hot path, and a larger build than PR-d. Rather than ship a green
26	harness over a gate that cannot do its job (a future landmine: a later engineer enables the labeled-
27	synthesis path trusting the gate, and recombination fabrications ship), PR-d ships the safe subset
28	that already renders — verbatim re-presentation, faithful by identity — and the unsound synthesis
29	machinery is REMOVED. Any future labeled-synthesis feature must build the entailment gate first.
30	
31	Adds NO new model/slug/resolver and NO new hot-path LLM judge; the render is purely DETERMINISTIC
32	(no spend). Does NOT modify ``strict_verify`` nor any I-arch-010 tail code.
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import os
38	import re
39	from typing import Any, Callable
40	
41	# key_findings is the SIBLING extractive-summary module (same generator family). PR-d reuses its
42	# PROVEN faithfulness filters so a gap-disclosure / leaked-header line is never lifted as a finding,
43	# and its sentence-match regex (which keeps a trailing ``[N]`` attached to its sentence). These are
44	# the same primitives the existing Key-Findings front block ships on — deliberate shared coupling,
45	# not incidental: PR-d is the draft-last sibling of Key Findings.
46	from src.polaris_graph.generator.key_findings import (
47	    _CITATION_RE,
48	    _GAP_MARKER_RE,
49	    _SENTENCE_RE,
50	    _first_verified_sentences,
51	    _relevance_weight,
52	    _strip_leading_markdown_headers,
53	    cap_citation_marker_runs,
54	)
55	
56	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", ""})
57	_TRUE_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})
58	
59	# Default-OFF master flag. Flag-OFF -> build_abstract()/build_conclusion() return "" so the
60	# report.md ARTIFACT is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-PR-d stack. Gate-B / the run slate activates it.
61	_ENABLED_ENV = "PG_SYNTHESIS_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION"
62	
63	# How many leading verified sentences to lift per section into the abstract (headline finding).
64	_ABSTRACT_SENTENCES_PER_SECTION = 1
65	# Hard cap so the abstract stays a summary (mirrors key_findings _MAX_BULLETS posture).
66	_ABSTRACT_MAX_SENTENCES = 6
67	# How many trailing/most-significant verified sentences the conclusion re-presents.
68	_CONCLUSION_MAX_SENTENCES = 5
69	
70	# I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 3: per-run citation cap for the Abstract/Conclusion summary (LAW VI). A
71	# closing summary line carries the body span VERBATIM (every marker already span-supported); this
72	# only bounds how many co-citations one RUN displays. The body + bibliography keep every reference,
73	# so the cap can never orphan a citation. Floored at 1; fail-soft on a non-int.
74	_MAX_SUMMARY_MARKERS_ENV = "PG_SYNTHESIS_MAX_MARKERS"
75	_DEFAULT_SUMMARY_MARKERS = 3
76	
77	
78	def _max_summary_markers() -> int:
79	    raw = os.getenv(_MAX_SUMMARY_MARKERS_ENV, "").strip()
80	    if not raw:
81	        return _DEFAULT_SUMMARY_MARKERS
82	    try:
83	        return max(1, int(raw))
84	    except ValueError:
85	        return _DEFAULT_SUMMARY_MARKERS
86	
87	# Disclosed "no verified evidence" line (NEVER fabricated filler — §-1.3).
88	_INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE = (
89	    "No claim in this report survived span/numeric/entailment verification against its "
90	    "cited source; there is insufficient verified evidence to summarize."
91	)
92	
93	
94	def synthesis_abstract_conclusion_enabled() -> bool:
95	    """Default OFF. Set ``PG_SYNTHESIS_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION=1`` to render the abstract + conclusion."""
96	    return os.getenv(_ENABLED_ENV, "0").strip().lower() in _TRUE_VALUES
97	
98	
99	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
100	# Verified-body sentence harvesting (VERBATIM re-presentation — the only render path)
101	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
102	def _section_is_verified(sr: Any) -> bool:
103	    """A section contributes verified prose iff it is not dropped, not a gap stub, and has
104	    at least one verified sentence (the SAME skip contract Key Findings uses)."""
105	    if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
106	        return False
107	    if getattr(sr, "is_gap_stub", False) or getattr(sr, "sentences_verified", 1) == 0:
108	        return False
109	    return bool((getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "").strip())
110	
111	
112	def _verified_sentences_in_order(verified_text: str) -> list[str]:
113	    """Every span-verified (cited, non-gap, non-header) sentence in a section's verified_text,
114	    in document order. Reuses the proven key_findings filters so a gap-disclosure / leaked-header
115	    line is never lifted as a finding."""
116	    text = _strip_leading_markdown_headers(verified_text or "")
117	    # _first_verified_sentences applies the citation/gap/header filters; ask for a high cap so we
118	    # get ALL verified sentences in order (the abstract/conclusion select among them deterministically).
119	    return _first_verified_sentences(text, 10_000)
120	
121	
122	def _harvest_abstract_sentences(
123	    sections: list[Any],
124	    *,
125	    sentence_relevance: "Callable[[str], float] | None" = None,
126	) -> list[str]:
127	    """The abstract leads with each section's HEADLINE verified finding (its first verified
128	    sentence), verbatim with its citation, capped. Pure re-presentation — zero new claims.
129	
130	    ``sentence_relevance`` (headline_relevance, I-deepfix-001): OPTIONAL caller-wired question-
131	    relevance ranker. WEIGHT never filter (§-1.3): within-section the most on-topic verified
132	    sentence leads, and the harvested headlines are then GLOBALLY ordered by descending relevance so
133	    an on-topic finding buried past the ``_ABSTRACT_MAX_SENTENCES`` cap is SURFACED — it NEVER drops
134	    or re-verifies a sentence. ``None`` (every existing caller) => byte-identical document order (the
135	    prior first-N-in-document-order harvest)."""
136	    candidates: list[tuple[float, str]] = []
137	    for sr in sections or []:
138	        if not _section_is_verified(sr):
139	            continue
140	        verified_text = getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or ""
141	        for sentence in _first_verified_sentences(
142	            _strip_leading_markdown_headers(verified_text),
143	            _ABSTRACT_SENTENCES_PER_SECTION,
144	            sentence_relevance=sentence_relevance,
145	        ):
146	            weight = (
147	                _relevance_weight(sentence_relevance, sentence)
148	                if sentence_relevance is not None
149	                else 0.0
150	            )
151	            candidates.append((weight, sentence))
152	    if sentence_relevance is not None:
153	        # STABLE descending-weight order: ties keep document order; off-topic sinks past the cap.
154	        candidates = sorted(candidates, key=lambda c: -c[0])
155	    return [s for _, s in candidates][:_ABSTRACT_MAX_SENTENCES]
156	
157	
158	def _harvest_conclusion_sentences(sections: list[Any]) -> list[str]:
159	    """The conclusion re-presents the CLOSING verified finding of each section (its last verified
160	    sentence), verbatim with its citation, capped. Pure re-presentation — zero new claims. Falls
161	    back to the first verified sentence if a section has only one (so a single-finding section is
162	    still represented), de-duplicating against what was already chosen."""
163	    out: list[str] = []
164	    seen: set[str] = set()
165	    for sr in sections or []:
166	        if not _section_is_verified(sr):
167	            continue
168	        ordered = _verified_sentences_in_order(getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "")
169	        if not ordered:
170	            continue
171	        sentence = ordered[-1]
172	        if sentence in seen:
173	            continue
174	        seen.add(sentence)
175	        out.append(sentence)
176	        if len(out) >= _CONCLUSION_MAX_SENTENCES:
177	            break
178	    return out
179	
180	
181	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
182	# Block builders (front abstract + end conclusion)
183	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	# I-beatboth-011 §3.2 (#1289): HONEST self-cert label. The prior absolute "span-verified
185	# statement" phrasing over-claimed — it is the §-1.1 lethal-miss a blind reader hears by ear: a
186	# verbatim self-quote tautologically passes strict_verify, so the absolute phrasing implied a
187	# guarantee the engine does not make (on-topic / peer-reviewed / corroborated). State the REAL guarantee +
188	# denominator discipline instead (mirrors the Reliability header). LABEL honesty only — the
189	# faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED.
190	_ABSTRACT_HEADER = (
191	    "## Abstract\n\n"
192	    "_Each sentence below is verbatim text carried up from a cited body span; it passes strict_verify "
193	    "(evidence-id in pool, span bounds, numeric match, ≥2 content-word grounding) but is single-origin "
194	    "unless marked corroborated, and span-grounding is NOT a peer-reviewed or on-topic guarantee. "
195	    "Citations are the body's; this summary introduces no new claim._\n\n"
196	)
197	_CONCLUSION_HEADER = (
198	    "## Conclusion\n\n"
199	    "_Each sentence below is verbatim text carried up from a cited body span; it passes strict_verify "
200	    "(span bounds + numeric match + ≥2 content-word grounding) but is single-origin unless marked "
201	    "corroborated, and span-grounding is NOT a peer-reviewed or on-topic guarantee. Citations are the "
202	    "body's; this conclusion introduces no new claim._\n\n"
203	)
204	# idx32/42 render-gate: a run whose binding D8 gate did NOT certify release MUST NOT stamp a
205	# verification-complete label. The caller passes ``release_certified=False`` when
206	# release_allowed is False / the 4-role D8 is incomplete; the block then leads with this caveat.
207	_NOT_CERTIFIED_CAVEAT = (
208	    "_⚠️ BINDING D8 GATE INCOMPLETE — NOT RELEASE-CERTIFIED. The 4-role adjudication did not complete "
209	    "/ the release gate withheld this run, so the statements below are span-grounded but NOT release-"
210	    "verified. Treat as provisional._\n\n"
211	)
212	
213	
214	def _apply_unit_screen(sentences: list[str], unit_screen) -> list[str]:
215	    """I-beatboth-011 KEYSTONE (#1289): WITHHOLD flagged chrome sentences from the summary surface
216	    BEFORE the join. ``unit_screen`` (injectable, default None) takes the harvested sentence LIST and
217	    returns the subset to KEEP — the authoritative benchmarked span gate catches the glued-mid-prose
218	    chrome the blind ``_is_render_chrome_claim`` harvest filter is structurally blind to. §-1.3
219	    FLAG-NOT-DROP: a withheld sentence is excluded from the Abstract/Conclusion summary only; it is
220	    UNTOUCHED in the body / evidence pool. ``None`` (the legacy caller) -> byte-identical (every
221	    sentence kept). Fail-CONSERVATIVE: on any screen error the input list is returned UNCHANGED (never
222	    silently empty the summary). The screen must return a subset of the input order; defensive: keep
223	    only inputs the screen returned, preserving order."""
224	    if unit_screen is None or not sentences:
225	        return sentences
226	    try:
227	        kept = unit_screen(sentences)
228	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-conservative: never empty the summary on a screen fault
229	        return sentences
230	    if not isinstance(kept, list):
231	        return sentences
232	    kept_set = {s for s in kept if isinstance(s, str)}
233	    return [s for s in sentences if s in kept_set]
234	
235	
236	def build_abstract(
237	    sections: list[Any],
238	    release_certified: bool = True,
239	    *,
240	    unit_screen=None,
241	    sentence_relevance: "Callable[[str], float] | None" = None,
242	) -> str:
243	    """Return a markdown ``## Abstract`` block: verbatim span-grounded headline findings carried
244	    up from the body, drafted LAST. Returns "" when disabled (flag-OFF byte-identity). When the
245	    body has NO verified prose, renders the disclosed insufficient-evidence line (NEVER empty
246	    fabricated filler). ``release_certified=False`` (idx32/42) prepends the NOT-RELEASE-CERTIFIED
247	    caveat for a run whose binding D8 gate did not certify release.
248	
249	    I-beatboth-011 KEYSTONE (#1289): ``unit_screen`` (injectable, default None) is the authoritative
250	    benchmarked span gate — when supplied, a harvested headline sentence the gate flags as chrome is
251	    WITHHELD from this Abstract surface BEFORE the join (§-1.3 FLAG-NOT-DROP: withheld from the summary
252	    only; untouched in the body). ``None`` = byte-identical legacy behaviour. The Abstract is the front
253	    sandwich, so it rides the SAME authoritative screen as Key-Findings, not the blind harvest filter
254	    alone.
255	
256	    ``sentence_relevance`` (headline_relevance, I-deepfix-001): OPTIONAL caller-wired question-
257	    relevance ranker, mirroring ``key_findings.build_key_findings``. WEIGHT never filter (§-1.3): the
258	    verbatim headline findings are ORDERED by descending on-topicness so the Abstract leads with the
259	    most on-topic verified finding; nothing is dropped or re-verified. ``None`` (every existing
260	    caller) => byte-identical document order."""
261	    if not synthesis_abstract_conclusion_enabled():
262	        return ""
263	    _caveat = "" if release_certified else _NOT_CERTIFIED_CAVEAT
264	    sentences = _harvest_abstract_sentences(
265	        sections, sentence_relevance=sentence_relevance
266	    )
267	    # KEYSTONE span-quality screen BEFORE the join (no rendered-prose splitting). When every sentence
268	    # is withheld the disclosed insufficient-evidence line renders (NEVER empty fabricated filler).
269	    sentences = _apply_unit_screen(sentences, unit_screen)
270	    if not sentences:
271	        return _ABSTRACT_HEADER + _caveat + _INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE + "\n\n"
272	    # I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 3: constrain each summary line's marker RUNS (render-only).
273	    _cap = _max_summary_markers()
274	    sentences = [cap_citation_marker_runs(s, _cap) for s in sentences]
275	    return _ABSTRACT_HEADER + _caveat + " ".join(sentences) + "\n\n"
276	
277	
278	def build_conclusion(sections: list[Any], release_certified: bool = True, *, unit_screen=None) -> str:
279	    """Return a markdown ``## Conclusion`` block: verbatim span-grounded closing findings carried
280	    up from the body, drafted LAST. Returns "" when disabled (flag-OFF byte-identity). When the
281	    body has NO verified prose, renders the disclosed insufficient-evidence line (NEVER empty
282	    fabricated filler). ``release_certified=False`` (idx32/42) prepends the NOT-RELEASE-CERTIFIED
283	    caveat for a run whose binding D8 gate did not certify release.
284	
285	    I-beatboth-011 KEYSTONE (#1289): ``unit_screen`` (injectable, default None) — a closing sentence
286	    the authoritative benchmarked span gate flags as chrome is WITHHELD from this Conclusion surface
287	    BEFORE the join (§-1.3 FLAG-NOT-DROP). ``None`` = byte-identical legacy behaviour."""
288	    if not synthesis_abstract_conclusion_enabled():
289	        return ""
290	    _caveat = "" if release_certified else _NOT_CERTIFIED_CAVEAT
291	    sentences = _harvest_conclusion_sentences(sections)
292	    # KEYSTONE span-quality screen BEFORE the join (no rendered-prose splitting).
293	    sentences = _apply_unit_screen(sentences, unit_screen)
294	    if not sentences:
295	        return _CONCLUSION_HEADER + _caveat + _INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE + "\n\n"
296	    # I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 3: constrain each closing line's marker RUNS so a conclusion sentence
297	    # cites only the few most-relevant spans (the body span is verbatim — markers stay span-grounded).
298	    _cap = _max_summary_markers()
299	    sentences = [cap_citation_marker_runs(s, _cap) for s in sentences]
300	    return _CONCLUSION_HEADER + _caveat + " ".join(sentences) + "\n\n"
301	
302	
303	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
304	# Post-redaction refilter (the duplicate-claim landmine, brief P2-2)
305	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
306	_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"(?m)^##\s*(?:Abstract|Conclusion)\s*$")
307	
308	
309	def refilter_abstract_conclusion_block(report_text: str) -> str:
310	    """Drop Abstract / Conclusion sentences that became a redaction GAP STUB after the four-role
311	    seam, and remove a block left with no real finding (no empty heading) — the exact analog of
312	    ``key_findings.refilter_key_findings_block``.
313	
314	    The abstract/conclusion sentences are VERBATIM re-presentations of body claims, so the
315	    multi-occurrence ``reconcile_report_against_verdicts`` loop already redacts the abstract/
316	    conclusion copy of a non-VERIFIED claim into the gap-replacement text in the SAME pass it
317	    redacts the body copy (no orphan). This pass then tidies the block: it removes the
318	    gap-replacement sentence(s) the redactor wrote into the abstract/conclusion summary line and,
319	    if a block has no verified sentence left, drops the whole block (heading + preamble). Idempotent
320	    + byte-identical when nothing in a block was redacted.
321	    """
322	    if not synthesis_abstract_conclusion_enabled():
323	        return report_text
324	    out = report_text
325	    for header_re in (
326	        re.compile(r"(?m)^##\s*Abstract\s*$"),
327	        re.compile(r"(?m)^##\s*Conclusion\s*$"),
328	    ):
329	        out = _refilter_one_block(out, header_re)
330	    return out
331	
332	
333	def _refilter_one_block(report_text: str, header_re: re.Pattern[str]) -> str:
334	    """Within the bounded block starting at ``header_re``, drop any gap-disclosure sentence the
335	    redactor wrote (``_GAP_MARKER_RE``), and drop the whole block if no real finding remains."""
336	    header_match = header_re.search(report_text)
337	    if not header_match:
338	        return report_text
339	    block_start = header_match.start()
340	    rest = report_text[header_match.end():]
341	    next_header = re.search(r"(?m)^#{1,6}\s", rest)
342	    block_end = header_match.end() + (next_header.start() if next_header else len(rest))
343	    block = report_text[block_start:block_end]
344	
345	    # The block body is the heading + an italic preamble + ONE summary line of space-joined
346	    # sentences. Split that summary line into sentences and drop any that became a gap stub.
347	    kept_any_finding = False
348	    new_lines: list[str] = []
349	    for line in block.splitlines():
350	        stripped = line.strip()
351	        if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#") or stripped.startswith("_"):
352	            new_lines.append(line)
353	            continue
354	        # The summary line: drop gap-disclosure sentences, keep verified (still-cited) ones. Use
355	        # key_findings' _SENTENCE_RE (a MATCH, not a split) so a trailing ``[N]`` citation stays
356	        # attached to its sentence ("...20 percent.[1] Next..." segments correctly).
357	        sentences = [m.group(0).strip() for m in _SENTENCE_RE.finditer(line.strip())]
358	        if not sentences:  # a line with no sentence-terminator (defensive) — treat whole line as one
359	            sentences = [line.strip()]
360	        kept_sentences = [s for s in sentences if s and not _GAP_MARKER_RE.search(s)]
361	        if any(_CITATION_RE.search(s) for s in kept_sentences):
362	            kept_any_finding = True
363	        if kept_sentences:
364	            new_lines.append(" ".join(kept_sentences))
365	        # else: every sentence on this line was a gap stub -> drop the whole summary line.
366	
367	    if kept_any_finding:
368	        new_block = "\n".join(new_lines)
369	        if not new_block.endswith("\n"):
370	            new_block += "\n"
371	        return report_text[:block_start] + new_block + report_text[block_end:]
372	
373	    # No verified finding remains in the block -> drop the whole block (no empty heading).
374	    trimmed = report_text[:block_start] + report_text[block_end:]
375	    return re.sub(r"^\n+", "", trimmed) if block_start == 0 else trimmed
376

1	"""Analytical-depth heuristic — single source of truth for Pipeline A + Pipeline B.
2	
3	The depth heuristic (regex counts of comparison / aggregation / challenge markers, markdown tables, and
4	``**Key Findings**`` subsections, plus a per-section "deficient" flag and a ``passed`` threshold) was
5	historically inlined in ``synthesizer._evaluate_analytical_depth`` and drove the Pipeline-B RC-8 gate.
6	It is extracted here, byte-for-byte, so:
7	
8	1. The benchmark (Pipeline A, ``run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query``) can import the heuristic **without**
9	   importing ``synthesizer`` (which pulls ``OpenRouterClient`` + tracer + schemas + state — heavy, with
10	   construction side-effects). This module is stdlib-only (``re`` + typing); zero new dependency, zero
11	   network, zero cost.
12	2. The two pipelines share one implementation and cannot drift. ``synthesizer._evaluate_analytical_depth``
13	   now delegates here, so RC-8 behavior is unchanged.
14	
15	The benchmark uses ``evaluate_analytical_depth`` as an **ADVISORY, non-gating** annotation only (see
16	``run_honest_sweep_r3``). The ``passed`` boolean and ``deficient_sections`` list are computed over a
17	``split_report_into_sections`` view of the assembled report (split on ATX headers), which differs from
18	Pipeline B's semantic ``report_sections``: Bibliography / Methods / sub-headers each become their own
19	"section", so the benchmark ``passed`` / ``deficient_sections`` are an **advisory split read, NOT the
20	RC-8 gate verdict**. The benchmark NEVER gates on this signal.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	import re
27	from typing import Any
28	
29	# ATX markdown header: 1-6 '#' then whitespace then a title (e.g. "## Key Findings", "### Limitations").
30	_ATX_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^(#{1,6})\s+(.*?)\s*$")
31	
32	# I-wire-012 (#1326) depth fix — count the AGGREGATED ``## Key Findings`` ATX block + ``### <title>``
33	# synthesis headers, not only the per-section ``**Key Findings**`` bold form. Default-ON kill-switch
34	# ``PG_DEPTH_COUNT_ATX_KEY_FINDINGS``. A report that ships a ``## Key Findings`` block (or the
35	# ``## Analytical synthesis`` per-section headings) previously scored key_findings=0 here because the
36	# regex matched ONLY the bold form — the depth=0 the I-wire-012 acceptance flags. Broadening can ONLY
37	# RAISE the count, which makes the Pipeline-B RC-8 depth heuristic EASIER to pass (never a faithfulness
38	# relaxation — RC-8 is an advisory depth heuristic, not the faithfulness engine); =0 restores the exact
39	# pre-I-wire-012 count for byte-identical RC-8 parity.
40	_COUNT_ATX_KEY_FINDINGS_ENV = "PG_DEPTH_COUNT_ATX_KEY_FINDINGS"
41	_DEPTH_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", ""})
42	
43	
44	def _count_atx_key_findings_enabled() -> bool:
45	    """Default ON. ``PG_DEPTH_COUNT_ATX_KEY_FINDINGS=0`` restores the bold-only count (RC-8 parity)."""
46	    return os.getenv(_COUNT_ATX_KEY_FINDINGS_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in _DEPTH_OFF_VALUES
47	
48	
49	def evaluate_analytical_depth(report_sections: list[dict]) -> dict:
50	    """RC-8: Evaluate analytical depth using regex-based heuristics.
51	
52	    Checks for the 5 analytical operations across all sections.
53	    Returns: {"passed": bool, "scores": {...}, "deficient_sections": [...]}
54	
55	    Body MOVED VERBATIM from ``synthesizer._evaluate_analytical_depth`` (same regex strings, same
56	    per-section ``ops_present`` logic, same thresholds, same return keys) — Pipeline-B RC-8 parity.
57	
58	    NOTE (benchmark form): the ``key_findings`` regex matches the **bold** ``**Key Findings**``
59	    subsection form (the per-section form emitted by the synthesis prompt). The benchmark's *front*
60	    aggregated Key Findings block is emitted as an **ATX** header (``## Key Findings`` —
61	    ``generator/key_findings.py``), which this regex deliberately does NOT count. Per-section bold
62	    ``**Key Findings**`` subsections ARE counted. Kept verbatim rather than broadened so Pipeline-B
63	    RC-8 counts cannot shift; the benchmark consumes this advisory-only, so a conservative
64	    (undercounting, never inflating) key-findings tally is the safe direction.
65	    """
66	    comparison_markers = re.compile(
67	        r'\b(compared to|in contrast|whereas|however|unlike|alternatively|'
68	        r'on the other hand|differs from|outperformed|underperformed)\b', re.I
69	    )
70	    aggregation_markers = re.compile(
71	        r'\b(across \d+ studies|multiple sources|ranged from|median of|'
72	        r'average of|converging|majority of evidence|consistently)\b', re.I
73	    )
74	    challenge_markers = re.compile(
75	        r'\b(limitation|however contradictory|conflicting|gap in|'
76	        r'insufficient evidence|notable absence|remains unclear|'
77	        r'further research needed|caveat)\b', re.I
78	    )
79	    table_pattern = re.compile(r'\|[^|]+\|[^|]+\|')
80	    # I-wire-012 (#1326): when enabled (default), ALSO count the aggregated ``## Key Findings`` ATX
81	    # block and the ``## Analytical synthesis`` synthesis header (which ``split_report_into_sections``
82	    # turns into a section TITLE, not body content) — not only the per-section ``**Key Findings**``
83	    # bold form — so a report that ships a Key-Findings block scores key_findings>0 instead of 0.
84	    count_atx_kf = _count_atx_key_findings_enabled()
85	    # I-wire-013 (#1327): ALSO match an ATX ``## Key Findings`` header that appears INSIDE a section's
86	    # CONTENT - not only the bold ``**Key Findings**`` form, and not only when ``split_report_into_
87	    # sections`` lifts it to a TITLE. Pipeline B scores whole-report ``content`` blobs whose depth /
88	    # analytical-synthesis block carries an ATX ``## Key Findings`` / ``### <title>`` header inline; the
89	    # bold-only regex scored those 0. Broadening can ONLY RAISE the advisory count (RC-8 is a heuristic,
90	    # never the faithfulness engine), so it makes the depth heuristic EASIER to pass; ``=0`` (the
91	    # ``PG_DEPTH_COUNT_ATX_KEY_FINDINGS`` kill-switch) restores the exact bold-only count for RC-8 parity.
92	    if count_atx_kf:
93	        key_findings_pattern = re.compile(
94	            r'\*\*Key Findings?\*\*|^#{1,6}\s+Key Findings?\b', re.I | re.M
95	        )
96	    else:
97	        key_findings_pattern = re.compile(r'\*\*Key Findings?\*\*', re.I)
98	    atx_kf_title_pattern = re.compile(r'^(?:Key Findings?|Analytical synthesis)$', re.I)
99	
100	    total_comparison = 0
101	    total_aggregation = 0
102	    total_challenge = 0
103	    total_tables = 0
104	    total_key_findings = 0
105	    deficient = []
106	
107	    for section in report_sections:
108	        content = section.get("content", "")
109	        comp = len(comparison_markers.findall(content))
110	        agg = len(aggregation_markers.findall(content))
111	        chal = len(challenge_markers.findall(content))
112	        tables = len(table_pattern.findall(content))
113	        kf = len(key_findings_pattern.findall(content))
114	        # I-wire-012 (#1326): count a section whose TITLE is the aggregated "Key Findings" / the
115	        # "Analytical synthesis" header (the ATX block split_report_into_sections lifts to the title).
116	        if count_atx_kf and atx_kf_title_pattern.match((section.get("title", "") or "").strip()):
117	            kf += 1
118	
119	        total_comparison += comp
120	        total_aggregation += agg
121	        total_challenge += chal
122	        total_tables += tables
123	        total_key_findings += kf
124	
125	        ops_present = sum([comp > 0, agg > 0, chal > 0, tables > 0, kf > 0])
126	        if ops_present < 2:
127	            deficient.append(section.get("title", "?"))
128	
129	    passed = (
130	        total_comparison >= 10 and
131	        total_tables >= 2 and
132	        total_key_findings >= 3 and
133	        total_challenge >= 3 and
134	        len(deficient) <= 2
135	    )
136	
137	    return {
138	        "passed": passed,
139	        "comparison_markers": total_comparison,
140	        "aggregation_patterns": total_aggregation,
141	        "challenge_markers": total_challenge,
142	        "tables": total_tables,
143	        "key_findings": total_key_findings,
144	        "deficient_sections": deficient,
145	    }
146	
147	
148	def split_report_into_sections(report_md: str) -> list[dict]:
149	    """Split an assembled markdown report into ``[{"title", "content"}]`` on ATX headers.
150	
151	    Used by the benchmark advisory annotation so the depth metric covers the **full delivered report**
152	    (front Key Findings block, body sections, Trial Summary tables, Per-Trial subsections, Limitations,
153	    and the V30 Methods disclosure if present) — not just the body ``verified_text``.
154	
155	    Rules:
156	    - Each line matching ``^#{1,6}\\s+<title>`` starts a new section; ``<title>`` is the header text.
157	    - Text BEFORE the first header becomes a single ``{"title": "Preamble", "content": ...}`` section
158	      (so a leading block with no header is still scored).
159	    - The header line itself is excluded from the section ``content`` (only the body text is scored),
160	      matching Pipeline B, which scores ``section["content"]`` (body), not the heading.
161	    - Empty / whitespace-only input → ``[]``. A report with no headers → one ``Preamble`` section.
162	    """
163	    if not report_md or not report_md.strip():
164	        return []
165	
166	    sections: list[dict] = []
167	    current_title = "Preamble"
168	    current_lines: list[str] = []
169	
170	    def _flush() -> None:
171	        content = "\n".join(current_lines).strip()
172	        # Drop a leading empty Preamble (report that opens directly with a header), but keep any
173	        # later empty section so the deficient-section accounting stays faithful to the structure.
174	        if content or current_title != "Preamble" or sections:
175	            sections.append({"title": current_title, "content": content})
176	
177	    for line in report_md.splitlines():
178	        match = _ATX_HEADER_RE.match(line)
179	        if match:
180	            _flush()
181	            current_title = match.group(2).strip() or "?"
182	            current_lines = []
183	        else:
184	            current_lines.append(line)
185	    _flush()
186	
187	    return sections
188

content.content:
1	"""I-beatboth-005 (#1282) — the FAITHFUL ABSTRACTIVE WRITER for per-basket verified-compose.
2	
3	Replaces the deterministic ``build_short_member_sentence`` render-probe stub (verified_compose.py)
4	that ships SPAN-FAITHFUL but BROKEN prose (dangling markdown-link fragments, orphaned section
5	numbers, HR/Reddit chrome) with an LLM writer that rephrases each verified span into CLEAN plain
6	declarative news-style prose carrying the EXACT canonical provenance token + every span numeric
7	verbatim. Each rewritten sentence is RE-RUN through the UNCHANGED ``verify_sentence_provenance``
8	(+ the existing region gate) by the unchanged ``_compose_one_basket`` loop; on FAIL the loop falls
9	back to today's verbatim K-span. Default-OFF behind ``PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER``; byte-identical OFF.
10	
11	ARCHITECTURE (design §3, ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_DESIGN.md):
12	  * The writer is async (OpenRouter) but ``_compose_one_basket`` calls ``writer_fn`` SYNCHRONOUSLY.
13	    An async PRE-PASS (:func:`abstractive_pre_pass`) precomputes one verified draft per basket up
14	    front (LLM + bounded retry, bounded-parallel); the sync ``writer_fn``
15	    (:func:`make_abstractive_writer_fn`) is a pure dict lookup keyed by the basket's canonical id.
16	  * The writer-specific verify wrapper (:func:`make_writer_verify_fn`) is STRICTER than the K-span
17	    path — the four P1 closures, all in the wrapper / pre-pass, ENGINE UNTOUCHED:
18	      - P1-1: a TRANSPORT entailment ``judge_error`` is a WRITER FAILURE (the wrapper forces
19	        ``is_verified=False`` -> retry -> K-span). An advisory judge-error never ships as a
20	        paraphrase, only ever as the grounded-by-construction K-span.
21	      - P1-2: the wrapper verifies with ``allow_local_window_fallback=False`` (the K-span path keeps
22	        the ``True`` default via the unwrapped ``verify_fn``); a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED bound span
23	        cannot pass on a same-row local window.
24	      - P1-3: a numeric COMPLETENESS guard (span->sentence) — every substantive span numeric must
25	        appear verbatim in the rewrite, else ``is_verified=False`` -> retry -> K-span. The
26	        guard reuses the ENGINE'S OWN substantive-numeral definition (``_decimals_in`` /
27	        ``_numbers_in`` / ``_INTEGER_PERCENT_RE`` / ``_strip_dose_patterns``), so "substantive"
28	        means the same thing in both directions; it lives HERE, never in ``verify_sentence_provenance``.
29	      - P1-4: the async/sync adapter above.
30	  * Fail-closed activation (design §3.6 / §5.5): the writer REFUSES to activate (raises, fail-LOUD,
31	    LAW II) unless ``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT`` resolves to ``enforce`` — the writer's only
32	    semantic guarantee for a paraphrase IS the entailment leg. The env guard is the ACTIVATION
33	    precondition; the per-call ``judge_error`` demotion (P1-1) is the call-time enforcement.
34	
35	FAITHFULNESS: ``verify_sentence_provenance`` / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / ``build_verified_span_draft``
36	/ ``_compose_one_basket`` / ``_compose_section_per_basket`` / the region gate are UNTOUCHED. Every
37	abstractive rewrite is re-run through the unchanged verifier + region gate; fabrication degrades to
38	the verbatim K-span. Always-release; never strand; never empty.
39	"""
40	from __future__ import annotations
41	
42	import asyncio
43	import dataclasses
44	import logging
45	import math
46	import os
47	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	
51	# ── env knobs (LAW VI — all parameters env-configurable; defaults documented) ────────────────────
52	_ENV_ENABLE = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER"
53	# Model: the writer is a GENERATOR-role call (design §5.6 / §9.1.8). The campaign generator arm =
54	# z-ai/glm-5.2 (config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml generator.model_slug), which is also
55	# the operator-specified default for #1282. Resolves to the GENERATOR-role slug at call time so it
56	# tracks the lock (and the campaign's all-GLM-5.2 override) rather than hardcoding a value that
57	# could drift from the lock; env-overridable per LAW VI.
58	_ENV_MODEL = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MODEL"
59	_DEFAULT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"
60	_ENV_MAX_RETRIES = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_RETRIES"
61	_DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES = 1                       # 2 total attempts (design §5.2)
62	_ENV_MAX_TOKENS = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS"
63	# §9.1.8 "never starve": 2048 STARVED the writer. GLM-5.2 reaches the _ALWAYS_REASON branch
64	# (openrouter_client.py:1778), which (a) floors max_tokens at PG_GLM5_MIN_MAX_TOKENS=4096 and (b)
65	# burns a real reasoning budget BEFORE content — so a 2048 cap is bumped to 4096 then largely
66	# consumed by reasoning, truncating the rephrase on a many-span basket -> empty/degraded synthesis.
67	# OpenRouter /api/v1/models reports z-ai/glm-5.2 top_provider.max_completion_tokens=32768
68	# (context_length 1,048,576), verified 2026-06-25. Per §9.1.8 ("reasoning effort + max_tokens ALWAYS
69	# go MAX", "set max_tokens to the model's REAL OpenRouter limit") the default is the FULL 32768 provider
70	# cap: ~8x headroom over the 4096 GLM floor, so GLM's reasoning budget can run high WITHOUT truncating
71	# the content rephrase on a many-span basket. max_tokens is a CAP not a target (billed by actual usage),
72	# so the generous ceiling is free insurance — never starve the writer.
73	_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 32768
74	_ENV_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS"
75	# I-wire-009 (#1323) SUPERSEDES the prior I-wire-005 effort=high posture for this leg. The
76	# floor_abstractive writer is the LOCKED active composer and reaches the GLM-5.2 _ALWAYS_REASON
77	# branch (openrouter_client.py:1778). The I-wire-005 assumption — that a generous 32768 content
78	# ceiling makes an UNCAPPED effort=high reasoning pool safe — is exactly what this issue disproves:
79	# at the outline leg GLM-5.2 spent the whole budget reasoning and returned content="" regardless of
80	# the ceiling. So BOUND the reasoning pool by default (a fixed reasoning_max_tokens is mutually
81	# exclusive with effort on the GLM branch, openrouter_client.py:1784-1788 — the cap deliberately
82	# suppresses effort=high here). 16384 matches the analyst-synthesis + legacy-section siblings: a
83	# generous reasoning slice that still leaves the bulk of the 32768 ceiling for the content rephrase.
84	# LAW VI: an operator may restore the uncapped effort=high posture for a NON-reasoning-first model
85	# by setting PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS<=0 (the <=0 -> None escape hatch below).
86	_DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS = 16384          # I-wire-009: bounded by default; <=0 via env => effort=high
87	_ENV_CONCURRENCY = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY"
88	_DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY = 8                       # bounded fan-out (§3.5), matches campaign verify fan-out
89	_ENV_TEMPERATURE = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_TEMPERATURE"
90	_DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE = 0.2                     # low — faithful rephrase, not creative
91	_ENV_CALL_DEADLINE_S = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S"
92	_DEFAULT_CALL_DEADLINE_S = 120.0               # per-call total deadline -> force-close to K-span (§3.5)
93	# OUTER wall-deadline on the WHOLE pre-pass (I-arch-007 wall+abandon pattern, async-native form).
94	# The per-call deadline (above) bounds ONE call; under a provider connection/429 storm the bounded pool
95	# of N calls + their retry backoffs can still balloon the pre-pass wall-clock with NO outer bound, and
96	# an asyncio.wait_for cancellation that stalls in httpx client teardown (the proven hang class) would
97	# wedge asyncio.gather forever. The pre-pass therefore runs the basket tasks under asyncio.wait(timeout)
98	# and ABANDONS (never awaits) any still-pending task at the wall -> those baskets are simply absent from
99	# the precomputed dict -> the unchanged compose loop K-span-falls-back (always-release, disclosed,
100	# fail-OPEN). Sized comfortably above the legit worst case (a healthy basket = (max_retries+1) *
101	# call_deadline ~= 240s; observed real waves ran ~180-200s) so HEALTHY baskets are never abandoned.
102	_ENV_WALL_DEADLINE_S = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S"
103	# Sized to NOT abandon HEALTHY baskets under uniform provider slowness. Worst-case makespan =
104	# ceil(n_baskets / concurrency) waves * (max_retries+1) attempts * call_deadline. At the default
105	# 23 baskets / 8 concurrency / 1 retry / 120s call = ceil(23/8)=3 waves * 2 * 120 = 720s. The wall is
106	# set to that worst case so a uniformly-slow-but-eventually-healthy run completes rather than abandons
107	# its last wave; observed real waves ran ~180-200s, so the wall only ever bites a genuinely stuck call.
108	_DEFAULT_WALL_DEADLINE_S = 720.0               # outer wall -> abandon stuck baskets to K-span (never infinite)
109	
110	# ── I-deepfix-001 B3/B4 (#1370): writer throughput + transport-resilience knobs (all DEFAULT-OFF) ──
111	# B4 root cause: the flat 720s wall was sized in-code for 23 baskets @ concurrency 8 (the makespan note
112	# above). drb_72 sections carry 71-113 baskets, so under a provider slowdown the wall exhausts and
113	# ABANDONS the whole still-pending wave to K-span (19/104, 14/86, 9/113 drafted). The fixes are all
114	# THROUGHPUT / RESILIENCE mechanisms, NEVER a blind time-wall raise:
115	#   * PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED — size the wall from the code's OWN makespan formula on the ACTUAL
116	#     basket count (not the frozen 23-basket budget). max(flat, scaled) so it only ever scales UP for
117	#     big sections and never shrinks below the proven-safe flat baseline. DEFAULT OFF => flat 720s.
118	#   * PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS — after the wall bites, run ONE bounded second pass over the still-
119	#     undrafted baskets before dumping them to K-span. DEFAULT OFF => the legacy immediate-abandon path.
120	#   * PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE — (a) _call_writer catches the httpx transport-disconnect family
121	#     (ConnectTimeout/ConnectError/RemoteProtocolError/ReadError) into a clean K-span so a raw
122	#     ConnectTimeout never escapes as an unretrieved-task leak; (b) a per-call transport stall does NOT
123	#     consume a PRODUCTIVE retry attempt (reconnect/stall time is not charged — a bounded set of fresh
124	#     reconnect windows is granted); (c) the outer wall gets one bounded reconnect-window of headroom.
125	#     DEFAULT OFF => byte-identical (no catch, single hard per-call deadline, no wall headroom).
126	# The bounded concurrency raise is B4's fourth lever but is an ENV-VALUE change only
127	# (PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY, already wired at _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY / line ~640) — the box runs
128	# verify at 30, so the relaunch sets 24. No code default changes here (§-1.3: no hardcoded target).
129	_ENV_WALL_BASKET_SCALED = "PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED"
130	_ENV_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS = "PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS"
131	_ENV_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE = "PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE"
132	
133	# Sentinel returned by _call_writer when (and only when) the transport-aware catch swallows an httpx
134	# disconnect — a value that can NEVER appear in real model content, so _pre_pass_one_basket can tell a
135	# transport disconnect apart from a genuine empty completion and never leak it into a draft.
136	_WRITER_TRANSPORT_DISCONNECT = "\x00__polaris_writer_transport_disconnect__\x00"
137	
138	
139	def _flag_enabled(name: str) -> bool:
140	    """Generic DEFAULT-OFF env flag (same falsey vocabulary as ``_abstractive_writer_enabled``)."""
141	    return os.getenv(name, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
142	
143	
144	def _wall_basket_scaled_enabled() -> bool:
145	    return _flag_enabled(_ENV_WALL_BASKET_SCALED)
146	
147	
148	def _kspan_recovery_pass_enabled() -> bool:
149	    return _flag_enabled(_ENV_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS)
150	
151	
152	def _deadline_transport_aware_enabled() -> bool:
153	    return _flag_enabled(_ENV_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE)
154	
155	
156	def _makespan_wall_seconds(
157	    n_baskets: int, concurrency: int, max_retries: int, call_deadline_s: float,
158	) -> float:
159	    """The code's OWN worst-case makespan for the pre-pass (design note at lines ~102-107):
160	    ``ceil(n_baskets / concurrency)`` waves * ``(max_retries + 1)`` attempts * ``call_deadline_s``.
161	
162	    Invariant proof (the frozen flat default): (23, 8, 1, 120.0) => ceil(23/8)=3 waves * 2 * 120 = 720.0,
163	    exactly ``_DEFAULT_WALL_DEADLINE_S`` — so the scaled wall is a generalization of the flat budget to
164	    the ACTUAL basket count, never an arbitrary raise. n_baskets<=0 => 0.0 (caller floors it)."""
165	    if n_baskets <= 0:
166	        return 0.0
167	    concurrency = max(1, concurrency)
168	    waves = math.ceil(n_baskets / concurrency)
169	    return float(waves) * float(max_retries + 1) * float(call_deadline_s)
170	
171	
172	# ── teardown-drain mechanism (I-wire-001 W6 #1314) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	# PROVEN pattern ported VERBATIM-IN-STRUCTURE from src/tools/access_bypass.py:1861-2026 (I-cd-032
174	# #632). A bare ``t.cancel()`` on a still-pending basket task is INSUFFICIENT: a task wedged inside
175	# httpx client teardown — or any task that swallows ``CancelledError`` and re-blocks — IGNORES the
176	# cancel and stays pending. ``asyncio.run``'s built-in shutdown then calls ``_cancel_all_tasks`` which
177	# ``gather``-awaits EVERY still-pending task BEFORE ``loop.close()`` — so one uncancellable task hangs
178	# the whole process at shutdown. Mitigation (mirrors access_bypass exactly): at the wall we (a) register
179	# each abandoned task in a module-level detached set, (b) ``cancel()`` it best-effort, then (c)
180	# FORCE-CLOSE its underlying coroutine via ``_coro.close()`` (raises GeneratorExit -> finally/except
181	# blocks run synchronously, no new await is possible, the task is finalized as ``done()``). A finalized
182	# task is excluded from ``_cancel_all_tasks``'s await-list, so shutdown completes. Belt-and-suspenders:
183	# :func:`install_teardown_drain_hook` ALSO patches the loop's ``_cancel_all_tasks`` phase so an
184	# UNTRACKED wedged task (not in the detached set) is force-closed before it is awaited. Faithfulness
185	# engine UNTOUCHED — this is purely a process-teardown safety net on the abandon (fail-open) path.
186	_DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS: "set[asyncio.Task]" = set()
187	
188	
189	def _drain_detached_writer_task(task: "asyncio.Task") -> None:
190	    """Done-callback for a detached basket task: drop the strong ref and retrieve any exception so
191	    asyncio does not log 'exception never retrieved'. Mirrors access_bypass._drain_detached."""
192	    _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.discard(task)
193	    if not task.cancelled():
194	        try:
195	            task.exception()
196	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — exception retrieval is best-effort
197	            pass
198	
199	
200	def _force_drop_detached_writer_task(task: "asyncio.Task") -> None:
201	    """Forcibly finalize a wedged detached basket task so ``asyncio.run``'s ``_cancel_all_tasks``
202	    cannot await it. Closes the task's underlying coroutine via ``_coro.close()`` — that raises
203	    GeneratorExit into the coroutine's current suspension point, runs any finally/except blocks
204	    SYNCHRONOUSLY (GeneratorExit suppresses new yields, so the frame cannot re-block), and finalizes
205	    the task as cancelled/done. Best-effort: if ``_coro`` is absent the strong ref is simply dropped.
206	    Mirrors access_bypass._force_drop_detached_task (I-cd-032 #632)."""
207	    if task.done():
208	        _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.discard(task)
209	        return
210	    coro = getattr(task, "_coro", None)
211	    if coro is None:
212	        _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.discard(task)
213	        return
214	    try:
215	        coro.close()
216	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — close() must never raise here
217	        pass
218	    _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.discard(task)
219	
220	
221	def install_teardown_drain_hook(loop: "asyncio.AbstractEventLoop") -> None:
222	    """Belt-and-suspenders teardown guard. ``asyncio.run`` calls the loop's ``_cancel_all_tasks``
223	    phase (which ``gather``-awaits every pending task) BEFORE ``loop.close()``, so an UNTRACKED wedged
224	    task — one not in :data:`_DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS` — would still hang shutdown. This patches
225	    ``loop.close`` to first force-close every still-tracked detached task; the primary defense remains
226	    the abandon-time force-close in :func:`abstractive_pre_pass`, which finalizes tracked tasks before
227	    ``_cancel_all_tasks`` ever runs. Idempotent. Mirrors access_bypass.install_teardown_drain_hook."""
228	    if getattr(loop, "_polaris_writer_drain_installed", False):
229	        return
230	    original_close = loop.close
231	
232	    def _drain_then_close() -> None:
233	        for task in list(_DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS):
234	            _force_drop_detached_writer_task(task)
235	        original_close()
236	
237	    loop.close = _drain_then_close  # type: ignore[method-assign]
238	    try:
239	        loop._polaris_writer_drain_installed = True  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
240	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — marker is best-effort
241	        pass

292	# ── P1-3 numeric COMPLETENESS guard helpers (reuse the ENGINE'S substantive-numeral definition) ──
293	def _substantive_span_numerics(span_text: str) -> set[str]:
294	    """The set of SUBSTANTIVE numerals in a span, using the ENGINE'S OWN definition so "substantive"
295	    means the same thing in both directions (design §3.2c): every decimal (``_decimals_in`` over
296	    dose-stripped text) PLUS every percent-expressed integer (``_INTEGER_PERCENT_RE``). Structural /
297	    study-marker integers (``STEP 1``, ``week 68``, ``104 weeks``, ``phase 3``) are NOT substantive
298	    — the engine's sentence->span numeric check exempts them via ``_DECIMAL_NUMBER_RE``, so the
299	    reverse span->sentence guard must exempt them too (else it would demand study markers the engine
300	    itself ignores and force a universal K-span no-op)."""
301	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
302	        _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE,
303	        _decimals_in,
304	        _numbers_in,
305	        _strip_dose_patterns,
306	    )
307	    stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(span_text or "")
308	    decimals = _decimals_in(stripped)
309	    # Percent-expressed integers (e.g. "50%", "19 percent") ARE claimed values; capture them but
310	    # exclude any that are already decimals.
311	    pct_ints = {m.group(1) for m in _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE.finditer(stripped)} - decimals
312	    # Restrict pct_ints to genuine integers present as standalone numbers in the span (mirrors the
313	    # engine's claimed_pct_ints handling, which subtracts decimals and checks integer membership).
314	    span_numbers = _numbers_in(stripped)
315	    pct_ints = {n for n in pct_ints if n in span_numbers}
316	    return decimals | pct_ints
317	
318	
319	def _numeral_appears_verbatim(numeral: str, sentence: str) -> bool:
320	    """True iff ``numeral`` appears verbatim among the sentence's numbers (after the same
321	    dose-strip + unicode-minus normalization the engine applies), so e.g. "13.0" vs "13" is not a
322	    false match. Reuses ``_numbers_in`` so extraction matches the engine exactly."""
323	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
324	        _numbers_in,
325	        _strip_dose_patterns,
326	    )
327	    return numeral in _numbers_in(_strip_dose_patterns(sentence or ""))

330	def _cited_span_text_for(tokens: list, scoped_pool: dict) -> str:
331	    """The combined CITED sub-span text for the parsed provenance ``tokens`` against ``scoped_pool``,
332	    i.e. ``direct_quote[token.start:token.end]`` per token (NOT the whole row — the whole-row text
333	    would demand OTHER claims' numerics and force a universal K-span no-op). Used by the
334	    completeness guard's span->sentence direction. Bounds-safe (out-of-range slices to "")."""
335	    parts: list[str] = []
336	    for tok in tokens or []:
337	        eid = str(getattr(tok, "evidence_id", "") or "")
338	        row = (scoped_pool or {}).get(eid) or {}
339	        haystack = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
340	        start = int(getattr(tok, "start", -1))
341	        end = int(getattr(tok, "end", -1))
342	        if 0 <= start < end <= len(haystack):
343	            parts.append(haystack[start:end])
344	    return " ".join(parts)

347	def make_writer_verify_fn(base_verify: Callable[..., Any]) -> Callable[..., Any]:
348	    """The WRITER-SPECIFIC verify wrapper injected as ``_compose_one_basket``'s ``verify_fn``
349	    (design §3.2). Pure (verify-only; no retry, no LLM). The K-span path keeps the bare
350	    ``base_verify`` (default ``allow_local_window_fallback=True``); ONLY the writer path wraps it.
351	    ``_compose_one_basket``'s signature + body are byte-identical — it just receives a different
352	    ``verify_fn``. The wrapper is STRICTER than the K-span path:
353	
354	      P1-2: verifies with ``allow_local_window_fallback=False`` (no same-row local-window rescue).
355	      P1-1: a TRANSPORT entailment ``judge_error`` (the durable ``SentenceVerification.judge_error``
356	            field) is forced to ``is_verified=False`` (``writer_judge_error_fail_closed``).
357	      P1-3: a DROPPED substantive span numeric (span->sentence completeness) forces
358	            ``is_verified=False`` (``writer_numeric_dropped``).
359	    """
360	
361	    def _wrapped(sentence: str, scoped_pool: dict, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
362	        # P1-2: pin the local-window loophole shut for the writer path. (The K-span path keeps the
363	        # bare base_verify default True via the unwrapped verify_fn — the shared default is untouched.)
364	        kwargs.setdefault("allow_local_window_fallback", False)
365	        res = base_verify(sentence, scoped_pool, *args, **kwargs)
366	
367	        # P1-1: a TRANSPORT entailment judge_error is a WRITER FAILURE. The shared verifier (with
368	        # PG_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_ADVISORY=1, the I-arch-010 default) advisory-KEEPS such a result
369	        # as is_verified=True and sets the durable judge_error=True marker. That advisory keep is
370	        # CORRECT for the deterministic K-span (a verbatim substring is grounded by construction) but
371	        # WRONG for an abstractive paraphrase, whose ONLY semantic guarantee is the entailment leg.
372	        # Read the BOOL FIELD (the canonical marker), not the soft_warnings string.
373	        if getattr(res, "judge_error", False) and bool(getattr(res, "is_verified", False)):
374	            res = dataclasses.replace(
375	                res,
376	                is_verified=False,
377	                failure_reasons=[*list(getattr(res, "failure_reasons", []) or []),
378	                                 "writer_judge_error_fail_closed"],
379	            )
380	
381	        # P1-3: span->sentence numeric COMPLETENESS (the reverse direction the engine does NOT check).
382	        # Only meaningful when the sentence is otherwise verified (a sentence already failing for
383	        # another reason needs no completeness escalation). The cited span text is resolved from the
384	        # parsed tokens of the INPUT sentence against the scoped pool (the exact cited sub-spans), so
385	        # the denominator is the writer's own cited spans — never the whole row.
386	        if bool(getattr(res, "is_verified", False)):
387	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
388	                parse_provenance_tokens,
389	            )
390	            cited_span_text = _cited_span_text_for(parse_provenance_tokens(sentence), scoped_pool)
391	            span_numerics = _substantive_span_numerics(cited_span_text)
392	            if span_numerics and not all(
393	                _numeral_appears_verbatim(n, sentence) for n in span_numerics
394	            ):
395	                res = dataclasses.replace(
396	                    res,
397	                    is_verified=False,
398	                    failure_reasons=[*list(getattr(res, "failure_reasons", []) or []),
399	                                     "writer_numeric_dropped"],
400	                )
401	        return res
402	
403	    return _wrapped
404

406	# ── the writer prompt contract (design §3.3) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
407	_WRITER_SYSTEM = (
408	    "You rewrite already-verified evidence spans into clean, plain, declarative news-style "
409	    "sentences. You NEVER add a fact that is not in a provided span. You copy every number "
410	    "(decimal, percent, integer, dose) exactly as written — never round, never convert units. "
411	    "You end each sentence with the exact provenance token supplied for the span it rephrases, "
412	    "copied character-for-character; you never invent or edit a token. You write subject-verb-object "
413	    "sentences that name the specific finding, number, and actor. You copy every epistemic or "
414	    "scope qualifier bound to a number exactly as written — hedges ('may', 'approximately', "
415	    "'up to'), non-factive verbs ('estimated', 'projected', 'suggests'), source attribution "
416	    "('according to', 'reportedly'), and conditional / scenario restrictors ('if', 'under the "
417	    "... scenario') — never restate a hedged or conditional figure as a settled fact. You do NOT "
418	    "use markdown, links, "
419	    "bullets, headings, section numbers, captions, or academic chrome like 'this study' or "
420	    "'the framework'. You output exactly one sentence per span, nothing else."
421	)
422	
423	
424	# I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344) — the GROUP writer contract. IDENTICAL faithfulness rules to
425	# ``_WRITER_SYSTEM`` (never a fact outside a span; every number verbatim; every sentence ends with its
426	# exact provenance token copied char-for-char; every epistemic/scope qualifier preserved; no markdown /
427	# chrome) EXCEPT the final clause: instead of "one sentence per span" the group contract asks for ONE
428	# coherent connected multi-sentence narrative over a GROUP of verified spans. Selected at call time by
429	# ``group_mode`` (default OFF => never referenced) so ``_WRITER_SYSTEM`` stays byte-unchanged and the
430	# gate-B force-set ``PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER`` path is byte-identical when ``PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY`` is unset.
431	_WRITER_SYSTEM_GROUP = (
432	    "You rewrite already-verified evidence spans into clean, plain, declarative news-style "
433	    "sentences. You NEVER add a fact that is not in a provided span. You copy every number "
434	    "(decimal, percent, integer, dose) exactly as written — never round, never convert units. "
435	    "You end each sentence with the exact provenance token supplied for the span it rephrases, "
436	    "copied character-for-character; you never invent or edit a token. You write subject-verb-object "
437	    "sentences that name the specific finding, number, and actor. You copy every epistemic or "
438	    "scope qualifier bound to a number exactly as written — hedges ('may', 'approximately', "
439	    "'up to'), non-factive verbs ('estimated', 'projected', 'suggests'), source attribution "
440	    "('according to', 'reportedly'), and conditional / scenario restrictors ('if', 'under the "
441	    "... scenario') — never restate a hedged or conditional figure as a settled fact. You do NOT "
442	    "use markdown, links, "
443	    "bullets, headings, section numbers, captions, or academic chrome like 'this study' or "
444	    "'the framework'. Write ONE coherent, connected multi-sentence narrative that covers this "
445	    "GROUP of verified spans in a logical order; each sentence ends with the exact provenance "
446	    "token(s) of the span(s) it rests on; you may order and connect the facts with plain "
447	    "connectives, but never state a fact not present in a provided span, and never merge two "
448	    "spans' numbers into a new aggregate."
449	)

452	def _build_writer_prompt(
453	    members: list,
454	    evidence_pool: dict,
455	    *,
456	    revise_reasons: Optional[list[str]] = None,
457	    group_mode: bool = False,
458	) -> str:
459	    """Build the user prompt: one SUPPORTS member per line, each given its verified span text and
460	    the EXACT canonical token (the same ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` ``build_verified_span_draft``
461	    would emit, computed by ``_member_global_span``). The writer rephrases each span into one
462	    declarative sentence ending with that token. On a retry, the specific wrapper failure reasons
463	    are fed back (RARR-style revise)."""
464	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import _member_global_span  # noqa: PLC0415
465	
466	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): the lead instruction is the ONLY difference in group mode; the
467	    # spans+tokens block and the revise_reasons block below are UNCHANGED. group_mode=False =>
468	    # byte-identical to the pre-Wave-1a prompt.
469	    if group_mode:
470	        lead = (
471	            "Write ONE connected paragraph covering ALL the verified spans below, in a logical "
472	            "order; each sentence ends with the exact provenance token for the span(s) it rests on, "
473	            "copied character-for-character. Copy every number verbatim. Order and connect the facts "
474	            "with plain connectives, but never state a fact not present in a provided span, and "
475	            "never merge two spans' numbers into a new aggregate."
476	        )
477	    else:
478	        lead = (
479	            "Rewrite each verified evidence span below into ONE clean, plain, declarative "
480	            "news-style sentence. End each sentence with the exact provenance token shown for it, "
481	            "copied character-for-character. Copy every number verbatim. Output one sentence per span, "
482	            "in order, one per line, and nothing else."
483	        )
484	    lines: list[str] = [lead, ""]
485	    for i, m in enumerate(members, start=1):
486	        eid = str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "")
487	        gspan = _member_global_span(m, evidence_pool)
488	        quote = str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
489	        if not eid or gspan is None or not quote:
490	            continue
491	        start, end = gspan
492	        token = f"[#ev:{eid}:{start}-{end}]"
493	        lines.append(f"SPAN {i}: {quote}")
494	        lines.append(f"TOKEN {i} (append verbatim to sentence {i}): {token}")
495	        lines.append("")
496	    if revise_reasons:
497	        lines.append(
498	            "Your previous attempt FAILED verification for these reasons — fix them and try again:"
499	        )
500	        for r in revise_reasons:

content.text: This is heavily documented but names look professional/technical (no marketing adjectives found so far). Let's continue reading the rest of this file.

content.content:
500	        for r in revise_reasons:
501	            lines.append(f"  - {r}")
502	        lines.append("")
503	    return "\n".join(lines)

506	# ── the async per-basket LLM writer call ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
507	async def _call_writer(
508	    members: list,
509	    evidence_pool: dict,
510	    *,
511	    model: str,
512	    max_tokens: int,
513	    reasoning_max_tokens: int,
514	    temperature: float,
515	    revise_reasons: Optional[list[str]] = None,
516	    group_mode: bool = False,
517	    catch_transport: bool = False,
518	) -> str:
519	    """ONE LLM writer call for a basket: rephrase the SUPPORTS members' verified spans into clean
520	    declarative prose carrying the canonical tokens. Returns the raw draft text (re-verified by the
521	    unchanged compose loop). On any error returns "" -> the loop falls back to the K-span (fail-loud
522	    to the K-span, never a silent crash).
523	
524	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): ``group_mode`` selects the GROUP writer contract
525	    (``_WRITER_SYSTEM_GROUP`` + the connected-paragraph lead) — one coherent multi-sentence narrative
526	    over the whole basket's spans instead of one sentence per span. Default False => byte-identical.
527	
528	    I-deepfix-001 B3 (#1370): ``catch_transport`` (set only by the transport-aware pre-pass branch)
529	    catches the httpx transport-DISCONNECT family — ConnectTimeout / ConnectError / RemoteProtocolError
530	    / ReadError — and returns the ``_WRITER_TRANSPORT_DISCONNECT`` sentinel instead of letting the
531	    exception escape as an unretrieved-task leak ("Task exception was never retrieved"). Default False =>
532	    NO catch => byte-identical (the exception propagates exactly as before, K-span via the caller)."""
533	    import httpx  # noqa: PLC0415 — local import (transport-disconnect classes; B3 catch only)
534	
535	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
536	
537	    prompt = _build_writer_prompt(
538	        members, evidence_pool, revise_reasons=revise_reasons, group_mode=group_mode,
539	    )
540	    system = _WRITER_SYSTEM_GROUP if group_mode else _WRITER_SYSTEM
541	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
542	    # §9.1.8: a NEGATIVE/zero reasoning cap is the "unset" sentinel -> pass None so GLM-5.2's
543	    # _ALWAYS_REASON branch runs at effort=high (its default) instead of a starving fixed cap.
544	    reasoning_arg = reasoning_max_tokens if reasoning_max_tokens and reasoning_max_tokens > 0 else None
545	    try:
546	        response = await client.generate(
547	            prompt=prompt,
548	            system=system,
549	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
550	            temperature=temperature,
551	            reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_arg,
552	        )
553	        return str(getattr(response, "content", "") or "")
554	    except (
555	        httpx.ConnectTimeout,
556	        httpx.ConnectError,
557	        httpx.RemoteProtocolError,
558	        httpx.ReadError,
559	    ) as exc:
560	        # B3: only the transport-aware branch requests this catch. It converts a transport DISCONNECT
561	        # into a clean sentinel (the caller then grants a fresh reconnect window without charging a
562	        # productive attempt, and the basket K-span-falls-back if the budget is exhausted) — the raw
563	        # ConnectTimeout never orphans as an unretrieved task. Flag OFF => re-raise => legacy behavior.
564	        if not catch_transport:
565	            raise
566	        logger.warning(
567	            "[abstractive_writer] writer call transport-disconnect (%s) -> clean reconnect sentinel",
568	            type(exc).__name__,
569	        )
570	        return _WRITER_TRANSPORT_DISCONNECT
571	    finally:
572	        try:
573	            await client.close()
574	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001  — best-effort teardown; never mask the writer result
575	            logger.debug("[abstractive_writer] client.close() raised on teardown", exc_info=True)
576
