578	def _draft_passes_wrapper(
579	    draft: str,
580	    basket: Any,
581	    evidence_pool: dict,
582	    writer_verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
583	) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
584	    """Verify a candidate draft with the WRITER WRAPPER + region gate against the basket-scoped pool
585	    — exactly the gate ``_compose_one_basket`` will apply — to decide whether the pre-pass needs a
586	    retry. Returns (all_sentences_pass, failure_reasons). This MIRRORS the loop's accept condition;
587	    the loop remains the authoritative gate (the pre-pass only proposes)."""
588	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
589	        _basket_member_regions,
590	        _basket_scoped_pool,
591	        _tokens_within_basket_regions,
592	        split_into_sentences,
593	    )
594	    scoped_pool = _basket_scoped_pool(basket, evidence_pool)
595	    regions = _basket_member_regions(basket, evidence_pool)
596	    sentences = split_into_sentences(draft or "")
597	    if not sentences:
598	        return False, ["writer_empty_draft"]
599	    reasons: list[str] = []
600	    for sentence in sentences:
601	        res = writer_verify_fn(sentence, scoped_pool)
602	        verified_text = str(getattr(res, "sentence", "") or "").strip() or sentence.strip()
603	        if not (bool(getattr(res, "is_verified", False))
604	                and _tokens_within_basket_regions(verified_text, regions)):
605	            rs = list(getattr(res, "failure_reasons", []) or [])
606	            reasons.extend(rs or ["writer_sentence_rejected"])
607	            return False, reasons
608	    return True, reasons
609	
610	
611	async def _pre_pass_one_basket(
612	    basket: Any,
613	    evidence_pool: dict,
614	    *,
615	    writer_verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
616	    model: str,
617	    max_retries: int,
618	    max_tokens: int,
619	    reasoning_max_tokens: int,
620	    temperature: float,
621	    call_deadline_s: float,
622	    group_mode: bool = False,
623	) -> Optional[str]:
624	    """Compute one basket's draft: call the LLM writer, verify the candidate with the writer wrapper,
625	    and on failure retry up to ``max_retries`` times feeding the specific failure reasons back. The
626	    LAST attempt's draft is returned (even if failing) — the unchanged compose loop re-verifies it
627	    and falls back to the K-span if it does not pass (the pre-pass NEVER emits the K-span itself).
628	    Returns None only when the basket has no resolvable SUPPORTS member (writer skipped).
629	
630	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): ``group_mode`` selects the GROUP writer contract on the attempt-0
631	    draft (one coherent multi-sentence narrative over the whole basket) so the SYNTH_PRIMARY keystone
632	    effect materializes on the FIRST draft — not only after a compose-level repair. Default False =>
633	    byte-identical single-sentence-per-span pre-pass."""
634	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
635	        _basket_supports_members,
636	        _compose_junk_screen,
637	    )
638	
639	    members = _basket_supports_members(basket)
640	    if not members:
641	        return None
642	
643	    # §3.1 input-screen (#1289, advisor 2026-06-21): drop chrome members BEFORE the LLM writer call.
644	    # The abstractive path emits via the precomputed dict, NOT build_verified_span_draft, so the §3.4
645	    # OUTPUT junk screen never runs on it — AND a paraphrase mangles the multi-word chrome markers, so
646	    # only an INPUT screen catches them. Reuse the §3.4 high-precision allowlist screen on each
647	    # member's verified span text. Faithfulness-safe (§-1.3): boilerplate is not a corroborating
648	    # source. A MIXED basket (chrome member + real member) keeps the real member + its citation; an
649	    # ALL-chrome basket leaves zero members -> writer skipped (None) -> the loop K-span-falls-back and
650	    # §3.4 screens that too, so the all-chrome basket emits nothing (it never cascades a REAL section
651	    # to empty — only baskets that carry no real content drop out).
652	    screened_members = [
653	        m for m in members
654	        if not _compose_junk_screen(str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or ""))
655	    ]
656	    if not screened_members:
657	        logger.info(
658	            "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s all SUPPORTS members screened as chrome -> writer skipped "
659	            "(K-span fallback)", _basket_key(basket),
660	        )
661	        return None
662	    members = screened_members
663	
664	    # I-deepfix-001 B3 (#1370): the transport-aware retry (default OFF => the verbatim legacy for-loop).
665	    if not _deadline_transport_aware_enabled():
666	        revise_reasons: Optional[list[str]] = None
667	        last_draft = ""
668	        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
669	            try:
670	                draft = await asyncio.wait_for(
671	                    _call_writer(
672	                        members, evidence_pool,
673	                        model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens,
674	                        reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_max_tokens, temperature=temperature,
675	                        revise_reasons=revise_reasons, group_mode=group_mode,
676	                    ),
677	                    timeout=call_deadline_s,
678	                )
679	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
680	                logger.warning(
681	                    "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s writer call exceeded %.0fs deadline (attempt %d) "
682	                    "-> K-span fallback", _basket_key(basket), call_deadline_s, attempt + 1,
683	                )
684	                break
685	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any writer error degrades to K-span, never crashes the run
686	                logger.warning(
687	                    "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s writer call raised (attempt %d) -> K-span fallback",
688	                    _basket_key(basket), attempt + 1, exc_info=True,
689	                )
690	                break
691	            last_draft = draft
692	            passed, reasons = _draft_passes_wrapper(draft, basket, evidence_pool, writer_verify_fn)
693	            if passed:
694	                return draft
695	            revise_reasons = reasons or None
696	        # Return the last (failing) draft; the unchanged compose loop will fall back to the K-span.
697	        return last_draft
698	
699	    # ── transport-aware branch (PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE ON) ────────────────────────────
700	    # A transport DISCONNECT / per-call stall does NOT consume a PRODUCTIVE retry attempt: it is granted
701	    # a FRESH ``call_deadline_s`` reconnect window (reconnect/stall time not charged), bounded by
702	    # ``max(1, max_retries)`` extra reconnect windows so a persistently-dead provider still degrades to
703	    # K-span in finite time (the outer wall is the hard ceiling). Only a completed writer draft that
704	    # FAILS verification burns a productive attempt — the productive budget is identical to the legacy
705	    # ``range(max_retries + 1)``. The _call_writer catch (catch_transport=True) makes the disconnect a
706	    # clean sentinel, killing the "Task exception was never retrieved" leak.
707	    revise_reasons = None
708	    last_draft = ""
709	    productive_attempt = 0
710	    transport_reattempt = 0
711	    max_transport_reattempts = max(1, max_retries)
712	    while productive_attempt <= max_retries:
713	        try:
714	            draft = await asyncio.wait_for(
715	                _call_writer(
716	                    members, evidence_pool,
717	                    model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens,
718	                    reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_max_tokens, temperature=temperature,
719	                    revise_reasons=revise_reasons, group_mode=group_mode,
720	                    catch_transport=True,
721	                ),
722	                timeout=call_deadline_s,
723	            )
724	        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
725	            # A per-call stall is a TRANSPORT event under transport-awareness: grant a fresh window
726	            # without charging a productive attempt, bounded by max_transport_reattempts.
727	            if transport_reattempt < max_transport_reattempts:
728	                transport_reattempt += 1
729	                logger.warning(
730	                    "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s writer call stalled at %.0fs -> fresh reconnect "
731	                    "window (transport %d/%d, productive %d/%d)",
732	                    _basket_key(basket), call_deadline_s, transport_reattempt,
733	                    max_transport_reattempts, productive_attempt + 1, max_retries + 1,
734	                )
735	                continue
736	            logger.warning(
737	                "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s writer stall reconnect budget exhausted -> K-span",
738	                _basket_key(basket),
739	            )
740	            break
741	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any non-transport writer error degrades to K-span
742	            logger.warning(
743	                "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s writer call raised -> K-span fallback",
744	                _basket_key(basket), exc_info=True,
745	            )
746	            break
747	        if draft == _WRITER_TRANSPORT_DISCONNECT:
748	            # A clean transport-disconnect sentinel: same treatment as a stall (fresh reconnect window,
749	            # no productive attempt charged, never leaked into last_draft).
750	            if transport_reattempt < max_transport_reattempts:
751	                transport_reattempt += 1
752	                logger.warning(
753	                    "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s transport-disconnect -> fresh reconnect window "
754	                    "(transport %d/%d, productive %d/%d)",
755	                    _basket_key(basket), transport_reattempt, max_transport_reattempts,
756	                    productive_attempt + 1, max_retries + 1,
757	                )
758	                continue
759	            logger.warning(
760	                "[abstractive_writer] basket=%s transport-disconnect budget exhausted -> K-span",
761	                _basket_key(basket),
762	            )
763	            break
764	        last_draft = draft
765	        passed, reasons = _draft_passes_wrapper(draft, basket, evidence_pool, writer_verify_fn)
766	        if passed:
767	            return draft
768	        revise_reasons = reasons or None
769	        productive_attempt += 1
770	    return last_draft
771	
772	
773	async def abstractive_pre_pass(
774	    baskets: list,
775	    evidence_pool: dict,
776	    *,
777	    writer_verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
778	    group_mode: bool = False,
779	) -> dict:
780	    """ASYNC pre-pass (design §3.4a): precompute one verified draft per basket up front, under a
781	    ``PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY`` semaphore with a per-call total deadline AND an OUTER
782	    wall-deadline. Keyed by the basket's canonical ``claim_cluster_id`` so the sync ``writer_fn`` is a
783	    deterministic dict lookup. A basket that the writer skipped/failed/was abandoned at the wall is
784	    simply absent (or maps to a failing draft) -> the sync writer_fn returns "" / the failing draft ->
785	    the unchanged loop K-span-falls-back. Never raises on a per-basket failure (always-release).
786	
787	    HANG FIX (I-wire-001 W6 #1314, I-arch-007 wall+abandon pattern in its async-native form): the
788	    per-call ``asyncio.wait_for(call_deadline_s)`` bounds ONE writer call, but under a provider
789	    connection/429 storm the bounded pool's calls + retry backoffs can balloon the pre-pass wall-clock
790	    with NO outer bound, and a ``wait_for`` cancellation that stalls inside httpx client teardown (the
791	    proven hang class) would wedge ``asyncio.gather`` forever (gather awaits EVERY task incl. an
792	    uncancellable one). So the basket tasks run under ``asyncio.wait(timeout=wall_deadline_s)`` and any
793	    still-pending task at the wall is ABANDONED (cancelled best-effort, NEVER awaited) — its basket is
794	    absent from ``out`` -> the loop K-span-falls-back (fail-OPEN, disclosed). The wall is finite and
795	    env-configurable (``PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S``); it is NEVER infinite."""
796	    model = _resolve_model()
797	    max_retries = max(0, _env_int(_ENV_MAX_RETRIES, _DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES))
798	    max_tokens = max(1, _env_int(_ENV_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS))
799	    reasoning_max_tokens = max(0, _env_int(_ENV_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS))
800	    temperature = _env_float(_ENV_TEMPERATURE, _DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE)
801	    call_deadline_s = max(1.0, _env_float(_ENV_CALL_DEADLINE_S, _DEFAULT_CALL_DEADLINE_S))
802	    wall_deadline_s = max(1.0, _env_float(_ENV_WALL_DEADLINE_S, _DEFAULT_WALL_DEADLINE_S))
803	    concurrency = max(1, _env_int(_ENV_CONCURRENCY, _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY))
804	
805	    # I-deepfix-001 B4 (#1370): basket-count-SCALED wall from the code's own makespan formula (NOT a
806	    # blind time-wall raise). The flat _DEFAULT_WALL_DEADLINE_S was sized for 23 baskets @ conc 8; a
807	    # 71-113-basket drb_72 section overruns it and abandons the whole wave. max(flat, scaled) scales UP
808	    # for big sections while NEVER shrinking below the proven-safe flat baseline for small ones. OFF =>
809	    # the flat/env wall is used verbatim. I-deepfix-001 B3: transport-awareness adds ONE bounded
810	    # reconnect-window of headroom so a wall that would bite purely on transport-stall gets one more
811	    # window before abandoning (finite; not a blind raise).
812	    n_keyed = sum(1 for b in (baskets or []) if _basket_key(b))
813	    if _wall_basket_scaled_enabled():
814	        scaled = _makespan_wall_seconds(n_keyed, concurrency, max_retries, call_deadline_s)
815	        if scaled > wall_deadline_s:
816	            logger.info(
817	                "[abstractive_writer] basket-scaled wall: n=%d conc=%d retries=%d call=%.0fs -> "
818	                "wall=%.0fs (flat floor %.0fs)",
819	                n_keyed, concurrency, max_retries, call_deadline_s, scaled, wall_deadline_s,
820	            )
821	            wall_deadline_s = scaled
822	    if _deadline_transport_aware_enabled():
823	        wall_deadline_s += call_deadline_s
824	
825	    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
826	    out: dict = {}
827	
828	    async def _one(basket: Any, semaphore: "asyncio.Semaphore") -> None:
829	        key = _basket_key(basket)
830	        if not key:
831	            return
832	        async with semaphore:
833	            draft = await _pre_pass_one_basket(
834	                basket, evidence_pool,
835	                writer_verify_fn=writer_verify_fn,
836	                model=model, max_retries=max_retries,
837	                max_tokens=max_tokens, reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_max_tokens,
838	                temperature=temperature, call_deadline_s=call_deadline_s,
839	                group_mode=group_mode,
840	            )
841	        if draft is not None:
842	            # mutate the shared dict as a SIDE EFFECT so an abandoned (never-awaited) task's
843	            # already-completed siblings are still captured — out is the source of truth, not gather().
844	            out[key] = draft
845	
846	    def _abandon_pending(pending_tasks: "set[asyncio.Task]") -> None:
847	        # ABANDON the stuck baskets — do NOT await (awaiting a task wedged in httpx teardown, or one
848	        # that swallows CancelledError and re-blocks, is the very hang we are bounding). A bare
849	        # t.cancel() is INSUFFICIENT for such a task. So we port the access_bypass detach/drain/
850	        # force-close pattern: register each abandoned task in the detached set, attach the drain
851	        # done-callback, cancel() best-effort, then FORCE-CLOSE its underlying coroutine via
852	        # _coro.close() so the task is finalized as done() NOW -> excluded from asyncio.run's
853	        # _cancel_all_tasks await-list -> shutdown cannot hang. Those baskets are absent from `out`
854	        # -> the compose loop K-span-falls-back. This is the wall's fail-OPEN, always-release behavior.
855	        for t in pending_tasks:
856	            _DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.add(t)
857	            t.add_done_callback(_drain_detached_writer_task)
858	            t.cancel()
859	            _force_drop_detached_writer_task(t)
860	
861	    def _surface_completed_exceptions(done_tasks: "set[asyncio.Task]") -> None:
862	        # Surface any non-cancellation exception from a COMPLETED task (never let one die silently) — a
863	        # per-basket writer error already degrades to "" inside _pre_pass_one_basket, so a completed
864	        # task raising here is a genuine unexpected fault worth logging (still non-fatal: always-release).
865	        for t in done_tasks:
866	            exc = t.exception()
867	            if exc is not None:
868	                logger.warning("[abstractive_writer] a pre-pass basket task raised: %r", exc)
869	
870	    tasks = [asyncio.ensure_future(_one(b, sem)) for b in (baskets or [])]
871	    if not tasks:
872	        logger.info("[abstractive_writer] pre-pass complete: 0/0 baskets drafted (model=%s)", model)
873	        return out
874	
875	    # Install the belt-and-suspenders teardown hook on the running loop BEFORE the wall can bite, so an
876	    # untracked wedged task is also force-closed at shutdown (the primary defense is the abandon-time
877	    # force-close below, which finalizes tracked tasks before _cancel_all_tasks ever runs).
878	    try:
879	        install_teardown_drain_hook(asyncio.get_running_loop())
880	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — hook install is best-effort, never fatal to the pre-pass
881	        logger.debug("[abstractive_writer] teardown-drain hook install skipped", exc_info=True)
882	
883	    done, pending = await asyncio.wait(tasks, timeout=wall_deadline_s)
884	    if pending:
885	        logger.warning(
886	            "[abstractive_writer] pre-pass WALL-DEADLINE %.0fs hit: ABANDONING %d/%d still-pending "
887	            "basket task(s) -> K-span fallback (fail-open, disclosed). %d drafted before the wall.",
888	            wall_deadline_s, len(pending), len(tasks), len(out),
889	        )
890	        _abandon_pending(pending)
891	    _surface_completed_exceptions(done)
892	
893	    # I-deepfix-001 B4 (#1370): bounded K-span RECOVERY second-pass. Before the still-undrafted baskets
894	    # fall to K-span, give them ONE more bounded pass with FRESH tasks (the abandoned originals were
895	    # wedged on a dead socket; a clean re-submit — especially with B3's fresh-connection routing — often
896	    # succeeds). Bounded by its own makespan wall over just the recovery set; anything still undrafted
897	    # after it K-span-falls-back exactly as before. DEFAULT OFF => this block is skipped => the legacy
898	    # immediate-abandon path is byte-identical. Faithfulness-neutral: K-span remains the ultimate
899	    # fallback and every recovered draft still passes through the unchanged compose-loop verifier.
900	    if pending and _kspan_recovery_pass_enabled():
901	        recovery_baskets = [
902	            b for b in (baskets or [])
903	            if _basket_key(b) and _basket_key(b) not in out
904	        ]
905	        if recovery_baskets:
906	            rec_wall = max(
907	                1.0, _makespan_wall_seconds(len(recovery_baskets), concurrency, max_retries, call_deadline_s),
908	            )
909	            logger.warning(
910	                "[abstractive_writer] K-span recovery pass: re-drafting %d still-undrafted basket(s) "
911	                "under a %.0fs bounded wall before K-span.", len(recovery_baskets), rec_wall,
912	            )
913	            rec_sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
914	            rec_before = len(out)
915	            rec_tasks = [asyncio.ensure_future(_one(b, rec_sem)) for b in recovery_baskets]
916	            rec_done, rec_pending = await asyncio.wait(rec_tasks, timeout=rec_wall)
917	            if rec_pending:
918	                logger.warning(
919	                    "[abstractive_writer] K-span recovery wall %.0fs hit: ABANDONING %d/%d recovery "
920	                    "task(s) -> K-span.", rec_wall, len(rec_pending), len(rec_tasks),
921	                )
922	                _abandon_pending(rec_pending)
923	            _surface_completed_exceptions(rec_done)
924	            logger.info(
925	                "[abstractive_writer] K-span recovery pass complete: %d/%d basket(s) recovered.",
926	                len(out) - rec_before, len(recovery_baskets),
927	            )
928	
929	    logger.info(
930	        "[abstractive_writer] pre-pass complete: %d/%d baskets drafted (model=%s, retries=%d, "
931	        "wall=%.0fs, abandoned=%d)",
932	        len(out), len(baskets or []), model, max_retries, wall_deadline_s, len(pending),
933	    )
934	    return out
935	
936	
937	def make_abstractive_writer_fn(precomputed: dict) -> Callable[[Any, dict], str]:
938	    """The SYNC ``writer_fn`` ``_compose_one_basket`` reads: a pure dict lookup keyed by the basket's
939	    canonical id. A MISSING key returns "" — the loop treats a writer-empty basket as a K-span
940	    fallback (never a crash). So a pre-pass that skipped/failed a basket degrades safely."""
941	
942	    def _writer_fn(basket: Any, _scoped_pool: dict) -> str:
943	        return str(precomputed.get(_basket_key(basket), "") or "")
944	
945	    return _writer_fn
946	
947	
948	def assert_activation_preconditions() -> None:
949	    """Fail-closed activation guard (design §3.6 / §5.5). When the writer is ON it REFUSES to
950	    activate (raises, fail-LOUD per LAW II — no silent downgrade) unless the entailment leg resolves
951	    to ``enforce``: the writer's ONLY semantic guarantee for a paraphrase is the entailment leg, so
952	    activating it with entailment ``off``/``warn`` would ship un-entailment-checked rewritten prose.
953	    The env guard is the ACTIVATION precondition; the per-call ``judge_error`` demotion (P1-1) is the
954	    per-sentence call-time enforcement — both are required."""
955	    from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import _entailment_mode  # noqa: PLC0415
956	
957	    mode = _entailment_mode()
958	    if mode != "enforce":
959	        raise RuntimeError(
960	            "abort_abstractive_writer_unsafe_activation: PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER is ON but "
961	            f"PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT resolves to {mode!r}, not 'enforce'. The abstractive "
962	            "writer's only semantic guarantee for a paraphrase is the entailment leg; activating it "
963	            "without entailment=enforce would ship un-entailment-checked rewritten prose. Set "
964	            "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce or disable PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER."

1	"""I-bug-105 — Analyst Synthesis (two-layer report contract).
2	
3	Per Codex strategic-review iter 1 + I-bug-105 brief APPROVE:
4	
5	POLARIS's verified core (the multi-section prose, all per-sentence
6	span-verified by the entailment gate) is the audit-grade differentiator.
7	But it is also short — ~14 verified sentences, ~974 words on a typical
8	clinical question — because strict_verify drops ~73% of attempted
9	sentences. Frontier DR systems (ChatGPT DR, Gemini DR) ship 4000-7000
10	words but without the audit guarantee.
11	
12	This module ships the SECOND layer: an "Analyst Synthesis" pass that
13	takes the verified prose + the bibliography + the evidence pool and
14	writes 1500-3000 words of interpretive expert commentary. The synthesis:
15	  - is CLEARLY labeled in report.md (not span-verified)
16	  - hedges appropriately ("consistent with verified findings...",
17	    "the literature suggests...", "in clinical practice this profile
18	    is consistent with...")
19	  - cites sources via bibliography [N] markers
20	  - MUST NOT carry [#ev:...] tokens (those are the audit-grade signal;
21	    using them in synthesis would dilute their meaning) — enforced by
22	    a scrub guardrail in this module + a regression test
23	  - is OMITTED from the report when generation fails (no empty disclosure
24	    block per Codex iter-1 guidance)
25	
26	The two-layer disclosure preserves POLARIS's faithfulness wedge while
27	materially closing the narrative_length gap to frontier DR.
28	"""
29	
30	from __future__ import annotations
31	
32	import logging
33	import os
34	import re
35	from collections import Counter
36	from typing import Any, Callable
37	
38	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
39	
40	
41	# D-1 / I-ready-017 (#1182): analyst-synthesis CONTENT token budget.
42	#
43	# The default writer (deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro per I-cd-009 Carney lock) is
44	# REASONING-FIRST: it emits 6k-42k+ reasoning tokens BEFORE the synthesis prose.
45	# The prior hardcoded `4000` ceiling starved the content phase, so
46	# finish_reason=length truncated mid-planning and the FX-01 (#1105)
47	# reasoning->content promotion guard correctly REFUSED to ship the scratchpad,
48	# omitting the synthesis. Per LAW VI this is a NAMED, env-overridable module
49	# constant (no magic number), defaulting generous so the writer has room to
50	# FINISH planning AND write the 1500-3000-word synthesis.
51	#
52	# IMPORTANT (scope honesty): openrouter_client clamps every reasoning-first
53	# request to PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP (default 16384, DeepInfra's verified
54	# deepseek-v4-pro cap). So on the DEFAULT provider this constant above 16384 is
55	# forward-compat HEADROOM, not active room (>16384 clamps down to 16384; <16384
56	# floors UP to 16384). It only takes effect once an operator points the writer at
57	# a higher-tier endpoint AND raises PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP above the model's
58	# reasoning burn. The truncation GUARD lives in openrouter_client's promotion
59	# path and is untouched here — we only widen the requested content budget.
60	PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS: int = int(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", "24000"))
61	
62	
63	# Disclosure preamble (Codex iter-1 suggested rewrite, verbatim).
64	ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE = (
65	    "This section is analyst synthesis: interpretive commentary based on "
66	    "the verified findings above and the cited evidence. Unlike the "
67	    "Verified Findings section, these sentences are not individually "
68	    "span-verified; use them as hedged context, not as audit-grade claims."
69	)
70	
71	
72	ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are an analyst writing the
73	"Analyst Synthesis" section of a research report. The earlier "Verified
74	Findings" section contains short, per-sentence span-verified prose. Your
75	job is to write a longer interpretive narrative that synthesizes those
76	verified findings with the broader evidence pool — the kind of expert
77	commentary a research analyst would write to contextualize the audit
78	core for the reader.
79	
80	CORE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
81	
82	1. **Cite by bibliography [N] markers, NEVER by [#ev:...] tokens.** The
83	   [#ev:...] tokens are POLARIS's audit-grade signal reserved for the
84	   Verified Findings section. Using them in synthesis would dilute their
85	   meaning. Only [1], [2], [3], etc. — bibliography numbers from the
86	   global bibliography list provided.
87	
88	2. **Hedge appropriately when going beyond the verified core.** Use
89	   phrases like:
90	     - "Consistent with the verified findings above, ..."
91	     - "The literature broadly suggests ..."
92	     - "These results are typically interpreted as ..."
93	     - "In clinical practice, this profile is consistent with ..."
94	     - "While not directly span-verified, the evidence base suggests ..."
95	   Do NOT make declarative claims about specifics that go beyond what
96	   the cited evidence supports. The reader is depending on the hedge
97	   wording to know which claims are interpretive vs. audited.
98	
99	3. **Do not introduce facts NOT supported by the evidence pool.** You
100	   have the same evidence rows the verified pipeline used. Synthesis is
101	   permitted to discuss implications, comparisons, mechanisms, clinical
102	   context, and trade-offs — but every concrete factual claim
103	   (numbers, study names, mechanisms, regulatory facts) MUST be
104	   traceable to the provided bibliography.
105	
106	4. **Reference the Verified Findings.** Make the synthesis flow from
107	   the audit core: "The verified efficacy data show X; clinically, this
108	   is interpreted as ...". This makes the two-layer architecture
109	   readable as a single document.
110	
111	STRUCTURE:
112	
113	Organize the synthesis into 4-6 sub-sections (your choice based on the
114	question + verified prose):
115	  - "Mechanism interpretation"
116	  - "Clinical implications"
117	  - "Comparative considerations"
118	  - "Regulatory and practice context"
119	  - "Open questions and future directions"
120	  - (or others appropriate to the domain)
121	
122	Use ### subheadings for each sub-section (NOT ## — those are reserved
123	for the parent section header that wraps the synthesis block). Total
124	length: 1500-3000 words.
125	
126	OUTPUT: plain prose with [N] citation markers and ### subheadings. No
127	preamble, no sign-off, no [#ev:...] tokens anywhere. Do NOT emit ##
128	headers — the renderer wraps this prose under a single ## heading."""
129	
130	
131	# I-meta-002-q1d (#946): match BOTH the prefixed audit token `[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]` AND a bare
132	# `[ev_012]` / `[ev_012:1-5]` leak. The `[:_]` after `ev` is the guard: it requires `ev:`/`ev_` (optionally
133	# `#`-prefixed), so numeric `[N]` citations and ordinary bracketed words (`[event]`, `[evidence]`) are NOT
134	# matched. The synthesis layer must cite by bibliography `[N]` only; any ev-token there is a leak to scrub.
135	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#?ev[:_][^\]]*\]")
136	# I-bug-108 iter-2 P0 fix: extend regex to catch malformed markers
137	# (negative numbers, leading zeros, whitespace) that pass through the
138	# original positive-integer-only pattern. Downstream PT12 parser may
139	# treat any of these as invalid; scrub them all to be safe.
140	_N_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[(-?\d+)\]")
141	_MALFORMED_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\s*(-?\d+)\s*\]")
142	
143	
144	# I-bug-110: process-lifetime telemetry for synthesis [N] scrub.
145	# When the synthesis LLM hallucinates [N] markers beyond the
146	# bibliography size, _scrub_invalid_n_markers strips them at runtime.
147	# An operator monitoring synthesis quality wants two signals:
148	#   - synthesis_n_scrub_count: total markers scrubbed across this
149	#     process's lifetime (cumulative).
150	#   - synthesis_n_scrub_runs: number of synthesis calls that needed
151	#     ANY scrub (i.e., at least one invalid marker present).
152	# These mirror the entailment-judge telemetry pattern at
153	# polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge._JUDGE_TELEMETRY.
154	_SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
155	    "synthesis_n_scrub_count": 0,
156	    "synthesis_n_scrub_runs": 0,
157	    # I-meta-002-q1d (#953 q1d-c, clinical-safety): redactions applied to the analyst evidence pool
158	    # by sanitize_evidence_text (§9.1.7 injection/delimiter defense) and qualitative-negation SAFETY
159	    # sentences DROPPED from the unverified synthesis output (the lethal fabrication class).
160	    "synthesis_evidence_redaction_count": 0,
161	    "synthesis_negation_dropped_count": 0,
162	    # I-deepfix-001 B13 (#1357): the deviation-check LABEL counts — synthesis sentences whose cited
163	    # span does NOT support them (labeled low confidence) and sentences whose [N] resolved NO span
164	    # (labeled no-source). KEEP-and-LABEL, never deleted — the analyst layer brought under the
165	    # faithfulness engine via the "verify AFTER compose = label, never hold" pattern.
166	    "synthesis_deviation_labeled_count": 0,
167	    "synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count": 0,
168	}
169	
170	
171	# I-meta-002-q1d (#953): qualitative-negation SAFETY screen for the UNVERIFIED analyst layer. A sentence
172	# carrying BOTH a negation cue AND a safety/clinical-consequence term is DROPPED fail-closed — the
173	# unverified layer must NOT assert the negation class already caught fabricating on a real smoke
174	# ("did not lead to discontinuation"). Pure regex, no network/LLM. Over-dropping is the SAFE direction
175	# (the span-verified core retains any real finding); false positives only remove an unverified sentence.
176	# Broad negation cues — bare `no`/`not`/`n't` subsume "did not"/"does not"/"not contraindicated"/etc.
177	# (fail-closed: over-matching only removes UNVERIFIED sentences). Codex diff-gate iter-1.
178	_NEGATION_CUE_RE = re.compile(
179	    r"(\bno\b|\bnot\b|\bnever\b|\bwithout\b|\babsent\b|\bnone\b|\bneither\b|\bnor\b"
180	    r"|n['’]t\b|\black(?:s|ed|ing)?\b|\bfree of\b|\bfail(?:s|ed)? to\b)",
181	    re.IGNORECASE,
182	)
183	# Safety / clinical-consequence terms WITH suffix handling (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: truncated stems
184	# wrapped by `\b` did NOT match "hospitalization"/"contraindicated"/"toxicity"/"pregnancy"/"teratogenic"
185	# — `\w*` restores the inflected/plural forms).
186	_SAFETY_TERM_RE = re.compile(
187	    r"(\bdiscontinu\w*|\badverse\b|\bcontraindicat\w*|\binteraction\w*|\bmortality\b|"
188	    r"\bdeaths?\b|\bfatal\w*|\bserious\b|\bhospitali\w*|\bwithdraw\w*|\btoxicit\w*|"
189	    r"\bside\s+effects?\b|\bharm\w*|\bwarning\w*|\bblack[-\s]?box\w*|\bpregnan\w*|"
190	    r"\bteratogen\w*|\brenal\b|\bhepatic\b)",
191	    re.IGNORECASE,
192	)
193	# Conservative sentence splitter: terminator + whitespace + a likely new-sentence/list start. A decimal
194	# ("0.3") has no whitespace after the dot so it never splits; common abbreviations are masked first.
195	_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z(\[\d])")
196	_SYNTH_ABBREVIATIONS = ("e.g.", "i.e.", "etc.", "et al.", "vs.", "fig.", "no.", "approx.", "ca.", "dr.")
197	_SYNTH_ABBREV_RE = re.compile("|".join(re.escape(a) for a in _SYNTH_ABBREVIATIONS), re.IGNORECASE)
198	_SYNTH_PERIOD_MASK = "\x00"
199	
200	
201	def _split_sentences(line: str) -> list[str]:
202	    """Split one prose line into sentences, protecting abbreviation periods (no network)."""
203	    masked = _SYNTH_ABBREV_RE.sub(lambda m: m.group(0).replace(".", _SYNTH_PERIOD_MASK), line)
204	    parts = _SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE.split(masked)
205	    return [p.replace(_SYNTH_PERIOD_MASK, ".") for p in parts]
206	
207	
208	def _screen_qualitative_negations(text: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
209	    """Drop UNVERIFIED qualitative-negation SAFETY sentences from the analyst synthesis (fail-closed).
210	
211	    Pure / no-network. Operates per LINE so markdown structure (### subheadings, blank lines, paragraph
212	    breaks) is preserved; only prose sentences matching BOTH a negation cue AND a safety term are removed.
213	    Returns (cleaned_text, dropped_count). Returns the input unchanged when nothing is dropped.
214	    """
215	    if not text or not text.strip():
216	        return text, 0
217	    out_lines: list[str] = []
218	    dropped = 0
219	    for line in text.split("\n"):
220	        stripped = line.lstrip()
221	        if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#"):
222	            out_lines.append(line)  # preserve blank lines + headings verbatim
223	            continue
224	        kept: list[str] = []
225	        for sentence in _split_sentences(line):
226	            if _NEGATION_CUE_RE.search(sentence) and _SAFETY_TERM_RE.search(sentence):
227	                dropped += 1
228	                continue
229	            kept.append(sentence)
230	        out_lines.append(" ".join(s.strip() for s in kept if s.strip()))
231	    if dropped == 0:
232	        return text, 0
233	    cleaned = "\n".join(out_lines)
234	    return cleaned, dropped
235

237	# I-bug-111: WARN-level alert threshold per single scrub call. When
238	# synthesis hallucinates >5 [N] markers in ONE call, the bibliography
239	# rendering / synthesis prompt is likely degenerating (typical clean
240	# runs scrub 0; a healthy run with one stray hallucination scrubs 1-2).
241	# Operators see a distinct "synthesis_n_scrub_alert" log + the manifest
242	# bool flag below.
243	SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_THRESHOLD = 5
244	
245	_SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_FIRED = False
246	
247

249	    """Return True if any scrub call in this process exceeded the
250	    alert threshold (`SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_THRESHOLD`).
251	
252	    Operator-facing: aggregators (sweep manifest writers) read this
253	    once at end-of-run and surface the bool as
254	    `manifest.synthesis_n_scrub_alert`. The flag is sticky across
255	    calls (true if any single call tripped it) — a transient spike
256	    in any one call is still worth surfacing.
257	    """
258	    return _SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_FIRED

262	    """Reset the sticky alert flag in-place. Tests + sweep
263	    orchestrators call this at start-of-run.
264	    """
265	    global _SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_FIRED
266	    _SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_FIRED = False

270	    """Snapshot of process-lifetime synthesis-scrub counters.
271	
272	    Operator-facing: the existence of any scrub on a production run
273	    is a signal the synthesis prompt or bibliography rendering may
274	    be drifting. Aggregated trends should be tracked.

279	def reset_synthesis_telemetry() -> None:
280	    """Zero all synthesis-telemetry counters in-place.
281	
282	    Public so callers (sweep orchestrators, tests) can deliberately
283	    bound the counter window to a single run.
284	    """
285	    for key in _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY:
286	        _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY[key] = 0

288	
289	def _scrub_invalid_n_markers(text: str, biblio_size: int) -> tuple[str, int]:
290	    """Remove [N] bibliography markers where N is invalid.
291	
292	    Invalid means:
293	      - N > biblio_size (out-of-range hallucination)
294	      - N < 1 (zero, negative — bibliography indices start at 1)
295	      - whitespace-padded forms like [ 5 ] (parser-fragile)
296	
297	    I-bug-108 P0 fix: the synthesis LLM occasionally hallucinates [N]
298	    indices beyond the bibliography. The downstream evaluator rule
299	    PT12 (rule_pt12_invalid_citation_marker) aborts the report when
300	    max_marker > evidence_pool size. Scrub them runtime so production
301	    runs don't abort. Returns (cleaned_text, num_scrubbed).
302	
303	    Iter-2 P0 hardening per Codex review: also catch [-N], [ N ],
304	    [01], etc. — anything that looks like a citation marker but isn't
305	    a valid 1..biblio_size index.
306	    """
307	    scrubbed = 0
308	
309	    def _replace(match: re.Match) -> str:
310	        nonlocal scrubbed
311	        try:
312	            n = int(match.group(1))
313	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
314	            scrubbed += 1
315	            return ""
316	        if n < 1 or n > biblio_size:
317	            scrubbed += 1
318	            return ""  # drop the invalid marker entirely
319	        # Also drop if the original raw form had padding/leading zero
320	        # (bibliography emits "[1]" not "[ 1 ]" or "[01]")
321	        raw = match.group(0)
322	        canonical = f"[{n}]"
323	        if raw != canonical:
324	            scrubbed += 1
325	            return ""
326	        return raw
327	
328	    cleaned = _MALFORMED_MARKER_RE.sub(_replace, text)
329	    if scrubbed > 0:
330	        logger.warning(
331	            "[analyst_synthesis] scrubbed %d invalid [N] marker(s) "
332	            "(out-of-range OR malformed; biblio_size=%d) — "
333	            "synthesis LLM hallucinated indices",
334	            scrubbed, biblio_size,
335	        )
336	        # I-bug-110: increment process-lifetime telemetry counters.
337	        # synthesis_n_scrub_count is total marker count; synthesis_n_scrub_runs
338	        # is the number of synthesis calls that needed any scrub.
339	        _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY["synthesis_n_scrub_count"] += scrubbed
340	        _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY["synthesis_n_scrub_runs"] += 1
341	        # I-bug-111: trip the sticky alert flag on any high-scrub call.
342	        # Threshold = 5 markers in ONE call — typical healthy synthesis
343	        # scrubs 0; the I-bug-108 incident scrubbed 6 in one call,
344	        # validating this threshold empirically.
345	        if scrubbed > SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_THRESHOLD:
346	            global _SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_FIRED
347	            _SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_FIRED = True
348	            logger.warning(
349	                "[analyst_synthesis] synthesis_n_scrub_alert: %d markers "
350	                "scrubbed in single call (threshold=%d). Synthesis "
351	                "prompt or bibliography rendering may be degenerating.",
352	                scrubbed, SYNTHESIS_SCRUB_ALERT_THRESHOLD,
353	            )
354	    return cleaned, scrubbed
355	
356	
357	def _scrub_ev_tokens(text: str) -> str:
358	    """Remove any ev-token ([#ev:...] OR bare [ev_NNN]) from synthesis output.
359	
360	    Codex iter-1 P0: must be a runtime guardrail, not just a test.
361	    I-meta-002-q1d (#946): the original pattern matched only the prefixed
362	    [#ev:...] token; a bare [ev_012] leaked into a published report.md.
363	    Synthesis prose MUST NOT carry any ev-token — it cites by bibliography
364	    [N] markers only. If the LLM emits one anyway, scrub it and log a warning.
365	    """
366	    cleaned, n = _EV_TOKEN_RE.subn("", text)
367	    if n > 0:
368	        logger.warning(
369	            "[analyst_synthesis] scrubbed %d ev-token(s) ([#ev:...] or bare "
370	            "[ev_NNN]) from synthesis output — synthesis MUST cite by [N] only",
371	            n,
372	        )
373	    return cleaned

376	def _format_bibliography_for_prompt(bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
377	    """Render the global bibliography as numbered [N] entries for the prompt.
378	
379	    Mirrors the bibliography format the Verified Findings section already
380	    uses, so the synthesis cites the same [N] indices the audit core does.
381	    """
382	    lines = []
383	    for i, entry in enumerate(bibliography, start=1):
384	        title = entry.get("title") or "Untitled"
385	        url = entry.get("url") or ""
386	        tier = entry.get("tier") or ""
387	        tier_str = f" (tier {tier})" if tier else ""
388	        lines.append(f"[{i}] {title} — {url}{tier_str}")
389	    return "\n".join(lines)
390	
391	
392	def _format_evidence_pool_for_prompt(
393	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
394	    max_rows: int | None = None,
395	) -> str:
396	    """Render the evidence pool as <<<evidence:ev_X>>> blocks the LLM
397	    can read for context. Capped at max_rows to bound the prompt size.
398	    """
399	    # I-deepfix-001 P2 (box2): env-tunable so the synthesis writer can SEE enough evidence spans to cite
400	    # valid [N] indices instead of hallucinating out-of-range ones. LAW VI; default 30 = byte-identical.

400	    # valid [N] indices instead of hallucinating out-of-range ones. LAW VI; default 30 = byte-identical.
401	    if max_rows is None:
402	        try:
403	            max_rows = int(os.getenv("PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_EVIDENCE_MAX_ROWS", "30") or "30")
404	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
405	            max_rows = 30
406	    # I-meta-002-q1d (#953 q1d-c): sanitize evidence text AND the id (§9.1.7 delimiter/injection defense)
407	    # BEFORE building the <<<evidence>>> blocks — the analyst layer previously passed RAW evidence,
408	    # letting content forge a closing/opening delimiter (the same defense the verified path already has).
409	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import sanitize_evidence_text
410	
411	    blocks = []
412	    total_redacted = 0
413	    for row in evidence_rows[:max_rows]:
414	        ev_id_raw = str(row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("id") or "ev_?")
415	        ev_id, ev_id_red = sanitize_evidence_text(ev_id_raw)
416	        quote_raw = (
417	            row.get("direct_quote")
418	            or row.get("statement")
419	            or ""
420	        )[:1200]
421	        quote, quote_red = sanitize_evidence_text(quote_raw)
422	        total_redacted += ev_id_red + quote_red
423	        # Use the SAME closing delimiter as the verified wrap (`<<<end_evidence>>>`) so a forged copy
424	        # inside evidence is in sanitize_evidence_text's redaction set (bare `<<<end>>>` was NOT).
425	        blocks.append(f"<<<evidence:{ev_id}>>>\n{quote}\n<<<end_evidence>>>")
426	    if total_redacted:
427	        _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY["synthesis_evidence_redaction_count"] += total_redacted
428	        logger.info(
429	            "[analyst_synthesis] sanitized analyst evidence pool: %d redactions", total_redacted
430	        )
431	    return "\n\n".join(blocks)
432	
433	
434	def _format_prior_verified_context(prior_verified_context: list[dict[str, Any]] | None) -> str:
435	    """Render the campaign KG-reuse advisory block (I-meta-002-q1d #948). Each item is a prior-VERIFIED
436	    claim that the MECHANICAL match-gate already confirmed is INDEPENDENTLY supported by THIS question's
437	    evidence (anchored to a CURRENT evidence id). Advisory only — the analyst still cites by [N] from the
438	    current bibliography; no prior evidence ids appear. Empty input → empty block (prompt byte-identical)."""
439	    items = [c for c in (prior_verified_context or []) if c.get("claim_text")]
440	    if not items:
441	        return ""
442	    lines = [
443	        "=== CROSS-QUESTION CONSISTENCY (advisory) ===\n",
444	        "These facts were VERIFIED on prior campaign questions AND are independently supported by THIS",
445	        "question's evidence pool. Prioritise them where relevant, but cite ONLY by [N] from the",
446	        "bibliography above (the matching current source); do NOT invent citations or reuse prior ids.\n",
447	    ]
448	    for c in items:
449	        lines.append(f"- {c['claim_text']}  (supported here by evidence {c.get('evidence_id', '')})")
450	    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n\n"

453	def _promote_mode_active() -> bool:
454	    """True iff the D3 fail-closed PROMOTE gate is active. Probes the deviation-check module's own
455	    ``promote_grounded_enabled`` (which requires the deviation check ON AND the promote flag ON); if
456	    that probe itself raises (e.g. the module failed to import — the very fault that trips the caller's
457	    fail-closed path), fall back to the RAW promote flag. Fail-closed is the safe direction whenever the
458	    promote gate was requested, so a probe failure must not silently choose the legacy keep path."""
459	    try:
460	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.analyst_synthesis_deviation_check import (
461	            promote_grounded_enabled as _pge,
462	        )
463	        return bool(_pge())
464	    except Exception:
465	        _off_values = {"", "0", "false", "off", "no", "disabled"}
466	        return (
467	            os.environ.get("PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED", "0").strip().lower()
468	            not in _off_values
469	        )

472	def _apply_synthesis_deviation_screen(
473	    cleaned: str,
474	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
475	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
476	    deviation_judge_fn: "Callable[[str, str], bool] | None",
477	) -> str:
478	    """Run the B13/D3 deviation screen over the composed synthesis prose and return the screened text.
479	
480	    A synthesis sentence whose cited ``[N]`` span does NOT support it is labeled (legacy advisory mode)
481	    or DROPPED (D3 promote mode). ``strict_verify`` is NOT touched — this is an additive gate on top of
482	    the frozen engine. Default-ON (``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK``) + shares the coarse
483	    ``PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS`` kill-switch.
484	
485	    I-deepfix-001 D3 P0 (#1344) FAIL-CLOSED: if the screen itself RAISES (wiring / import / checker
486	    fault) the synthesis was NEVER gated. Under promote mode returning the ungated ``cleaned`` would
487	    render UNGATED synthesis into the scored body — the fail-OPEN hole. So under promote mode a screen
488	    exception DROPS the whole synthesis block (returns ""); in legacy advisory mode the text ships
489	    UNLABELED (as before) with a fail-loud DISTINCT telemetry counter."""
490	    try:
491	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.analyst_synthesis_deviation_check import (
492	            screen_synthesis_against_baskets,
493	        )
494	        cleaned, _dev_tel = screen_synthesis_against_baskets(
495	            cleaned, bibliography, evidence_rows, judge_fn=deviation_judge_fn,
496	        )
497	        for _k, _v in _dev_tel.items():
498	            if _v:
499	                _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY[_k] = _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY.get(_k, 0) + _v
500	        if _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_dropped_count"):
501	            # I-deepfix-001 D3 (#1344): fail-closed mode DROPPED ungrounded/no-source synthesis
502	            # sentences from the scored body (the module already fail-loud-logs the exact sentences).
503	            logger.warning(
504	                "[analyst_synthesis] D3 fail-closed: %d ungrounded/no-source synthesis sentence(s) "
505	                "DROPPED from the scored body (never rendered as a body claim); %d promoted grounded",
506	                _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_dropped_count", 0),
507	                _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_promoted_count", 0),
508	            )
509	        if _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_labeled_count") or _dev_tel.get(
510	            "synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count"
511	        ):
512	            logger.info(
513	                "[analyst_synthesis] B13 deviation check labeled %d unsupported + %d unresolved "
514	                "synthesis sentence(s) (advisory KEEP-and-LABEL)",
515	                _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_labeled_count", 0),
516	                _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count", 0),
517	            )
518	        _rendered_any = _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count") or _dev_tel.get(
519	            "synthesis_deviation_promoted_count"
520	        )
521	        if _rendered_any and cleaned.strip() and ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE[:40] not in cleaned:
522	            # wave-2 (#1370) DEPTH: prepend ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE whenever the analyst block renders
523	            # ANY content (Fable depth-gate b1: the PROMOTED analyst sentences also cleared only the
524	            # deterministic floor, not strict_verify entailment — the WHOLE analyst section is interpretive
525	            # and must always carry its section-level "interpretive commentary, not individually span-verified"
526	            # label; each disclosed sentence ALSO carries a per-sentence "shown unverified" marker). Guarded
527	            # against double-prepend.
528	            logger.info(
529	                "[analyst_synthesis] wave-2 DEPTH: analyst block rendered (%d promoted + %d disclosed "
530	                "interpretation sentence(s)); section disclosure prepended",
531	                _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_promoted_count", 0),
532	                _dev_tel.get("synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count", 0),
533	            )
534	            cleaned = f"{ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE}\n\n{cleaned}"
535	        return cleaned
536	    except Exception as _dev_exc:  # the deviation check is ADDITIVE — never abort the report on it
537	        # A checker/wiring exception (distinct from an in-checker judge fault, which is already handled
538	        # LOW/DROP per-sentence) means the analyst layer was NOT screened. Fail-LOUD with a DISTINCT
539	        # telemetry counter so a wiring break is visible in the manifest, never mistaken for a clean run.
540	        _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY["synthesis_deviation_check_error_count"] = (
541	            _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY.get("synthesis_deviation_check_error_count", 0) + 1
542	        )
543	        if _promote_mode_active():
544	            # I-deepfix-001 D3 P0: under PROMOTE mode the synthesis is only admissible if it PASSED the
545	            # gate. It was never gated -> DROP the whole block (never render ungated synthesis).
546	            _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY["synthesis_deviation_promote_failclosed_drop_count"] = (
547	                _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY.get("synthesis_deviation_promote_failclosed_drop_count", 0) + 1
548	            )
549	            logger.error(
550	                "[analyst_synthesis] D3 PROMOTE fail-CLOSED: the deviation screen raised (%s) so the "
551	                "analyst synthesis was NEVER gated — DROPPING the whole synthesis block from the scored "
552	                "body (never render ungated synthesis under promote mode)",
553	                _dev_exc,
554	                exc_info=True,
555	            )
556	            return ""
557	        logger.error(
558	            "[analyst_synthesis] B13 deviation check FAILED (wiring/checker error — analyst layer "
559	            "ships UNLABELED in legacy advisory mode, faithfulness labels lost for this section; "
560	            "fail-loud telemetry emitted): %s",
561	            _dev_exc,
562	            exc_info=True,
563	        )
564	        return cleaned
565	
566	
567	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
568	# I-deepfix-006 C5 — clean analyst-synthesis render (PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN, default ON)
569	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
570	# De-clutters the rendered "Analyst Synthesis" block WITHOUT touching the frozen faithfulness engine
571	# or any claim. Two render-only edits:
572	#   (1) DEDUPE the DOUBLED disclosure. ``_apply_synthesis_deviation_screen`` prepends the
573	#       ``ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE`` INTO ``analyst_synthesis_text`` whenever the analyst block
574	#       renders, AND the sole report renderer (run_honest_sweep_r3.py ~L15436) ALSO emits that same
575	#       disclosure as the italic "## Analyst Synthesis" section preamble. Result in report.md: the
576	#       disclosure twice, back-to-back. This pass removes the IN-TEXT copy so the renderer's canonical
577	#       preamble is the single one. The section STAYS labeled interpretive (the renderer always adds
578	#       the preamble — it is the only consumer of ``analyst_synthesis_text`` — so the section is never
579	#       left unlabeled).
580	#   (2) LIFT the per-sentence ``[confidence: <bucket> — …]`` markers (appended by the B13/D3 deviation
581	#       check via ``claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker``) OUT of the prose into ONE compact
582	#       per-section confidence note (bucket counts). The section-level disclosure remains the binding
583	#       honesty signal; no claim is upgraded, none dropped — the labels are aggregated, not discarded.
584	# Faithfulness-neutral + render-only. Default-ON; OFF => byte-identical (returns the text unchanged);
585	# ON with an already-clean block (no in-text disclosure, no markers) is also byte-identical.
586	_RENDER_CLEAN_ENV = "PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN"
587	_RENDER_CLEAN_OFF_VALUES = ("0", "false", "off", "no")
588	# Mirrors analyst_synthesis_deviation_check._CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE (leading whitespace + the bracket).
589	_RENDER_CLEAN_CONF_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"[ \t]*\[confidence:[^\]]*\]")
590	# The bucket label is the first token after "[confidence:" up to the " — " separator; buckets are the
591	# claim_labeler VALID_BUCKETS (high / moderate / low / no-source-found — lowercase, may carry hyphens).
592	_RENDER_CLEAN_CONF_BUCKET_RE = re.compile(r"\[confidence:\s*([A-Za-z-]+)")
593	_RENDER_CLEAN_BUCKET_ORDER = ("high", "moderate", "low", "no-source-found")
594	
595	
596	def _render_clean_enabled() -> bool:
597	    """C5 kill-switch. Default-ON; OFF only for an explicit off-value (byte-identical legacy render)."""
598	    return (
599	        os.environ.get(_RENDER_CLEAN_ENV, "1").strip().lower()
600	        not in _RENDER_CLEAN_OFF_VALUES
601	    )
602	
603	
604	def _render_clean_synthesis(text: str) -> str:
605	    """Return the render-cleaned analyst-synthesis text (C5). See module block above.
606	
607	    Pure, no-network, faithfulness-neutral. Returns the input UNCHANGED when the flag is OFF or when
608	    there is nothing to clean (no in-text disclosure copy AND no inline confidence markers)."""
609	    if not text or not text.strip() or not _render_clean_enabled():
610	        return text
611	    had_disclosure = ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE in text
612	    buckets = [b.lower() for b in _RENDER_CLEAN_CONF_BUCKET_RE.findall(text)]
613	    if not had_disclosure and not buckets:
614	        return text  # already clean -> byte-identical
615	    cleaned = text
616	    if had_disclosure:
617	        # Remove EVERY in-text copy (guarded ≤1 upstream, but be defensive) — the renderer re-adds one.
618	        cleaned = cleaned.replace(ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE, "")
619	    if buckets:
620	        cleaned = _RENDER_CLEAN_CONF_MARKER_RE.sub("", cleaned)
621	    # Tidy whitespace the strips left behind (trailing spaces, >2 consecutive blank lines, leading blanks).
622	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]+\n", "\n", cleaned)
623	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", cleaned).lstrip("\n")
624	    if buckets:
625	        counts = Counter(buckets)
626	        ordered = [f"{counts[b]} {b}" for b in _RENDER_CLEAN_BUCKET_ORDER if counts.get(b)]
627	        for b in counts:  # any non-standard label -> keep it, appended after the known order
628	            if b not in _RENDER_CLEAN_BUCKET_ORDER:
629	                ordered.append(f"{counts[b]} {b}")
630	        note = (
631	            "_Confidence labels for this interpretive section: "
632	            + ", ".join(ordered)
633	            + ". Each was a per-sentence groundedness check, summarized here; the whole section is "
634	            "interpretive and not individually span-verified._"
635	        )
636	        cleaned = f"{note}\n\n{cleaned}" if cleaned.strip() else note
637	    return cleaned
638	
639	
640	async def generate_analyst_synthesis(
641	    *,
642	    verified_prose: str,
643	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
644	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
645	    research_question: str,
646	    prior_verified_context: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
647	    model: str = "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
648	    # D-1 / I-ready-017 (#1182): was a hardcoded 4000; the reasoning-first writer
649	    # needs room to finish planning before the synthesis prose. Named, env-overridable
650	    # default (openrouter_client clamps reasoning-first to PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP
651	    # =16384 on the default provider — see the module-level PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS note).
652	    max_tokens: int = PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS,
653	    temperature: float = 0.3,
654	    # I-deepfix-001 B13 (#1357): optional injectable groundedness judge ((claim, span) -> bool, True
655	    # == supported) for the post-compose deviation check. None (default) => the check lazily builds the
656	    # certified-Sentinel judge; the run path may inject its already-built sentinel transport judge.
657	    deviation_judge_fn: "Callable[[str, str], bool] | None" = None,
658	) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
659	    """Generate the Analyst Synthesis section.
660	
661	    Per I-cd-009 (GH#624) Carney demo lock: DeepSeek V4 Pro 1.6T (49B
662	    active MoE) is the writer (consistent with verified prose voice;
663	    Gemma 4 31B-it stays in the judge/evaluator role).
664	
665	    Returns (text, input_tokens, output_tokens). Returns ("", 0, 0)
666	    on failure — caller MUST treat empty text as "omit synthesis
667	    section entirely" (no empty disclosure block per Codex iter-1).
668	    """
669	    # I-meta-002-q1d (#953 q1d-c): operator kill-switch. Checked BEFORE prompt construction + the model
670	    # call so disabling the unverified analyst layer costs nothing (Codex brief-gate P2). When off, the
671	    # report ships the span-verified core ONLY (caller omits an empty synthesis section).
672	    if os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS", "1").strip() not in ("1", "true", "True"):
673	        logger.info(
674	            "[analyst_synthesis] PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS=0 — unverified layer omitted "
675	            "(verified core only)"
676	        )
677	        return "", 0, 0
678	
679	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) iter2 (Codex P0): EMITTER-level fail-closed. The Gate-B preflight refuses an
680	    # ungated launch, but the RESUME-render / direct-sweep path SKIPS that preflight — so the same guard
681	    # must bind HERE at the emitter. If the layer is ON while the D3 PROMOTE (drop-if-ungrounded) gate is
682	    # NOT active, shipping would emit legacy KEEP-and-LABEL (un-dropped) synthesis — the exact ungated
683	    # posture that was banned. Refuse it: ship the span-verified core ONLY. Default-ON kill-switch so only
684	    # an explicit operator opt-out disables the guard. The frozen faithfulness engine is untouched.
685	    if os.environ.get(
686	        "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_EMITTER_FAILCLOSED", "1"
687	    ).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "off", "no") and not _promote_mode_active():
688	        logger.warning(
689	            "[analyst_synthesis] EMITTER fail-CLOSED: layer ON but D3 PROMOTE gate is OFF — refusing "
690	            "UNGATED synthesis (verified core only). Set PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED=1 to ship it."
691	        )
692	        return "", 0, 0
693	
694	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
695	        OpenRouterClient,
696	        set_reasoning_call_context,
697	    )
698	
699	    biblio_block = _format_bibliography_for_prompt(bibliography)
700	    evidence_block = _format_evidence_pool_for_prompt(evidence_rows)
701	
702	    prompt = (
703	        f"Research question: {research_question}\n\n"
704	        f"=== VERIFIED FINDINGS (above the synthesis section) ===\n\n"
705	        f"{verified_prose}\n\n"
706	        f"=== BIBLIOGRAPHY (cite by [N] only) ===\n\n"
707	        f"{biblio_block}\n\n"
708	        f"=== EVIDENCE POOL (for synthesis context) ===\n\n"
709	        f"{evidence_block}\n\n"
710	        f"{_format_prior_verified_context(prior_verified_context)}"
711	        f"=== TASK ===\n\n"
712	        f"Write the Analyst Synthesis section now, following the rules. "
713	        f"Hedge interpretive claims; cite by [N] only; no [#ev:...] tokens; "
714	        f"4-6 sub-sections with ### subheadings; 1500-3000 words total."
715	    )
716	
717	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
718	    try:
719	        # I-gen-004 (#496): tag the analyst-synthesis call for the trace sink.
720	        set_reasoning_call_context(
721	            section="Analyst Synthesis", call_type="analyst_synthesis",
722	        )
723	        # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: role-tagging for the Path-B gate is applied at the
724	        # runner chokepoint (run_honest_sweep_r3.py wraps generate_multi_section_report
725	        # with llm_role("generator")) — this nested call inherits the contextvar.
726	        response = await client.generate(
727	            prompt=prompt,
728	            system=ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
729	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
730	            temperature=temperature,
731	            # I-wire-009 (#1323): BOUND the reasoning pool on the analyst-synthesis writer. The
732	            # generator (GLM-5.2 in-campaign) is in openrouter_client._ALWAYS_REASON_MODELS, whose
733	            # branch-1 path runs reasoning at effort=high with NO cap when no reasoning_max_tokens
734	            # is passed — it can consume the whole max_tokens (PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS=64000) ceiling
735	            # on reasoning and return content="". A generous reasoning slice leaves the bulk of the
736	            # ceiling for the 1500-3000-word synthesis. LAW VI env-tunable; §9.1.8 token-budget fix only,
737	            # faithfulness-neutral (this is the UNVERIFIED analyst layer; strict_verify governs the
738	            # verified core).
739	            # I-deepfix-001 M6 (Layer 2): the reasoning-first writer was returning empty in drb_72 (the
740	            # #1323/#1335 dark-synthesis trap — reasoning burned the slice, content=""). Per §9.1
741	            # token-MAX ("reasoning effort + max_tokens ALWAYS go MAX; a starved budget truncates
742	            # reasoning -> empty content"), raise the DEFAULT slice 16384 -> 32768 (a CAP, not a target,
743	            # billed by actual usage -> generous cap is free insurance). Still LAW VI env-overridable.
744	            reasoning_max_tokens=int(
745	                os.getenv("PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "32768")
746	            ),
747	        )
748	        text = (response.content or "").strip()
749	        in_tok = response.input_tokens
750	        out_tok = response.output_tokens
751	    except Exception as exc:
752	        logger.warning("[analyst_synthesis] generation failed: %s", exc)
753	        return "", 0, 0
754	    finally:
755	        try:
756	            await client.close()
757	        except Exception:
758	            pass
759	
760	    if not text:
761	        logger.warning("[analyst_synthesis] empty response — section omitted")
762	        return "", in_tok, out_tok
763	
764	    # Codex iter-1 P0 guardrail: scrub [#ev:...] tokens from synthesis.
765	    cleaned = _scrub_ev_tokens(text)
766	    # I-bug-108 P0: scrub [N] markers exceeding bibliography size
767	    # to prevent rule_pt12_invalid_citation_marker from aborting the
768	    # report. Synthesis LLM occasionally hallucinates indices; scrub
769	    # them runtime so production runs don't abort.
770	    cleaned, _ = _scrub_invalid_n_markers(cleaned, biblio_size=len(bibliography))
771	    # I-meta-002-q1d (#953 q1d-c, CLINICAL-SAFETY): fail-closed drop of unverified qualitative-negation
772	    # SAFETY sentences from the analyst layer (the lethal fabrication class) — runs AFTER the syntactic
773	    # scrubs, BEFORE the report appends this prose. The span-verified core above is untouched.
774	    cleaned, n_neg_dropped = _screen_qualitative_negations(cleaned)
775	    if n_neg_dropped:
776	        _SYNTHESIS_TELEMETRY["synthesis_negation_dropped_count"] += n_neg_dropped
777	        logger.warning(
778	            "[analyst_synthesis] synthesis_negation_dropped: %d unverified qualitative-negation "
779	            "safety sentence(s) removed from the analyst layer (fail-closed)", n_neg_dropped
780	        )
781	    # I-deepfix-001 B13 (#1357) + D3 (#1344): bring the analyst layer UNDER the faithfulness engine via
782	    # the deviation check. Extracted into a synchronous, testable helper (the fail-closed-on-exception
783	    # path is behavioral, not an untested branch). Runs AFTER the regex scrubs + negation screen,
784	    # BEFORE return.
785	    cleaned = _apply_synthesis_deviation_screen(
786	        cleaned, bibliography, evidence_rows, deviation_judge_fn,
787	    )
788	    # I-deepfix-006 C5 (PG_SYNTH_RENDER_CLEAN, default ON): dedupe the doubled section disclosure +
789	    # lift the per-sentence [confidence:…] markers into a compact per-section note. Render-only, runs
790	    # LAST (after the deviation screen has appended its confidence markers). OFF => byte-identical.
791	    cleaned = _render_clean_synthesis(cleaned)
792	    return cleaned, in_tok, out_tok
793

content.text: Clean, no unprofessional names. Now atom_refusal_validator.py, attribution_origin_guard.py, block_page_chrome_scrub.py, boundary_conditions.py, chrome_furniture_screen.py, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py.

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 B13 (#1357) — bring the Analyst-Synthesis layer UNDER the faithfulness engine.
2	
3	THE PROBLEM (forensic): ``analyst_synthesis.generate_analyst_synthesis`` is architecturally OUTSIDE
4	the faithfulness engine — its system prompt FORBIDS ``[#ev:]`` span tokens and cites by ``[N]``
5	bibliography markers only, so the per-sentence ``strict_verify`` entailment gate NEVER sees it. It is
6	the longest, most confident block in the report, and its grounded over-assertions (hedges upgraded to
7	assertions, interpolated specifics, model-asserted author names absent from the pool) ship UNGATED.
8	
9	THE FIX (the operator-locked "verify AFTER compose = LABEL, never DELETE" pattern, §-1.3 BASKET
10	FAITHFULNESS / `feedback_always_release_verifier_labels_never_holds_2026_06_14`): this module is a
11	thin, additive DEVIATION CHECK over the already-composed synthesis prose. It:
12	
13	  1. splits the synthesis into sentences (reusing ``provenance_generator.split_into_sentences``),
14	  2. for each sentence, parses its ``[N]`` markers (``report_claim_extractor._NUMBERED_RE``) and
15	     resolves ``N -> bibliography[N-1].evidence_id -> the cited evidence row's span``,
16	  3. asks a groundedness judge (the certified MiniMax-M2 Sentinel decomposition by default — a SECOND
17	     family vs the GLM/DeepSeek writer, so two-family holds) whether the cited span SUPPORTS the
18	     sentence,
19	  4. for an UNSUPPORTED sentence appends an inline confidence marker
20	     (``claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(BUCKET_LOW)``), and for a sentence whose ``[N]`` resolve
21	     NO span (``BUCKET_NO_SOURCE``) — KEEP the sentence, NEVER delete it.
22	
23	FAIL-CLOSED to ``BUCKET_LOW``: a judge error / transport flap / timeout LABELS the sentence low (over-
24	labeling is the SAFE direction for a previously-ungated layer), never a silent high, never a hold.
25	
26	FAITHFULNESS ENGINE UNTOUCHED: ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are
27	NOT modified. This is an additive, LABEL-only deviation leg over the previously-ungated synthesis
28	prose. The judge here is ADVISORY — it never drops a sentence, never changes ``is_verified``.
29	
30	LAW VI: the leg is gated default-ON by ``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK`` and shares the coarse
31	``PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS`` kill-switch (when the whole analyst layer is off, this never runs).
32	
33	TESTABILITY / NO HANG (I-arch-006/007 lesson): the per-sentence groundedness call is INJECTABLE
34	(``judge_fn``) so a test passes a deterministic fake; the real path resolves a bounded, deadline-wrapped
35	Sentinel judge lazily. The calls run BOUNDED-PARALLEL (never a serial unbounded loop on a trickle
36	socket — the documented hang class).
37	"""
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import logging
41	import os
42	import re
43	import time
44	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, TimeoutError as _FutureTimeout
45	from typing import Any, Callable
46	
47	from src.polaris_graph.generator import claim_labeler
48	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
49	    split_into_sentences,
50	    verify_sentence_provenance,
51	)
52	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.report_claim_extractor import _NUMBERED_RE
53	
54	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
55	
56	# LAW VI flags. The leg is default-ON; the coarse analyst-layer kill-switch ALSO gates it (when the
57	# whole analyst layer is off, this module never runs because the caller returns before invoking it).
58	_ENV_ENABLED = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_CHECK"
59	_ENV_ANALYST_KILL = "PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS"
60	# Bounded-parallel fan-out for the per-sentence groundedness calls (NEVER a serial unbounded loop on a
61	# trickle socket — the I-arch-006/007 hang class). LAW VI; default 8.
62	_ENV_MAX_INFLIGHT = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_MAX_INFLIGHT"
63	_DEFAULT_MAX_INFLIGHT = 8
64	# Per-call deadline (seconds). A judge socket flap fail-CLOSES to BUCKET_LOW (over-label = safe) rather
65	# than hanging the report. LAW VI; default 60s.
66	_ENV_DEADLINE_S = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DEVIATION_DEADLINE_S"
67	_DEFAULT_DEADLINE_S = 60.0
68	
69	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no", "disabled"})
70	
71	# I-deepfix-001 D3 (#1344) — FAIL-CLOSED PROMOTE gate. Default-OFF (LAW VI). This is the gate that lets
72	# the analyst-synthesis layer turn ON safely (the layer is REQUIRED_OFF today precisely because it was
73	# not span-verified). When this flag is ON:
74	#   * a synthesis sentence renders in the SCORED BODY only if it passes ALL THREE legs of the frozen
75	#     engine — the same gate every body claim clears (Codex iter-4 P0):
76	#       (1) the REAL verify_sentence_provenance re-pass (_frozen_engine_verifies_sentence): a genuine
77	#           [#ev:id:start-end] provenance token is rebuilt per cited source and the UNCHANGED
78	#           strict_verify + NLI-entailment engine must return is_verified — so a [N]-cited synthesis
79	#           sentence now carries and clears real [#ev] provenance exactly like a verified body claim;
80	#       (2) the deterministic span-grounding pre-check (strict_verify §9.1.3 principle — every decimal
81	#           in the sentence appears in the cited span AND >= _MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP shared content words); and
82	#       (3) the D8/NLI groundedness judge (judge_fn / the certified Sentinel).
83	#     A passing sentence is KEEP-and-PROMOTE'd (BUCKET_MODERATE "verified against the cited source");
84	#   * a sentence that FAILS either leg — ungrounded, no-source, or judge-fault — is DROPPED from the
85	#     body (fail-closed) and surfaced in the returned drop telemetry + a fail-LOUD log line, NEVER
86	#     rendered as a hedged body claim. Over-drop is the safe direction; no source is deleted (the drop
87	#     removes a model-generated sentence, not an evidence row), strict_verify / NLI / 4-role are NOT
88	#     touched — this gate is ADDED on top of the frozen engine.
89	# OFF (default) => the legacy advisory KEEP-and-LABEL leg runs, byte-identical: grounded sentences pass
90	# bare, ungrounded/no-source sentences are LABELED (never dropped).
91	_ENV_PROMOTE_GROUNDED = "PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED"
92	
93	# Deterministic span-grounding leg (the strict_verify §9.1.3 principle, reused faithfully at the
94	# synthesis seam so the D3 gate is the FROZEN ENGINE, not a lone judge). PURE, no I/O.
95	_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP = 2
96	_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^\]]*\]")          # [N] citation + [confidence:...] markers
97	# I-deepfix-001 D3 P1 (#1344): a pre-labeled sentence (already carrying a [confidence:...] marker —
98	# model-injected, or a defensive double-call) must be STRIPPED of that marker so the promote gate
99	# re-screens the bare prose through BOTH frozen-engine legs. Without this a hedged, ungrounded claim
100	# could carry its own [confidence: low] tag past the gate and survive in the scored body.
101	_CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\s*\[confidence:[^\]]*\]")
102	_DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?")
103	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
104	# A tiny stoplist so the >=2 content-word overlap is genuinely topical (not "the"/"is" agreement).
105	_GROUND_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
106	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "of", "in", "on", "for", "to", "with", "by", "at", "from",
107	    "as", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being", "this", "that", "these", "those",
108	    "it", "its", "their", "his", "her", "our", "your", "we", "they", "he", "she", "you",
109	    "not", "no", "but", "if", "then", "than", "so", "such", "into", "over", "under", "about",
110	    "which", "who", "whom", "whose", "what", "when", "where", "while", "also", "may", "can",
111	    "will", "would", "could", "should", "has", "have", "had", "more", "most", "some", "any",
112	})

115	def _strip_markers(text: str) -> str:
116	    """Remove bracketed ``[N]`` / ``[confidence:...]`` markers so number/word extraction reads the
117	    prose, not the citation index (``[1]`` must never be counted as the decimal ``1``)."""
118	    return _MARKER_RE.sub(" ", text or "")
119	
120	
121	def _strip_confidence_markers(text: str) -> str:
122	    """Remove any ``[confidence:...]`` marker(s) from ``text`` (leaving ``[N]`` citations intact) so a
123	    pre-labeled sentence is re-screened on its bare prose in PROMOTE mode. PURE."""
124	    return _CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE.sub("", text or "").strip()
125

127	def _decimals(text: str) -> "set[str]":
128	    """The distinct decimal number tokens in ``text`` (comma thousands-separators normalized out)."""
129	    return {d.replace(",", "") for d in _DECIMAL_RE.findall(text or "")}
130	
131	
132	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> "set[str]":
133	    """Lower-cased content tokens (>=3 chars, not a stopword)."""
134	    return {w for w in _WORD_RE.findall(str(text or "").lower())
135	            if len(w) >= 3 and w not in _GROUND_STOPWORDS}

138	def _span_grounds_sentence(sentence: str, span: str) -> bool:
139	    """Deterministic span-grounding leg of the D3 frozen-engine gate (strict_verify §9.1.3 principle).
140	
141	    True iff the sentence's prose is anchored in the cited span: (a) EVERY decimal in the sentence
142	    appears in the span (a synthesis sentence must not invent or alter a number the cited source did
143	    not state), AND (b) the sentence and the span share >= ``_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP`` content words. A
144	    blank span never grounds. PURE. This is a CONSERVATIVE gate — it can only REFUSE to promote (drop
145	    to the fail-closed audit tail); it never admits a sentence the judge rejected."""
146	    if not span or not span.strip():
147	        return False
148	    core = _strip_markers(sentence)
149	    sent_nums = _decimals(core)
150	    if sent_nums and not sent_nums.issubset(_decimals(span)):
151	        return False
152	    return len(_content_tokens(core) & _content_tokens(span)) >= _MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP
153

158	    promote_on = os.environ.get(_ENV_PROMOTE_GROUNDED, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
159	    return promote_on and deviation_check_enabled()

165	# entails (box2: 79/81 real cited cross-source sentences dropped). When ON, a synthesis sentence is KEPT iff it
166	# (1) resolves >=1 valid cited span AND (2) passes the DETERMINISTIC anti-fabrication floor (_span_grounds_sentence:
167	# every number in the sentence appears in the UNION of cited spans AND >=2 content-word overlap). Default OFF.

181	# source ``statement`` but not the exact quote slice. A number in NO cited source still drops (anti-fabrication

190	# I-deepfix-001 wave-2 (#1370) DEPTH — disclosed analyst layer. The analyst-synthesis composer is DESIGNED
191	# to write hedged interpretive framing UNCITED (its system prompt reserves [N] for concrete factual claims
192	# and instructs qualitative interpretation to be hedged, not cited), and the layer already carries the
193	# ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_DISCLOSURE preamble ("interpretive commentary … not individually span-verified"). The D3
194	# PROMOTE gate was fighting that design — dropping every uncited interpretive sentence (box2: 31 real
195	# analytical-framework sentences lost). This renders an uncited hedged QUALITATIVE interpretation under that
196	# disclosure instead of dropping it (verify-after-compose = LABEL, never DELETE — operator-authorized gated
197	# analyst-synthesis). Default OFF; only active WITH basket mode. A fabrication (uncited + a number / a named
