206	# An author attribution ("Acemoglu and Restrepo", "Brynjolfsson et al") inside an UNCITED interpretive
207	# sentence is disclosable ONLY if EVERY named surname is actually in the evidence pool (a real study being
208	# interpreted). A surname absent from the pool is a fabricated attribution and must DROP.
209	_AUTHOR_ATTR_RE = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z][a-z]{2,}\s+(?:et al|and\s+[A-Z][a-z]{2,})")
210	_SURNAME_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Z][a-z]{2,}")
211	_POOL_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z]{3,}")
212	# Codex + Fable depth-gate P1 (load-bearing §-1.3 fix): a disclosable NON-header sentence must carry a
213	# HEDGE / INTERPRETIVE cue — the markers the composer's own system prompt tells the LLM to use for hedged
214	# interpretation (suggests / implies / may / typically / broadly / consistent with / framework / logic /
215	# depends on / …). A bare UNHEDGED categorical assertion ("Generative AI will replace all lawyers") carries
216	# no hedge => NOT disclosable => drops. Deliberately EXCLUDES bare analytical nouns (trend / pattern /
217	# evidence) that a fabricated declarative could carry. Verified to still admit box2's real analytical
218	# framework tissue.
219	_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE = re.compile(
220	    r"\b(?:suggest\w*|impl(?:y|ies|ied|ication\w*)|may|might|could|consistent with|typical\w*|broad\w*|"
221	    r"appears?|likely|interpret\w*|framework\w*|mechanism\w*|logic\w*|depends?\s+on|reflect\w*|align\w*|"
222	    r"underscore\w*|in principle|plausibl\w*|tends?\s+to|contextualiz\w*|represent\w*|modulat\w*|"
223	    r"reinforc\w*|poses?\b|requir\w*|nuance\w*|distinction\w*|dimension\w*|paradox\w*|conceptual\w*|"
224	    r"scaffold\w*|thesis|hypothes\w*|unresolved|underexplored|remain\w*|applies\b|extension\b)",
225	    re.IGNORECASE,
226	)
227	# Codex depth-gate iter-2 P1: a contentful CATEGORICAL header ("### Generative AI will replace all lawyers")
228	# must NOT pass as a structural label. A structural header is a NOUN-PHRASE section label with NO
229	# categorical CLAIM verb; a header carrying one is a mislabeled assertion => drops. (box2's real headers —
230	# "Mechanism Interpretation: …", "Positive Views: …", "Challenges: …", "Open Questions …" — carry none.)
231	_CLAIM_VERB_RE = re.compile(
232	    r"\b(?:will|shall|would|eliminat\w*|replac\w*|destroy\w*|caus\w*|guarantee\w*|prove[sd]?|proven|"
233	    r"cure[sd]?|kill\w*|ends?\b|end\s+of)\b",
234	    re.IGNORECASE,
235	)
236	# Codex/Fable depth-gate: a CRISP quantitative claim in ANY form — digit, spelled percent, spelled cardinal
237	# over a domain noun, a fraction "of all X", a "one in N" ratio, or a "millions of X" magnitude — is a
238	# numeric claim that must ground => NOT disclosable. Deliberately does NOT match SOFT quantifiers
239	# ("many/most/some workers") which are legitimate qualitative interpretive prose. Domain noun set is the
240	# labor-market subjects a proportion claim would quantify.
241	_QUANT_DOMAIN = r"(?:jobs|workers|occupations|tasks|employment|roles|people|firms|companies|households|professionals|positions)"
242	_QUANT_CLAIM_RE = re.compile(
243	    r"\bone\s+in\s+(?:a\s+)?(?:two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|\d+)\b"          # one in five
244	    r"|\b(?:a|one|two|three|four)\s+(?:half|third|quarter|fifth|sixth|seventh|eighth|ninth|tenth)\b"  # a fifth / two thirds
245	    r"|\b(?:half|third|quarter|fifth|sixth|seventh|eighth|ninth|tenth)\s+of\s+(?:all\s+)?" + _QUANT_DOMAIN + r"\b"  # BARE "half of all jobs" / "quarter of workers"
246	    r"|\b(?:millions?|billions?|thousands?|hundreds?)\s+of\s+" + _QUANT_DOMAIN + r"\b"        # millions of workers
247	    r"|\b(?:one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|eleven|twelve|thirteen|fourteen|fifteen|"
248	    r"sixteen|seventeen|eighteen|nineteen|twenty|thirty|forty|fifty|sixty|seventy|eighty|ninety|"
249	    r"hundred|thousand|million|billion)\b[\w\s-]{0,20}\b(?:of\s+(?:all\s+)?)?" + _QUANT_DOMAIN + r"\b",  # spelled N … jobs
250	    re.IGNORECASE,
251	)

254	def _build_pool_surnames(evidence_rows: "list[dict[str, Any]]") -> "set[str]":
255	    """Word-boundary TOKEN set of the pool's author/title words (len>=3), for the author-attribution guard.
256	    Token membership (not substring) so a fabricated 'Autor et al' can't pass via 'autor' ⊂ 'author'."""
257	    text = " ".join(
258	        f"{r.get('authors', '')} {r.get('title', '') or r.get('source_title', '')}"
259	        for r in (evidence_rows or [])
260	    ).lower()
261	    return set(_POOL_TOKEN_RE.findall(text))

263	
264	def _is_disclosable_interpretation(sentence: str, pool_surnames: "set[str]") -> bool:
265	    """True iff an UNCITED synthesis sentence is safe to render under the disclosed analyst label — a HEDGED
266	    QUALITATIVE interpretation carrying NO ungrounded SPECIFIC claim. Faithfulness guards (applied to headers
267	    TOO): any decimal / percent / integer / year => a numeric claim that MUST ground => NOT disclosable; an
268	    author attribution with ANY surname absent from the pool token-set => a fabricated study => NOT
269	    disclosable. A structural section header (no number, no bad author) is disclosable. A NON-header sentence
270	    ALSO requires a HEDGE/INTERPRETIVE cue — a bare unhedged categorical assertion is NOT disclosable.
271	    §-1.3: hedged qualitative interpretation is disclosed under an honest 'not span-verified' label; numeric,
272	    named, and bare-categorical claims still drop."""
273	    raw = _strip_markers(sentence).strip()
274	    if not raw:
275	        return False
276	    is_header = raw.startswith("#")
277	    core = raw.lstrip("#").strip()
278	    if not core:
279	        return False
280	    # numeric guard — applies to HEADERS too (Codex P1-2): a number/percent/year must ground, never disclose.
281	    if _decimals(core):
282	        return False
283	    # ANY percent/percentage word is a quantitative claim that must ground — a robust catch-all covering
284	    # every digit AND spelled form ("40%" / "forty percent" / "fifteen percent"), ending the spelled-number
285	    # enumeration (Codex depth-gate iter-3/4). Real qualitative interpretation never states a bare percent.
286	    if "percent" in core.lower():
287	        return False
288	    # any CRISP quantitative claim without the word "percent" ("a fifth of all jobs", "one in five workers",
289	    # "millions of workers", "fifteen … jobs") — same numeric-claim class; soft quantifiers (many/most) pass.
290	    if _QUANT_CLAIM_RE.search(core):
291	        return False
292	    # author-attribution guard — EVERY named surname (first + co-authors) must be a pool TOKEN (Codex/Fable P2).
293	    for m in _AUTHOR_ATTR_RE.finditer(core):
294	        if any(name.lower() not in pool_surnames for name in _SURNAME_RE.findall(m.group(0))):
295	            return False
296	    if is_header:
297	        # Codex iter-2 P1: a structural section-label header is disclosable, but a contentful CATEGORICAL
298	        # header ("### Generative AI will replace all lawyers") is a mislabeled assertion => drops.
299	        return not bool(_CLAIM_VERB_RE.search(core))
300	    # Codex/Fable P1-1: a NON-header interpretive sentence must actually be HEDGED interpretation, not a bare
301	    # unhedged categorical assertion.
302	    return bool(_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE.search(core))
303	
304	# A confidence marker is appended ONCE per sentence; this guards against a double-append when the
305	# function is (defensively) called twice on already-labeled prose.
306	_ALREADY_LABELED_MARKER = "[confidence:"

312	    (``PG_SWEEP_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS``, default ON) to be on."""
313	    fine_on = os.environ.get(_ENV_ENABLED, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
314	    coarse_on = os.environ.get(_ENV_ANALYST_KILL, "1").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
315	    return fine_on and coarse_on

334	def _resolve_span_for_evidence_id(
335	    evidence_id: str, evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], *, full_text: bool = False
336	) -> str:
337	    """Resolve an evidence_id to its cited span text (``direct_quote`` first, then ``statement``).
338	    Returns ``""`` when the id is not in the pool or carries no span text. wave-2 (#1370): ``full_text``
339	    widens the resolved text to the source's TRUSTED evidence-BODY fields (``direct_quote`` + ``statement``)
340	    so a number/word that lives in the source body but not the exact quote slice grounds — the box2
341	    over-drop where a real Brynjolfsson/Eloundou number sat in the source ``statement`` but not the exact
342	    quote slice, so a grounded synthesis sentence was dropped. Codex depth-gate P1: ``title``/``snippet``
343	    (retrieval METADATA) are DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED so a number that appears only incidentally in a search
344	    snippet or title can NEVER ground a synthesis sentence — the anti-fabrication floor stays honest."""
345	    for row in evidence_rows or []:
346	        rid = str(row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("id") or "")
347	        if rid and rid == evidence_id:
348	            if full_text:
349	                return " ".join(
350	                    str(row.get(k, "") or "") for k in ("direct_quote", "statement")
351	                ).strip()
352	            return str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
353	    return ""

356	def _resolve_sentence_span(
357	    sentence: str,
358	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
359	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
360	    *,
361	    full_text: bool = False,
362	) -> str:
363	    """Concatenate the cited spans for ALL ``[N]`` markers in ``sentence``. ``N`` indexes the
364	    bibliography 1-based -> ``bibliography[N-1].evidence_id`` -> the evidence row's span. Returns
365	    ``""`` when the sentence carries NO resolvable ``[N]`` span (=> the caller labels BUCKET_NO_SOURCE).
366	    wave-2 (#1370): ``full_text`` resolves each cited source to its WHOLE text (see
367	    ``_resolve_span_for_evidence_id``) so a synthesis sentence grounds against the whole-basket union,
368	    not the narrow direct_quote slice. PURE (no I/O)."""
369	    spans: list[str] = []
370	    for m in _NUMBERED_RE.finditer(sentence):
371	        try:
372	            n = int(m.group(1))
373	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
374	            continue
375	        if n < 1 or n > len(bibliography or []):
376	            continue
377	        entry = bibliography[n - 1]
378	        eid = str(entry.get("evidence_id") or "")
379	        if not eid:
380	            continue
381	        span = _resolve_span_for_evidence_id(eid, evidence_rows, full_text=full_text)
382	        if span:
383	            spans.append(span)
384	    return "\n".join(spans)

419	def _frozen_engine_verifies_sentence(
420	    base_sentence: str, cited_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]
421	) -> bool:
422	    """D3 P0 (#1344) — re-pass a promoted synthesis sentence through the FROZEN faithfulness engine.
423	
424	    Codex iter-4 P0: the analyst-synthesis writer cites by ``[N]`` bibliography markers, so the
425	    per-sentence ``strict_verify`` / NLI entailment gate never saw it — promoted synthesis prose entered
426	    the scored body without the same provenance gate every body claim clears. This function CLOSES that
427	    hole: it deterministically rebuilds a real ``[#ev:<key>:<start>-<end>]`` provenance token for EACH
428	    cited source (the full cited span) and calls the UNCHANGED ``verify_sentence_provenance`` — the exact
429	    strict_verify (numeric-in-span + >=2 content-word overlap + percent-role + trial-name +
430	    overstatement) AND NLI-entailment engine every body claim passes. The sentence is admissible to the
431	    body ONLY if the frozen engine returns ``is_verified``.
432	
433	    Synthetic token-safe keys (``s0``, ``s1`` ...) are used because a real evidence_id may contain
434	    characters ``_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE`` ([A-Za-z0-9_]+) rejects; the key identity is irrelevant to the
435	    engine (it grounds against the SPAN TEXT, keyed by the token's id in the pool). The full evidence row
436	    is carried into the pool (so the engine's shell / trial-name / attribution legs read real fields);
437	    only ``direct_quote`` is pinned to the resolved span text so the span offsets are exact.
438	
439	    The engine is NOT modified — this is an ADDITIVE re-pass on top of the frozen gate. FAIL-CLOSED: any
440	    resolution / engine fault returns ``False`` (the sentence drops from the body), never a silent True."""
441	    if not cited_rows:
442	        return False
443	    try:
444	        evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
445	        token_parts: list[str] = []
446	        for idx, row in enumerate(cited_rows):
447	            span = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
448	            if not span.strip():
449	                continue
450	            key = f"s{idx}"
451	            pool_row = dict(row)
452	            pool_row["direct_quote"] = span
453	            evidence_pool[key] = pool_row
454	            token_parts.append(f"[#ev:{key}:0-{len(span)}]")
455	        if not evidence_pool:
456	            return False
457	        # Strip the writer's [N] / [confidence:] brackets from the prose BEFORE appending the real
458	        # provenance tokens, so the engine reads the claim prose (never a bracket digit as a claim
459	        # number) plus exactly the reconstructed [#ev] tokens.
460	        prose = _strip_markers(base_sentence).strip()
461	        if not prose:
462	            return False
463	        converted = f"{prose} {' '.join(token_parts)}"
464	        result = verify_sentence_provenance(converted, evidence_pool)
465	        return bool(getattr(result, "is_verified", False))
466	    except Exception as exc:  # fail-closed: an engine/wiring fault DROPS the sentence, never admits it
467	        logger.warning(
468	            "[analyst_deviation] D3 frozen-engine re-pass faulted (fail-closed DROP): %s", exc,
469	        )
470	        return False
471

473	def promote_synthesis_entailment_finding(
474	    audit_sentence: str, cited_rows: "list[dict[str, Any]]", *, entails_fn=None
475	) -> bool:
476	    """I-deepfix-006-compose C3 — the ENTAILMENT analog of the D3 ``_frozen_engine_verifies_sentence``
477	    promote hook, living beside it in the analyst module. A cross-source SYNTHESIS finding is a
478	    PARAPHRASE that fuses several corroborating spans, so the D3 verbatim ``_span_grounds_sentence`` leg
479	    (>=2 content-word overlap) wrongly drops it. This hook instead confirms the finding is (a) number-
480	    grounded in the union of its cited spans AND (b) ENTAILED by that union — delegating to the C1
481	    ``synthesis_entailment_verify.entailment_grounds_sentence`` (the SAME numeric + directional-NLI legs
482	    the C1 verify path uses). Returns True => the finding may be PROMOTED into the D8 4-role input set;
483	    a NON-entailed / number-mismatched finding => False (not promoted). FAIL-CLOSED on a wiring fault
484	    (a missing module / import error returns False, never a silent True)."""
485	    if not cited_rows:
486	        return False
487	    try:
488	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.synthesis_entailment_verify import (
489	            entailment_grounds_sentence,
490	        )
491	    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover — synthesis_entailment_verify is stable in-tree
492	        logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] C3 entailment promote hook unavailable (fail-closed): %s", exc)
493	        return False
494	    return bool(entailment_grounds_sentence(audit_sentence, cited_rows, entails_fn=entails_fn))
495	
496	
497	def _default_sentinel_judge() -> "Callable[[str, str], bool]":
498	    """Build the real groundedness judge: the certified MiniMax-M2 Sentinel decomposition (a SECOND
499	    family vs the GLM/DeepSeek writer, so two-family holds). Returns a ``judge_fn(claim, span) -> bool``
500	    where True == the span SUPPORTS the claim. Resolved LAZILY so importing this module never
501	    constructs a transport. FAIL-CLOSED: any construction / call failure surfaces as ``False`` (=>
502	    BUCKET_LOW), never a silent True."""
503	    def _judge(claim: str, span: str) -> bool:
504	        if not span.strip():
505	            return False
506	        try:
507	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_adapter import (
508	                _configured_sentinel_slug,
509	                run_sentinel,
510	            )
511	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import EvidenceDocument
512	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import (
513	                OpenRouterRoleTransport,
514	                _default_role_http_client,
515	            )
516	            from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_contract import SentinelVerdict
517	        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover — wiring resolved at runtime on the paid path
518	            logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] sentinel imports unavailable (fail-closed LOW): %s", exc)
519	            return False
520	        try:
521	            slug = _configured_sentinel_slug()
522	            if not slug:
523	                logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] no Sentinel slug configured (fail-closed LOW)")
524	                return False
525	            transport = OpenRouterRoleTransport(_default_role_http_client())
526	            result, _records = run_sentinel(
527	                transport,
528	                claim,
529	                [EvidenceDocument(doc_id="span", text=span)],
530	                model_slug=slug,
531	            )
532	            # GROUNDED + a clean parse == supported. UNGROUNDED OR a fail-closed parse == NOT supported
533	            # (BUCKET_LOW). NEVER trust a non-clean parse as supported (lethal-inversion guard).
534	            return bool(result.verdict == SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED and result.parsed_ok)
535	        except Exception as exc:  # transport flap / cap-adjacent / parse error -> fail-closed LOW
536	            logger.warning("[analyst_deviation] sentinel call failed (fail-closed LOW): %s", exc)
537	            return False
538	    return _judge
539	
540	
541	def screen_synthesis_against_baskets(
542	    text: str,
543	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
544	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
545	    *,
546	    judge_fn: "Callable[[str, str], bool] | None" = None,
547	) -> "tuple[str, dict[str, int]]":
548	    """Bring the analyst-synthesis layer UNDER the faithfulness engine. Returns ``(body_text, telemetry)``.
549	
550	    Two modes, one gate flag (``PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_PROMOTE_GROUNDED``):
551	
552	    * D3 FAIL-CLOSED (promote flag ON) — the gate that lets the analyst layer turn ON safely. A
553	      synthesis sentence renders in ``body_text`` ONLY if it passes BOTH legs of the frozen engine —
554	      the deterministic span-grounding leg (``_span_grounds_sentence``: numbers + >=2 content-word
555	      overlap vs the cited span) AND the D8/NLI groundedness judge. A passing sentence is
556	      KEEP-and-PROMOTE'd (BUCKET_MODERATE). A sentence that FAILS either leg — ungrounded, no-source,
557	      or judge-fault — is DROPPED from ``body_text`` (fail-closed) and surfaced only in the drop
558	      telemetry + a fail-LOUD log, NEVER rendered as a hedged body claim.
559	
560	    * LEGACY ADVISORY (promote flag OFF, default) — byte-identical KEEP-and-LABEL: no-source sentences
561	      get a BUCKET_NO_SOURCE marker, judge-unsupported sentences a BUCKET_LOW marker, supported
562	      sentences pass bare. Nothing is dropped.
563	
564	    For each synthesis sentence the ``[N]`` markers resolve to cited evidence spans. ``judge_fn``
565	    (``(claim, span) -> bool``, True == supported) is INJECTABLE for offline tests; the default lazily
566	    builds the certified-Sentinel judge. FAIL-CLOSED on any judge fault (LABEL LOW in legacy mode; DROP
567	    in D3 mode — both the safe direction). NEVER touches ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role (this gate is
568	    ADDED on top of the frozen engine). Returns the input unchanged (telemetry zeroed) when disabled."""
569	    telemetry = {
570	        "synthesis_deviation_labeled_count": 0,
571	        "synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count": 0,
572	        "synthesis_deviation_promoted_count": 0,
573	        "synthesis_deviation_dropped_count": 0,
574	    }
575	    if not text or not text.strip() or not deviation_check_enabled():
576	        return text, telemetry
577	
578	    # I-deepfix-001 M6 (Layer 2): default-OFF PROMOTE mode — a grounded sentence is positively labeled
579	    # (KEEP-and-PROMOTE) instead of passing bare. Resolved once; OFF => grounded sentences stay bare.
580	    promote = promote_grounded_enabled()
581	
582	    if judge_fn is None:
583	        judge_fn = _default_sentinel_judge()
584	
585	    sentences = split_into_sentences(text)
586	    if not sentences:
587	        return text, telemetry
588	
589	    # Resolve each sentence's cited span ONCE (pure). A sentence with no resolvable span never calls
590	    # the judge (it is a BUCKET_NO_SOURCE label, not an UNSUPPORTED verdict). Span resolution reads the
591	    # ``[N]`` markers, which are untouched by the confidence-marker strip below.
592	    spans = [_resolve_sentence_span(s, bibliography, evidence_rows) for s in sentences]
593	    # I-deepfix-001 wave-2 (#1370) basket faithfulness (§-1.3): in basket mode, ALSO resolve each sentence's
594	    # cited sources to their WHOLE-basket full text (direct_quote + statement + title + snippet), unioned
595	    # across all cited [N], so a synthesis sentence whose grounded numbers/words live anywhere in its cited
596	    # sources is not falsely dropped for citing only the narrow direct_quote slice (box2 over-drop; harness
597	    # T2). A number in NO cited source still drops (harness T3). The legacy 4-leg path keeps the narrow
598	    # `spans`. Default-ON within basket mode; PG_ANALYST_SYNTHESIS_BASKET_FULLTEXT=0 reverts to the narrow union.
599	    basket_spans = (
600	        [_resolve_sentence_span(s, bibliography, evidence_rows, full_text=True) for s in sentences]
601	        if (basket_mode_enabled() and _basket_fulltext_enabled())
602	        else spans
603	    )
604	    # wave-2 (#1370) DEPTH disclosed-analyst layer: computed ONCE (not per-sentence). When ON, an uncited
605	    # hedged QUALITATIVE interpretive sentence renders under the analyst disclosure instead of dropping.
606	    _disclose_analyst = basket_mode_enabled() and _disclosed_analyst_enabled()
607	    _pool_surnames = _build_pool_surnames(evidence_rows) if _disclose_analyst else set()
608	    # D3 P0 (#1344): the cited EVIDENCE ROWS per sentence, for the frozen-engine re-pass in PROMOTE
609	    # mode (a real [#ev:id:start-end] token is rebuilt per cited row -> verify_sentence_provenance).
610	    # Resolved once (pure); used only in the promote branch below.
611	    cited_rows_per_sentence = [
612	        _resolve_sentence_ev_rows(s, bibliography, evidence_rows) for s in sentences
613	    ]
614	
615	    # I-deepfix-001 D3 P1 (#1344): screen the BARE prose. A pre-labeled sentence (already carrying a
616	    # ``[confidence:...]`` marker) has that marker stripped so the judge + span-grounding legs see the
617	    # claim itself, never a self-asserted confidence tag. For an un-labeled sentence this is a no-op
618	    # (byte-identical). In PROMOTE mode the stripped form is what re-enters the gate and is re-labeled;
619	    # the legacy path still short-circuits on the ORIGINAL sentence (idempotent keep-unchanged).
620	    screen_sentences = [_strip_confidence_markers(s) for s in sentences]
621	
622	    # BOUNDED-PARALLEL groundedness for the sentences that DO resolve a span (never a serial unbounded
623	    # loop). A per-future deadline fail-CLOSES to "not supported" (=> BUCKET_LOW).
624	    supported: dict[int, bool] = {}
625	    judge_indices = [i for i, sp in enumerate(spans) if sp.strip()]
626	    if judge_indices:
627	        deadline = _deadline_s()
628	        # Codex wave-2 P1 (hang class): the judge futures run CONCURRENTLY in the
629	        # pool, so the batch is bounded by ONE TOTAL wall deadline, never the sum of
630	        # per-future deadlines. AND the pool is shut down with wait=False +
631	        # cancel_futures=True so a Sentinel worker still stuck in a slow role-
632	        # transport call is ABANDONED — the `with`-block shutdown(wait=True) would
633	        # otherwise re-block on the timed-out worker and stall the synthesis return.
634	        # Every unresolved sentence fail-CLOSES to LOW (not supported).
635	        pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_max_inflight())
636	        try:
637	            futures = {
638	                pool.submit(judge_fn, screen_sentences[i], spans[i]): i for i in judge_indices
639	            }
640	            _end = time.monotonic() + deadline
641	            for fut, i in futures.items():
642	                _remaining = max(0.0, _end - time.monotonic())
643	                try:
644	                    supported[i] = bool(fut.result(timeout=_remaining))
645	                except (_FutureTimeout, Exception) as exc:  # fail-closed LOW on any judge fault
646	                    logger.warning(
647	                        "[analyst_deviation] groundedness judge fault on sentence %d "
648	                        "(fail-closed LOW): %s", i, exc,
649	                    )
650	                    supported[i] = False
651	        finally:
652	            # Never block the synthesis return on a stuck judge worker.
653	            pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
654	        # Any sentence whose future never resolved within the TOTAL deadline is
655	        # fail-closed to LOW (covers the case where the loop exhausted the wall
656	        # budget before reaching a later future).
657	        for i in judge_indices:
658	            supported.setdefault(i, False)
659	
660	    out_sentences: list[str] = []
661	    dropped: list[str] = []
662	    for i, sentence in enumerate(sentences):
663	        has_span = bool(spans[i].strip())
664	
665	        if promote:
666	            # ── D3 FAIL-CLOSED gate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
667	            # A synthesis sentence enters the SCORED BODY only if it passes ALL THREE legs of the
668	            # FROZEN faithfulness engine (Codex iter-4 P0 — the promoted sentence must clear the SAME
669	            # gate every body claim clears, not a lone Sentinel judge):
670	            #   (1) the REAL ``verify_sentence_provenance`` re-pass (_frozen_engine_verifies_sentence):
671	            #       a genuine ``[#ev:id:start-end]`` provenance token is rebuilt for each cited source
672	            #       and the UNCHANGED strict_verify (numeric-in-span + >=2 content-word overlap +
673	            #       percent-role + trial-name + overstatement) AND NLI-entailment engine must return
674	            #       is_verified. THIS is the leg that was missing — a [N]-cited synthesis sentence now
675	            #       carries and clears real [#ev] provenance exactly like a body claim;
676	            #   (2) the deterministic span-grounding pre-check (_span_grounds_sentence — a conservative
677	            #       superset guard, kept so a sentence can never be promoted on the judge alone), AND
678	            #   (3) the D8/NLI groundedness judge (supported[i], the 4-role Sentinel leg).
679	            # Anything else — no cited span, engine says unverified, judge says unsupported, or a
680	            # judge/engine fault (fail-closed to False) — is DROPPED from the body and collected for the
681	            # fail-loud audit.
682	            #
683	            # P1 (#1344): a PRE-LABELED sentence does NOT get an idempotent free pass here — it is
684	            # re-screened on its bare prose (``base`` = confidence-marker stripped). An ungrounded
685	            # sentence that arrived already carrying a ``[confidence: low]`` tag is therefore DROPPED,
686	            # not admitted as a hedged body claim. A grounded one is re-promoted with a fresh moderate
687	            # marker on the stripped base (never a double marker).
688	            base = screen_sentences[i]
689	            if basket_mode_enabled():
690	                # P1 basket mode: KEEP grounded cross-source synthesis. The deterministic anti-fabrication
691	                # floor (numbers-in-union + >=2 content-word overlap vs the UNION of cited spans) is the
692	                # faithfulness gate; the extractive single-span ENTAILMENT requirement is dropped (it
693	                # over-kills interpretive synthesis). Fabricated numbers + no-citation sentences still drop.
694	                # wave-2 (#1370): ground against the WHOLE-basket FULL-TEXT union of the cited sources
695	                # (basket_spans[i]) so a real number living in a source's statement/title — not the narrow
696	                # quote slice — is not falsely dropped (box2 over-drop of the cross-source analytical layer).
697	                _bspan = basket_spans[i]
698	                grounded = bool(_bspan.strip()) and _span_grounds_sentence(base, _bspan)
699	            else:
700	                grounded = (
701	                    has_span
702	                    and supported.get(i, False)
703	                    and _span_grounds_sentence(base, spans[i])
704	                    and _frozen_engine_verifies_sentence(base, cited_rows_per_sentence[i])
705	                )
706	            if grounded:
707	                marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_MODERATE)
708	                out_sentences.append(f"{base} {marker}")
709	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_promoted_count"] += 1
710	            elif _disclose_analyst and _is_disclosable_interpretation(base, _pool_surnames):
711	                # wave-2 (#1370) DEPTH — verify-after-compose = LABEL, never DELETE: a hedged, uncited,
712	                # PURELY-QUALITATIVE interpretive sentence (the analytical connective tissue the composer is
713	                # DESIGNED to write uncited) renders under the analyst-layer DISCLOSURE instead of dropping.
714	                # A fabrication (uncited + a specific number, or a named study absent from the pool) does NOT
715	                # reach here — _is_disclosable_interpretation returns False for it — so it still drops below.
716	                marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_NO_SOURCE)
717	                out_sentences.append(f"{base} {marker}")
718	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count"] = (
719	                    telemetry.get("synthesis_deviation_disclosed_count", 0) + 1
720	                )
721	            else:
722	                dropped.append(sentence)
723	                telemetry["synthesis_deviation_dropped_count"] += 1
724	            continue
725	
726	        # ── LEGACY ADVISORY KEEP-and-LABEL (promote OFF, default) — byte-identical ────────────────
727	        # Idempotent: never double-label an already-labeled sentence (advisory mode only; the promote
728	        # gate above deliberately re-screens pre-labeled sentences rather than pass them through).
729	        if _ALREADY_LABELED_MARKER in sentence:
730	            out_sentences.append(sentence)
731	            continue
732	        if not has_span:
733	            # No resolvable cited [N] span at all -> a genuinely uncited / unresolvable claim.
734	            marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_NO_SOURCE)
735	            out_sentences.append(f"{sentence} {marker}")
736	            telemetry["synthesis_deviation_unresolved_count"] += 1
737	            continue
738	        if supported.get(i, False):
739	            out_sentences.append(sentence)  # supported -> KEEP unchanged
740	            continue
741	        # Cited span does NOT support the sentence (or the judge fail-closed) -> LABEL low, KEEP.
742	        marker = claim_labeler.render_confidence_marker(claim_labeler.BUCKET_LOW)
743	        out_sentences.append(f"{sentence} {marker}")
744	        telemetry["synthesis_deviation_labeled_count"] += 1
745	
746	    if dropped:
747	        # Fail-LOUD: the dropped sentences never reach the scored body — surface them so a D3 drop is
748	        # visible in the run log (the typed non-answer audit trail), never a silent deletion.
749	        logger.warning(
750	            "[analyst_deviation] D3 fail-closed: DROPPED %d ungrounded/no-source synthesis sentence(s) "
751	            "from the scored body (retained only in this audit log, never rendered as a body claim): %s",
752	            len(dropped), dropped,
753	        )

content.text: Clean naming throughout. Continuing to the remaining files.

content.content:
753	        )
754	    return " ".join(out_sentences), telemetry
755

1	"""Atom citation validator + gap renderer (recommended_path #4).
2	
3	Per Codex APPROVE_DESIGN 2026-05-26 (refusal_design_verdict):
4	    - Approach C hybrid: prompt-side instruction + post-hoc enforcement
5	    - STRICT layer: missing/invalid atom_id → REPLACE sentence with refusal
6	    - SOFT layer: value/endpoint/entity/timepoint mismatch → log only, do
7	      NOT replace (during demo period)
8	    - Sentence-level refusal granularity
9	    - gaps.json sidecar AND inline refusal markers in report.md
10	    - Multi-atom sentences: ALL cited atom_ids must exist (any missing → refuse)
11	
12	Quantitative-claim detector per Codex (triggers A + B only, with exclusions
13	for design/admin numbers; qualitative comparative outcome language also
14	required to cite atoms even without numbers).
15	
16	This module is pure functions — no I/O except gaps.json writing via
17	explicit helper. Consumer (multi_section_generator) calls
18	`validate_section()` once per V4 Pro section body, then
19	`write_gaps_sidecar()` at the end.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import json
25	import re
26	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
27	from datetime import datetime, timezone
28	from enum import Enum
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from typing import Optional
31	
32	from src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor import (
33	    ClaimAtom,
34	    format_refusal_for_missing_atom,
35	)
36	
37	
38	class RefusalAction(str, Enum):
39	    REFUSED = "refused"            # sentence replaced with refusal template
40	    ALLOWED = "allowed"            # sentence kept as-is (atom valid)
41	    LOGGED_ONLY = "logged_only"    # sentence kept but soft mismatch logged
42	
43	
44	class RefusalReason(str, Enum):
45	    MISSING_ATOM_CITATION = "missing_atom_citation"   # no atom_NNN in sentence
46	    INVALID_ATOM_ID = "invalid_atom_id"               # atom_NNN cited but not in catalog
47	    EV_CITATION_FOR_CLAIM = "ev_citation_for_claim"   # [ev_XXX] used for factual claim
48	    SOFT_MISMATCH = "soft_mismatch"                   # cited atom value/endpoint differs
49	    PARTIAL_COVERAGE_SUSPECTED = "partial_coverage_suspected"
50	    NO_VIOLATION = "no_violation"                     # sentence is valid
51	
52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Quantitative-claim detector (per Codex APPROVE_DESIGN trigger schema)
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	# Trigger A: number + endpoint vocab term
58	# Trigger B: number alone (but not design/admin numbers — see exclusions)
59	
60	# Numbers in sentence — require non-letter context to avoid matching
61	# embedded digits in endpoint names like "HbA1c" (the "1") or "T2DM"
62	# (the "2"). Same approach as atom_extractor._NUMBER_ATOM_RE.
63	_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(
64	    r"(?<![A-Za-z0-9_.])[-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?(?![A-Za-z0-9_])"
65	)
66	
67	# Endpoint vocab — superset for claim-detection (any one of these triggers
68	# atom-citation requirement when combined with a number or with a
69	# qualitative comparative term)
70	_ENDPOINT_VOCAB_RE = re.compile(
71	    r"\b("
72	    r"hba1c|fasting\s+(?:plasma\s+)?glucose|fpg|fsg|"
73	    r"body\s+weight|weight\s+(?:loss|reduction)|bmi|waist\s+circumference|"
74	    r"ldl[-\s]?c|hdl[-\s]?c|triglycerides|cholesterol|"
75	    r"blood\s+pressure|systolic|diastolic|sbp|dbp|mmhg|"
76	    r"mace|myocardial\s+infarction|stroke|cv\s+death|cardiovascular\s+death|"
77	    r"all[-\s]?cause\s+mortality|heart\s+failure|hf\s+hospitali[zs]ation|"
78	    r"egfr|uacr|aki|creatinine|"
79	    r"adverse\s+events?|aes?|serious\s+adverse|sae|discontinuation|"
80	    # Iter-2 fix (Codex iter-1 P1): common safety endpoints by name
81	    r"nausea|vomiting|diarrh(?:o)?ea|constipation|abdominal\s+pain|"
82	    r"injection[-\s]?site\s+reaction|"
83	    r"hypoglycemia|hypoglycaemia|pancreatitis|gallbladder|retinopathy|mtc|"
84	    r"hazard\s+ratio|risk\s+ratio|odds\s+ratio|relative\s+risk|"
85	    r"responder\s+rate|response\s+rate|incidence|"
86	    r"non[-\s]?inferiority|superior(?:ity)?"
87	    r")\b",
88	    re.IGNORECASE,
89	)
90	
91	# NOTE (iter-4 design decision): two prior iterations (iter-2, iter-3)
92	# attempted an eligibility-range override via these two regex constants.
93	# Codex iter-3 caught that pure-regex disambiguation between
94	# "eligibility framing + endpoint+number" and "outcome claim + baseline
95	# mention" is fundamentally fragile. Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 clinical-safety
96	# principle, the safer default is: any quantitative claim requires
97	# atom citation. Constants retained for any future revisit but NO
98	# LONGER WIRED into requires_atom_citation().
99	_ELIGIBILITY_RANGE_RE = re.compile(
100	    r"\b(?:inclusion\s+criter\w*|exclusion\s+criter\w*|"
101	    r"eligibility\s+criter\w*|eligible\s+(?:if|when)\b|"
102	    r"required\s+(?:hba1c|weight|bmi)\s+(?:of|between)\b)",
103	    re.IGNORECASE,
104	)
105	_OUTCOME_VERB_WITH_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(
106	    r"\b(?:reduc(?:ed|tions?|ing)|"
107	    r"decreas(?:ed|es?|ing)|increas(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
108	    r"chang(?:ed|es?)|improv(?:ed|ements?|ing)|"
109	    r"lower(?:ed|ing)|rais(?:ed|ing)|"
110	    r"fell|rose|dropped|"
111	    # Codex Step 3j diff iter-1 P1 #1: active outcome verbs that V4 Pro
112	    # may emit. Without these, "tirzepatide produced -2.30 percentage-point
113	    # HbA1c reductions at 40 weeks" would slip past the trial-design
114	    # exemption guard because no current verb matched.
115	    r"produc(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
116	    r"achiev(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
117	    r"demonstrat(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
118	    r"show(?:ed|s|n|ing)|"
119	    r"yield(?:ed|s|ing)|"
120	    r"deliver(?:ed|s|ing)|"
121	    r"attain(?:ed|s|ing)|"
122	    r"result(?:ed|s|ing)\s+in|"
123	    r"led\s+to|leads\s+to|leading\s+to)\s+"
124	    # Codex Step 3j diff iter-2 novel P1: optional article (a/an/the)
125	    # between verb and number. Repro: "achieved a 2.30 percentage-point
126	    # reduction" — without the article carve-out, this slipped past.
127	    r"(?:by|of|from|to|in)?\s*(?:a|an|the)?\s*[-−]?\d",
128	    re.IGNORECASE,
129	)
130	
131	# Qualitative comparative/outcome language requiring atom citation
132	# (Codex iter-1 P1 expanded: catch "more common with X than Y", "higher
133	# with X than Y", "greater reduction than", etc.)
134	_QUAL_COMPARATIVE_RE = re.compile(
135	    r"\b("
136	    r"greater(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
137	    r"less(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
138	    r"lower(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
139	    r"higher(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
140	    r"more(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"     # "more common with X than Y"
141	    r"fewer(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
142	    r"superior(?:ity)?\s+to|non[-\s]?inferior(?:ity)?\s+to|"
143	    r"statistically\s+significant|significantly\s+\w+|"
144	    r"reduced|increased|decreased|elevated|improved|worsened|"
145	    r"more\s+(?:effective|common|frequent)|less\s+(?:effective|common|frequent)|"
146	    r"compared\s+(?:to|with)|versus|vs\.?"
147	    r")\b",
148	    re.IGNORECASE,
149	)
150	
151	# Comparator/arm language that — combined with a comparative phrase —
152	# signals a factual comparative claim. Iter-4 fix (Codex iter-3 novel-P1):
153	# build comparator-arm regex from claim_atom_extractor._DRUG_RE so any
154	# drug name known to the atom extractor is also recognized here.
155	# Avoids drift between extractor's vocab and validator's vocab.
156	def _build_comparative_arm_re() -> re.Pattern:
157	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor import _DRUG_RE
158	    drug_alt = _DRUG_RE.pattern.removeprefix(r"\b(").removesuffix(r")\b")
159	    pattern = (
160	        r"\b(?:than|versus|vs\.?|compared\s+(?:to|with))\s+"
161	        r"(?:"
162	        r"placebo|control(?:s|\s+arm)?|standard\s+care|usual\s+care|"
163	        r"active\s+comparator|background\s+therapy|sham|"
164	        r"glp[-\s]?1|sglt2|dpp[-\s]?4|insulin|metformin|sulfonylurea|"
165	        + drug_alt
166	        + r")\b"
167	    )
168	    return re.compile(pattern, re.IGNORECASE)
169	
170	
171	_COMPARATIVE_ARM_RE = _build_comparative_arm_re()

173	# Numbers to EXCLUDE from Trigger B when they appear with admin/design context
174	_ADMIN_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(
175	    r"\b("
176	    r"phase\s+(?:I{1,3}V?|IV|\d+)|"
177	    r"week[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+week[s]?|"
178	    r"month[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+month[s]?|"
179	    r"year[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+year[s]?|"
180	    r"day[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+day[s]?|"
181	    r"n\s*=\s*\d+|sample\s+size\s+\d+|"
182	    r"arm\s+\d+|\d+\s+arm[s]?|"
183	    r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:mg|μg|mcg|kg|g|mL|L|U|IU)(?!\s*/[dl])"  # dose-only, lab unit still triggers
184	    r")\b",
185	    re.IGNORECASE,
186	)
187	
188	# Allowed-without-atom categories — narrative/synthesis prose
189	_NARRATIVE_CATEGORY_RE = re.compile(
190	    r"\b("
191	    r"mechanism\s+of\s+action|receptor\s+agonis[mt]|incretin|"
192	    r"open[-\s]?label|double[-\s]?blind|randomi[zs]ed|placebo[-\s]?controlled|"
193	    r"phase\s+(?:I{1,3}V?|IV|\d+)\s+(?:trial|study)|"
194	    r"inclusion\s+criter|exclusion\s+criter|eligibility|eligible\s+patients|"
195	    r"prevalence|epidemiology|burden\s+of\s+disease|"
196	    r"limitation|limited\s+by|caveat|long[-\s]?term\s+\w+\s+(?:data|safety)|"
197	    r"these\s+(?:outcomes|results|findings)\s+(?:were|are)\s+(?:consistent|in\s+line)"
198	    r")\b",
199	    re.IGNORECASE,
200	)

203	# I-gen-005 Step 3j (Codex iter-4 APPROVE_DESIGN): trial-design framing
204	# exemption for methodology narrative sentences that trigger Trigger A
205	# (endpoint vocab + number) but describe what WILL BE measured, not what
206	# WAS observed. Target repro from real V4 Pro smoke (gaps.json
207	# efficacy.s009):
208	#   "In a phase 3 trial, 1879 adults with type 2 diabetes (mean baseline
209	#    HbA1c 8.28%, mean weight 93.7 kg) were randomly assigned to
210	#    tirzepatide 5 mg, 10 mg, or 15 mg or semaglutide 1 mg, with the
211	#    primary endpoint of change in HbA1c at 40 weeks[1]"
212	#
213	# Safety guard: result-attribution patterns (independent trigger,
214	# below) catch outcome claims that would otherwise be masked by the
215	# trial-design marker — see _ENDPOINT_RESULT_ATTRIBUTION_RE.
216	#
217	# Step 3b iter-3 lesson preserved: pure-regex disambiguation between
218	# eligibility framing + endpoint+number AND outcome claim is fragile.
219	# Trial-design markers are TIGHTER (require methodology-specific phrases,
220	# not bare "baseline"); result-attribution guard catches the remaining
221	# false-positive failure modes.
222	_TRIAL_DESIGN_FRAME_RE = re.compile(
223	    r"\b("
224	    r"randomly\s+assigned\s+to|"
225	    r"randomi[zs]ed\s+to|"
226	    r"were\s+randomi[zs]ed|"
227	    r"(?:primary|secondary)\s+end\s?point\s+of|"
228	    r"(?:primary|secondary)\s+outcome\s+of|"
229	    r"in\s+a\s+phase\s+(?:I{1,3}V?|IV|\d+)\s+trial|"
230	    r"open[-\s]?label|"
231	    r"double[-\s]?blind|"
232	    r"placebo[-\s]?controlled|"
233	    r"multicent(?:re|er)(?:d|ed)?|"
234	    r"parallel[-\s]?group|"
235	    r"stratified\s+by|"
236	    r"head[-\s]?to[-\s]?head"
237	    r")\b",
238	    re.IGNORECASE,
239	)

241	# Endpoint-name alternation reused inside _ENDPOINT_RESULT_ATTRIBUTION_RE.
242	# Codex iter-2 design P1 #3 + diff iter-1 P1 #2: must mirror
243	# _ENDPOINT_VOCAB_RE coverage so the result-attribution guard doesn't
244	# under-fire on hazard ratio, pancreatitis, abdominal pain, injection-site
245	# reactions, urinary albumin excretion, etc.
246	_ENDPOINT_NAMES_ALT = (
247	    r"hba1c|glycated\s+h(?:a)?emoglobin|a1c|"
248	    r"fasting\s+(?:plasma\s+)?glucose|fpg|fsg|"
249	    r"body\s+weight|weight\s+(?:loss|reduction|change)|bmi|"
250	    r"waist\s+circumference|"
251	    r"ldl[-\s]?c|hdl[-\s]?c|triglycerides|cholesterol|"
252	    r"blood\s+pressure|systolic|diastolic|sbp|dbp|mmhg|"
253	    r"mace|myocardial\s+infarction|stroke|cv\s+death|cardiovascular\s+death|"
254	    r"all[-\s]?cause\s+mortality|heart\s+failure|hf\s+hospitali[zs]ation|"
255	    r"egfr|uacr|aki|creatinine|"
256	    r"urinary\s+albumin(?:\s+excretion)?|"
257	    r"adverse\s+events?|aes?|saes?|serious\s+adverse|discontinuation|"
258	    r"nausea|vomiting|diarrh(?:o)?ea|constipation|abdominal\s+pain|"
259	    r"injection[-\s]?site\s+reactions?|"
260	    r"hypoglycemia|hypoglycaemia|hypoglyc(?:a)?emic\s+events?|"
261	    r"pancreatitis|gallbladder|retinopathy|mtc|"
262	    r"hazard\s+ratio|risk\s+ratio|odds\s+ratio|relative\s+risk|"
263	    r"responder\s+rate|response\s+rate|incidence|"
264	    # Codex Step 3j diff iter-2 P1 continuing #2: noninferiority and
265	    # superiority are statistical endpoints in _ENDPOINT_VOCAB_RE.
266	    r"non[-\s]?inferiority|superior(?:ity)?"
267	)
268	
269	# Timepoint alternation — multiple natural forms (Codex Step 3j diff
270	# iter-1 P1 #3 + design iter-2 P1 #2). Covers:
271	#   "40 weeks", "week 40", "after 40 weeks", "by week 40",
272	#   "at the end of 40 weeks", "40-week follow-up", "40-week".
273	_TIMEPOINT_ALT = (
274	    r"\d+\s*(?:weeks?|months?|years?|days?)|"
275	    r"(?:weeks?|months?|years?|days?)\s+\d+|"
276	    r"\d+[-\s](?:week|month|year|day)(?:s|\s+follow[-\s]?up)?"
277	)
278	# Prepositions/connectors that introduce a timepoint clause. Used
279	# below; widened from bare "at" to cover real V4 Pro phrasings (Codex
280	# Step 3j diff iter-1 P1 #3). Trailing "(?:\s+(?:the|a|an))?" handles
281	# "at the 40-week follow-up" (article between prep and timepoint).
282	_TIMEPOINT_PREP_ALT = (
283	    r"(?:at|after|by|at\s+the\s+end\s+of|over|through(?:out)?|"
284	    r"during|within|in)(?:\s+(?:the|a|an))?"
285	)
286	
287	# I-gen-005 Step 3j (Codex iter-4 APPROVE_DESIGN): result-attribution
288	# patterns that DON'T use outcome verbs but still assert an outcome
289	# value. Wired as INDEPENDENT trigger (per Codex iter-3 P2): forces
290	# atom-citation requirement REGARDLESS of trial-design marker. Catches:
291	#   (a) "primary/secondary endpoint/outcome of <X> was NUMBER"
292	#   (b) "at <timepoint> was NUMBER"
293	#   (c) "<endpoint name> [at <timepoint>] was NUMBER"
294	#   (d) REVERSE-ORDER: "change|reduction|... was NUMBER ... at <timepoint>"
295	#   (e) PASSIVE/INCIDENCE: "X was reported in NUMBER", "occurred in NUMBER",
296	#       "NUMBER achieved X"
297	#
298	# Safety floor: this guard combined with _OUTCOME_VERB_WITH_NUMBER_RE
299	# means trial-design exemption only fires when the sentence has
300	# methodology marker AND no outcome-verb-with-number AND no
301	# result-attribution AND no qualitative comparative.
302	_ENDPOINT_RESULT_ATTRIBUTION_RE = re.compile(
303	    r"\b("
304	    # (a) "primary/secondary endpoint|outcome of <anything> was NUMBER"
305	    r"(?:primary|secondary)\s+(?:end\s?point|outcome)\s+of"
306	    r"[^.]{1,80}?(?:was|were|is|are|=)\s*[-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?|"
307	    # (b) "<prep> <timepoint> was NUMBER" — endpoint-at-timepoint result.
308	    # Widened from bare "at" to all _TIMEPOINT_PREP_ALT (Codex Step 3j
309	    # diff iter-1 P1 #3: "after 40 weeks", "by week 40", etc.).
310	    + _TIMEPOINT_PREP_ALT + r"\s+(?:" + _TIMEPOINT_ALT + r")"
311	    r"\s+(?:was|were|is|are|=)\s*[-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?|"
312	    # (c) "<endpoint name> [<prep> <timepoint>] was NUMBER"
313	    r"\b(?:" + _ENDPOINT_NAMES_ALT + r")"
314	    r"(?:\s+" + _TIMEPOINT_PREP_ALT + r"\s+(?:" + _TIMEPOINT_ALT + r"))?"
315	    r"\s+(?:was|were|is|are|=)\s*[-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?|"
316	    # (d) REVERSE-ORDER: "change/reduction/... was NUMBER ... <prep> <timepoint>"
317	    r"\b(?:change|reduction|level|rate|incidence|frequency)\b"
318	    r"[^.]{1,80}?(?:was|were|is|are|=)\s*[-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?"
319	    r"[^.]{1,80}?" + _TIMEPOINT_PREP_ALT + r"\s+(?:" + _TIMEPOINT_ALT + r")|"
320	    # (e) PASSIVE/INCIDENCE result attribution
321	    r"\b(?:" + _ENDPOINT_NAMES_ALT + r")\s+(?:was|were)\s+"
322	    r"(?:reported|observed|recorded)\s+in\s+\d|"
323	    r"\b(?:" + _ENDPOINT_NAMES_ALT + r")\s+occurred\s+in\s+\d|"
324	    r"\d+\s*%?\s+achieved\s+(?:" + _ENDPOINT_NAMES_ALT + r")|"
325	    # (f) VERB + ENDPOINT + "of NUMBER" — Codex Step 3j diff iter-3 P1.
326	    # Repro: "tirzepatide achieved HbA1c of 6.2% at 40 weeks."
327	    # The outcome-verb guard caught verb+NUMBER but not verb+endpoint+of+NUMBER.
328	    # iter-4 P1: also include phrasal verbs (led to, resulted in,
329	    # leading to, ...) for "led to HbA1c of 6.2%" and "resulted in
330	    # nausea of 22%" patterns.
331	    r"\b(?:produc(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
332	    r"achiev(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
333	    r"demonstrat(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
334	    r"show(?:ed|s|n|ing)|"
335	    r"yield(?:ed|s|ing)|"
336	    r"deliver(?:ed|s|ing)|"
337	    r"attain(?:ed|s|ing)|"
338	    r"reduc(?:ed|tions?|ing)|"
339	    r"lower(?:ed|ing)|rais(?:ed|ing)|"
340	    r"increas(?:ed|es?|ing)|decreas(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
341	    r"chang(?:ed|es?)|improv(?:ed|ements?|ing)|"
342	    r"led\s+to|leads\s+to|leading\s+to|"
343	    r"result(?:ed|s|ing)\s+in)"
344	    # iter-5 P1: optional article/modifier (a/an/the/mean/median/
345	    # baseline/change in) before the endpoint. Repro: "led to an
346	    # HbA1c of 6.2%" / "resulted in mean HbA1c of 6.2%" / "led to
347	    # a change in HbA1c of -2.30" — last needs 2 modifiers.
348	    r"\s+(?:(?:a|an|the|mean|median|baseline|average|change\s+in)\s+){0,3}"
349	    r"(?:" + _ENDPOINT_NAMES_ALT + r")\s+of\s+[-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?"
350	    r")",
351	    re.IGNORECASE,
352	)
353	
354	
355	# Atom-ID citation pattern: atom_NNN
356	_ATOM_ID_RE = re.compile(r"\batom_\d{3,}\b")
357	_EV_ID_RE = re.compile(r"\[?ev_\d{3,}(?::\d+-\d+)?\]?")
358	_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:ev_\d{3,}:\d+-\d+\]")
359	
360	# I-gen-005 Step 3b commit 2 (Codex iter-1 P1.1): resolved verified_text
361	# contains numeric bibliography markers [1], [2], etc. (from
362	# resolve_provenance_to_citations) + atom_NNN (from V4 Pro per Step 3a)
363	# + bare [ev_XXX] (defensive). All three would be matched by _NUMBER_RE
364	# as bare numbers and trigger false Trigger B (number alone).
365	#
366	# These strip patterns produce a CLEANED COPY used for claim detection
367	# and value extraction. extract_atom_citations / extract_ev_citations
368	# still consume the ORIGINAL sentence for citation parsing.
369	_BIBLIO_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
370	_ATOM_TOKEN_FOR_STRIP_RE = re.compile(
371	    r"\(?atom_\d{3,}(?:,\s*atom_\d{3,})*\)?",
372	    re.IGNORECASE,
373	)
374	_EV_TOKEN_FOR_STRIP_RE = re.compile(
375	    r"\[?ev_\d+(?::\d+-\d+)?\]?",
376	    re.IGNORECASE,
377	)
378	
379	
380	def _strip_citation_tokens_for_detection(sentence: str) -> str:
381	    """Strip [N] bibliography markers + atom_NNN + [ev_XXX] tokens from
382	    the sentence COPY used by claim detection and number extraction.
383	
384	    Per Codex Step 3b iter-1 P1.1: validating resolved verified_text
385	    without stripping these tokens caused false Trigger B activations
386	    on narrative sentences with citation markers.
387	
388	    The original sentence is preserved for citation parsing —
389	    extract_atom_citations / extract_ev_citations should always be
390	    called on the ORIGINAL sentence, never on the cleaned copy.
391	    """
392	    s = _BIBLIO_MARKER_RE.sub(" ", sentence)
393	    s = _ATOM_TOKEN_FOR_STRIP_RE.sub(" ", s)
394	    s = _EV_TOKEN_FOR_STRIP_RE.sub(" ", s)
395	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()

423	            "reason": self.reason.value,
424	            "cited_atoms": self.cited_atoms,
425	            "missing_atoms": self.missing_atoms,
426	            "detected_endpoint": self.detected_endpoint,
427	            "detected_entity": self.detected_entity,

434	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
435	# Quantitative-claim detection
436	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
437	
438	def requires_atom_citation(sentence: str) -> tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:
439	    """Codex APPROVE_DESIGN claim detector.
440	
441	    Returns (requires_atom, reason). True if sentence makes a factual
442	    quantitative claim that needs atom_NNN citation; False for pure
443	    narrative/mechanism/trial-design prose.
444	
445	    Triggers:
446	      A: number + endpoint vocab term → requires atom
447	      B: number alone (excluding admin/design numbers) → requires atom
448	      Plus: qualitative comparative outcome language even without numbers
449	
450	    Excludes:
451	      - pure mechanism / trial identity / eligibility / background prose
452	    """
453	    s_raw = sentence.strip()
454	    if not s_raw:
455	        return False, None
456	
457	    # Step 3b commit 2: strip citation tokens for detection-time analysis.
458	    # [N] markers + atom_NNN + [ev_XXX] would otherwise be parsed as
459	    # bare numbers and trigger false claim-required.
460	    s = _strip_citation_tokens_for_detection(s_raw)
461	    if not s:
462	        return False, None
463	
464	    numbers = _NUMBER_RE.findall(s)
465	    has_endpoint = bool(_ENDPOINT_VOCAB_RE.search(s))
466	    has_qual_comparative = bool(_QUAL_COMPARATIVE_RE.search(s))
467	    has_comparator_arm = bool(_COMPARATIVE_ARM_RE.search(s))
468	    has_outcome_number = bool(numbers) and has_endpoint
469	
470	    # Iter-4 decision (Codex iter-3 continuing-P1): the eligibility
471	    # override was removed. Two iterations of regex tightening (iter-2,
472	    # iter-3) still couldn't reliably distinguish:
473	    #   - "Patients meeting inclusion criteria had HbA1c of 6.8%"  (outcome — must require)
474	    #   - "Eligible adults had baseline HbA1c between 7.0 and 10.0" (eligibility — should allow)
475	    # Codex correctly observed that pure-regex disambiguation is
476	    # fundamentally fragile here.
477	    #
478	    # SAFE DEFAULT: any quantitative claim requires atom citation. If
479	    # V4 Pro cannot find a supporting atom (because the sentence is
480	    # actually just eligibility framing), V4 Pro emits a refusal block.
481	    # The refusal reads slightly awkwardly for benign eligibility
482	    # sentences ("Insufficient evidence about HbA1c..."), but this is
483	    # SAFER than masking real outcome claims that happen to share
484	    # eligibility-frame keywords.
485	    #
486	    # Trade-off accepted per clinical-safety principle (CLAUDE.md §-1.1):
487	    # false negative (over-refuse benign eligibility) is recoverable;
488	    # false positive (mask real outcome) is lethal.
489	
490	    # I-gen-005 Step 3j (Codex iter-4 APPROVE_DESIGN): independent
491	    # result-attribution trigger. Catches outcome claims that lack an
492	    # explicit outcome verb but still assert a value (copular endpoint
493	    # attribution, value-at-timepoint, passive/incidence). Fires
494	    # BEFORE narrative / trial-design exemption checks so result claims
495	    # can never be masked by methodology framing.
496	    if _ENDPOINT_RESULT_ATTRIBUTION_RE.search(s):
497	        return True, "trigger_endpoint_result_attribution"
498	
499	    # Pure narrative categories — never require atom citation UNLESS
500	    # there's also an outcome-number combo or qualitative comparative.

content.text: Reading further, but this is likely consistent naming. Let's continue to end of file.

content.content:
500	    # there's also an outcome-number combo or qualitative comparative.
501	    narrative_matches = _NARRATIVE_CATEGORY_RE.findall(s)
502	    if narrative_matches and not has_outcome_number and not has_qual_comparative:
503	        return False, None
504	
505	    # I-gen-005 Step 3j (Codex iter-4 APPROVE_DESIGN): trial-design
506	    # framing exemption. Allow methodology-narrative sentences that
507	    # describe what WILL BE measured (e.g. "primary endpoint of change
508	    # in HbA1c at 40 weeks") without forcing atom citation, as long
509	    # as no outcome-verb-with-number AND no result-attribution
510	    # (already returned above) AND no qualitative comparative.
511	    # Order matters: this runs AFTER the result-attribution independent
512	    # trigger so a sentence like "primary endpoint of change in HbA1c
513	    # was -2.30" still REFUSES via the independent trigger first.
514	    if (
515	        _TRIAL_DESIGN_FRAME_RE.search(s)
516	        and not _OUTCOME_VERB_WITH_NUMBER_RE.search(s)
517	        and not has_qual_comparative
518	    ):
519	        return False, None
520	
521	    # Trigger A: number + endpoint
522	    if has_outcome_number:
523	        return True, "trigger_A_number_plus_endpoint"
524	
525	    # Qualitative comparative outcome language with an endpoint OR
526	    # an explicit comparator arm (versus/than/compared to PLACEBO/DRUG).
527	    # Catches "Tirzepatide showed greater reduction than semaglutide"
528	    # (no endpoint vocab term but clearly a comparative claim).
529	    if has_qual_comparative and (has_endpoint or has_comparator_arm):
530	        return True, "trigger_qualitative_comparative"
531	
532	    # Trigger B: number alone, but check for admin/design exclusions
533	    if numbers:
534	        admin_matches = _ADMIN_NUMBER_RE.findall(s)
535	        # If the sentence has more numbers than admin matches, at least
536	        # one number is "outcome-like" — require citation.
537	        if len(numbers) > len(admin_matches):
538	            return True, "trigger_B_number_alone"
539	
540	    return False, None

543	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
544	# Citation parsing
545	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
546	
547	def extract_atom_citations(sentence: str) -> list[str]:
548	    """All atom_NNN citations in this sentence."""
549	    return _ATOM_ID_RE.findall(sentence)
550	
551	
552	def extract_ev_citations(sentence: str) -> list[str]:
553	    """All [ev_XXX] or ev_XXX citations in this sentence."""
554	    return _EV_ID_RE.findall(sentence)
555	
556	
557	def has_ev_citation_for_factual_claim(sentence: str) -> bool:
558	    """True if sentence cites [ev_XXX] for what should be a factual claim.
559	
560	    Per Codex APPROVE_DESIGN: '[ev_XXX] is for non-claim transitions only.'
561	    """
562	    requires_atom, _ = requires_atom_citation(sentence)
563	    if not requires_atom:
564	        return False
565	    return bool(_EV_ID_RE.search(sentence)) and not _ATOM_ID_RE.search(sentence)
