568	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
569	# Sentence splitter (decimal-aware, matches atom_extractor's logic)
570	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
571	
572	# Decimal-aware sentence split. Two boundary patterns (alternation):
573	#   (a) [.;!?] followed by whitespace + [A-Z\[] or end-of-text
574	#       (standard prose boundary; lookbehind handles decimals via
575	#        sentinel-pre-pass in split_sentences below)
576	#   (b) [.;!?]\[N\] + whitespace + [A-Z\[]
577	#       (resolved-citation boundary — Step 3b commit 2 follow-up iter-3
578	#        per Codex PR #906 iter-3 P1). resolve_provenance_to_citations
579	#        emits "<sentence>.[1] <next_sentence>.[2]" with the citation
580	#        marker GLUED to the period. Pattern (a) alone misses this:
581	#        "[1] sentence_two" matches but skips before "[1]". Pattern (b)
582	#        explicitly consumes the [N] marker as part of the boundary so
583	#        the SECOND sentence is its own validator input.
584	_SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
585	    r"[.;!?](?:\[\d+\])?(?=\s+(?:[A-Z\[]|$))"
586	)
587	
588	
589	def split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
590	    """Decimal-aware sentence split. Resolved bibliography markers
591	    (`.[N]`) attach to the preceding sentence; whitespace is the
592	    consumed delimiter only.
593	
594	    Step 3h fix (real-data smoke PR #911 follow-up): paren-aware —
595	    `;` and other punctuation INSIDE `(...)`, `[...]`, `{...}` do NOT
596	    boundary. V4 Pro routinely writes "...(95% CI, -0.28 to -0.03;
597	    P=0.02) for the 5 mg dose, -0.39 percentage points (..." as ONE
598	    sentence; the embedded `;` inside the CI parens previously broke
599	    it into fragments, causing false refusals.
600	
601	    Strategy:
602	      1. Protect "<digit>.<digit>" via sentinel so "2.30" stays intact.
603	      2. finditer boundary positions (punctuation + optional [N]).
604	      3. SKIP boundaries that fall inside an open paren/bracket/brace.
605	      4. Slice manually: take protected[last_end:boundary.end()] as the
606	         sentence, advance past whitespace, continue.
607	      5. Restore decimal sentinels in each piece.
608	    """
609	    if not text:
610	        return []
611	    sentinel = "\x00DEC\x00"
612	    protected = re.sub(
613	        r"(\d)\.(\d)",
614	        lambda m: f"{m.group(1)}{sentinel}{m.group(2)}",
615	        text,
616	    )
617	    pieces: list[str] = []
618	    last_end = 0
619	    n = len(protected)
620	    open_map = {"(": ")", "[": "]", "{": "}"}
621	    close_set = {")", "]", "}"}
622	
623	    def _is_inside_paren(pos: int) -> bool:
624	        """True if pos falls inside an unclosed paren/bracket/brace.
625	        Note: balanced [N] biblio markers don't count as 'inside' for
626	        boundaries past them — depth resets to 0 when closing bracket
627	        is seen."""
628	        depth = 0
629	        for i in range(pos):
630	            ch = protected[i]
631	            if ch in open_map:
632	                depth += 1
633	            elif ch in close_set and depth > 0:
634	                depth -= 1
635	        return depth > 0
636	
637	    for m in _SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE.finditer(protected):
638	        if _is_inside_paren(m.start()):
639	            continue
640	        end_pos = m.end()
641	        pieces.append(protected[last_end:end_pos])
642	        while end_pos < n and protected[end_pos].isspace():
643	            end_pos += 1
644	        last_end = end_pos
645	    if last_end < n:
646	        pieces.append(protected[last_end:])
647	    return [p.replace(sentinel, ".").strip() for p in pieces if p.strip()]
648	
649	
650	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
651	# Validation core
652	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
653	
654	def validate_sentence(
655	    sentence: str,
656	    sentence_index: int,
657	    section_id: str,
658	    section_title: str,
659	    catalog: dict[str, ClaimAtom],
660	) -> GapRecord:
661	    """Validate one sentence against the atom catalog.
662	
663	    Returns a GapRecord with action + reason.
664	
665	    STRICT layer (replaces sentence with refusal):
666	      - Sentence requires atom citation BUT no atom_NNN cited
667	      - Cited atom_NNN doesn't exist in catalog
668	      - [ev_XXX] cited for a factual claim (no atom_NNN)
669	      - Multi-atom: ANY cited atom_NNN missing → replace
670	
671	    SOFT layer (logged_only; sentence kept as-is):
672	      - Cited atom value differs from sentence value (paraphrase)
673	      - Cited atom endpoint differs from sentence endpoint
674	    """
675	    cited_atoms = extract_atom_citations(sentence)
676	    requires_atom, claim_trigger = requires_atom_citation(sentence)
677	
678	    # Non-claim sentence with no atoms cited → allowed (narrative)
679	    if not requires_atom and not cited_atoms:
680	        return GapRecord(
681	            section_id=section_id,
682	            section_title=section_title,
683	            sentence_index=sentence_index,
684	            original_sentence=sentence,
685	            rendered_text=sentence,
686	            action=RefusalAction.ALLOWED,
687	            reason=RefusalReason.NO_VIOLATION,
688	            notes="narrative/non-claim sentence; no atom citation required",
689	        )
690	
691	    # Requires atom but no atom cited
692	    if requires_atom and not cited_atoms:
693	        # Check for [ev_XXX] misuse
694	        if extract_ev_citations(sentence):
695	            return _build_refusal_record(
696	                sentence, sentence_index, section_id, section_title,
697	                reason=RefusalReason.EV_CITATION_FOR_CLAIM,
698	                claim_trigger=claim_trigger,
699	            )
700	        return _build_refusal_record(
701	            sentence, sentence_index, section_id, section_title,
702	            reason=RefusalReason.MISSING_ATOM_CITATION,
703	            claim_trigger=claim_trigger,
704	        )
705	
706	    # Cited atoms exist — validate each exists in catalog
707	    missing = [aid for aid in cited_atoms if aid not in catalog]
708	    if missing:
709	        return _build_refusal_record(
710	            sentence, sentence_index, section_id, section_title,
711	            reason=RefusalReason.INVALID_ATOM_ID,
712	            missing_atoms=missing,
713	            cited_atoms=cited_atoms,
714	            claim_trigger=claim_trigger,
715	        )
716	
717	    # All cited atoms valid — SOFT mismatch checks (logged_only)
718	    # Step 3b commit 2: extract numbers from the citation-stripped copy
719	    # so atom_NNN/biblio markers do not pollute detected_values.
720	    soft_notes = []
721	    sentence_for_values = _strip_citation_tokens_for_detection(sentence)
722	    detected_values = _NUMBER_RE.findall(sentence_for_values)
723	    detected_value_set = set(detected_values)
724	    for aid in cited_atoms:
725	        atom = catalog[aid]
726	        # Iter-2 fix (Codex iter-1 P2): tighten value matching to
727	        # numeric-token boundaries. Old substring check matched
728	        # "2.30" inside "12.30" (false negative on mismatch). New
729	        # check uses the same _NUMBER_RE that extracted detected_values
730	        # — equality on token strings (sign-normalized).
731	        # Step 3h fix (real-data smoke PR #911 finding): normalize
732	        # BOTH Unicode minus variants on BOTH sides. V4 Pro emits
733	        # U+2212 (MINUS SIGN, "−") + U+2013/2014 (en/em dash) in
734	        # clinical text; atom_extractor stores values with U+002D
735	        # (HYPHEN-MINUS, "-"). Without sentence-side normalization,
736	        # every clinical negative value reads as false SOFT_MISMATCH.
737	        # Real smoke showed 4/4 soft_mismatches were this artifact.
738	        def _normalize_minus(s: str) -> str:
739	            return s.replace("−", "-").replace("–", "-").replace("—", "-")
740	        atom_val_normalized = _normalize_minus(atom.value)
741	        atom_val_unsigned = atom_val_normalized.lstrip("-")
742	        sentence_unsigned = {
743	            _normalize_minus(v).lstrip("-") for v in detected_value_set
744	        }
745	        if atom_val_unsigned and atom_val_unsigned not in sentence_unsigned:
746	            soft_notes.append(
747	                f"atom={aid} value={atom.value!r} not in sentence numeric tokens"
748	            )
749	
750	    if soft_notes:
751	        return GapRecord(
752	            section_id=section_id,
753	            section_title=section_title,
754	            sentence_index=sentence_index,
755	            original_sentence=sentence,
756	            rendered_text=sentence,  # KEEP (soft layer)
757	            action=RefusalAction.LOGGED_ONLY,
758	            reason=RefusalReason.SOFT_MISMATCH,
759	            cited_atoms=cited_atoms,
760	            detected_values=detected_values,
761	            notes="; ".join(soft_notes),
762	        )
763	
764	    # Clean — allowed
765	    return GapRecord(
766	        section_id=section_id,
767	        section_title=section_title,
768	        sentence_index=sentence_index,
769	        original_sentence=sentence,
770	        rendered_text=sentence,
771	        action=RefusalAction.ALLOWED,
772	        reason=RefusalReason.NO_VIOLATION,
773	        cited_atoms=cited_atoms,
774	        detected_values=detected_values,
775	    )
776	
777	
778	def _build_refusal_record(
779	    sentence: str,
780	    sentence_index: int,
781	    section_id: str,
782	    section_title: str,
783	    *,
784	    reason: RefusalReason,
785	    missing_atoms: Optional[list[str]] = None,
786	    cited_atoms: Optional[list[str]] = None,
787	    claim_trigger: Optional[str] = None,
788	) -> GapRecord:
789	    """Build a refusal GapRecord. Renders the refusal template using
790	    detected endpoint/entity/timepoint from sentence text."""
791	    endpoint = _detect_endpoint_in_sentence(sentence) or "this outcome"
792	    entity = _detect_entity_in_sentence(sentence) or ""
793	    timepoint = _detect_timepoint_in_sentence(sentence) or ""
794	
795	    rendered = format_refusal_for_missing_atom(
796	        endpoint=endpoint,
797	        entity=entity,
798	        timepoint=timepoint,
799	    )
800	
801	    # Iter-2 fix (Codex iter-1 P2): preserve detected_values on refusal
802	    # records for downstream audit.
803	    detected_values = _NUMBER_RE.findall(sentence)
804	
805	    return GapRecord(
806	        section_id=section_id,
807	        section_title=section_title,
808	        sentence_index=sentence_index,
809	        original_sentence=sentence,
810	        rendered_text=rendered,
811	        action=RefusalAction.REFUSED,
812	        reason=reason,
813	        cited_atoms=cited_atoms or [],
814	        missing_atoms=missing_atoms or [],
815	        detected_endpoint=endpoint,
816	        detected_entity=entity,
817	        detected_timepoint=timepoint,
818	        detected_values=detected_values,
819	        notes=f"claim_trigger={claim_trigger}" if claim_trigger else None,
820	    )
821	
822	
823	def _detect_endpoint_in_sentence(sentence: str) -> Optional[str]:
824	    m = _ENDPOINT_VOCAB_RE.search(sentence)
825	    return m.group(1).lower() if m else None
826	
827	
828	def _detect_entity_in_sentence(sentence: str) -> Optional[str]:
829	    # Reuse drug regex from atom_extractor — local import to avoid cycle
830	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor import _DRUG_RE
831	    m = _DRUG_RE.search(sentence)
832	    return m.group(0).strip() if m else None
833	
834	
835	def _detect_timepoint_in_sentence(sentence: str) -> Optional[str]:
836	    m = re.search(
837	        r"\b(?:at|after|by|over)\s+(\d+\s*(?:weeks?|months?|years?|days?))\b",
838	        sentence, re.IGNORECASE,
839	    )
840	    return m.group(1).lower() if m else None
841	
842	
843	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
844	# Section-level validation
845	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
846	
847	@dataclass
848	class SectionValidationResult:
849	    section_id: str
850	    section_title: str
851	    original_text: str
852	    rendered_text: str
853	    gap_records: list[GapRecord] = field(default_factory=list)
854	
855	    @property
856	    def refusal_count(self) -> int:
857	        return sum(1 for g in self.gap_records if g.action == RefusalAction.REFUSED)
858	
859	    @property
860	    def soft_mismatch_count(self) -> int:
861	        return sum(1 for g in self.gap_records if g.action == RefusalAction.LOGGED_ONLY)
862	
863	    @property
864	    def allowed_count(self) -> int:
865	        return sum(1 for g in self.gap_records if g.action == RefusalAction.ALLOWED)
866	
867	
868	def validate_section(
869	    section_text: str,
870	    section_id: str,
871	    section_title: str,
872	    catalog: dict[str, ClaimAtom],
873	) -> SectionValidationResult:
874	    """Validate all sentences in a section, preserving paragraph
875	    structure. Sentence-level refusal: refused sentences are replaced
876	    in `rendered_text`, others kept as-is.
877	
878	    Step 3b commit 2 (Codex iter-2 P2.4): split on paragraph boundaries
879	    FIRST, validate per paragraph, join with \\n\\n. Sentence_index is
880	    MONOTONIC across paragraphs so gaps.json claim_id values stay
881	    unique. Previously a single " ".join collapsed all paragraphs into
882	    a single line — broke report.md formatting.
883	    """
884	    if not section_text or not section_text.strip():
885	        return SectionValidationResult(
886	            section_id=section_id,
887	            section_title=section_title,
888	            original_text=section_text,
889	            rendered_text=section_text,
890	            gap_records=[],
891	        )
892	
893	    paragraphs = re.split(r"\n{2,}", section_text)
894	    rendered_paragraphs: list[str] = []
895	    gap_records: list[GapRecord] = []
896	    sentence_index = 0  # monotonic across paragraphs
897	
898	    for para in paragraphs:
899	        para_stripped = para.strip()
900	        if not para_stripped:
901	            rendered_paragraphs.append(para)  # preserve whitespace-only paragraph
902	            continue
903	        sentences = split_sentences(para_stripped)
904	        rendered_sentences: list[str] = []
905	        for sent in sentences:
906	            record = validate_sentence(
907	                sent, sentence_index, section_id, section_title, catalog,
908	            )
909	            gap_records.append(record)
910	            rendered_sentences.append(record.rendered_text)
911	            sentence_index += 1
912	        rendered_paragraphs.append(" ".join(rendered_sentences))
913	
914	    rendered_text = "\n\n".join(rendered_paragraphs)
915	    return SectionValidationResult(
916	        section_id=section_id,
917	        section_title=section_title,
918	        original_text=section_text,
919	        rendered_text=rendered_text,
920	        gap_records=gap_records,
921	    )
922	
923	
924	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
925	# gaps.json sidecar writer
926	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
927	
928	def build_gaps_document(
929	    document_id: str,
930	    section_results: list[SectionValidationResult],
931	) -> dict:
932	    """Per Codex APPROVE_DESIGN gaps.json schema."""
933	    return {
934	        "document_id": document_id,
935	        "generated_at": datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
936	        "sections": [
937	            {
938	                "section_id": s.section_id,
939	                "section_title": s.section_title,
940	                "claims": [g.to_dict() for g in s.gap_records],
941	                "summary": {
942	                    "total_sentences": len(s.gap_records),
943	                    "refused": s.refusal_count,
944	                    "soft_mismatch": s.soft_mismatch_count,
945	                    "allowed": s.allowed_count,
946	                },
947	            }
948	            for s in section_results
949	        ],
950	        "totals": {
951	            "total_sentences": sum(len(s.gap_records) for s in section_results),
952	            "refused": sum(s.refusal_count for s in section_results),
953	            "soft_mismatch": sum(s.soft_mismatch_count for s in section_results),
954	            "allowed": sum(s.allowed_count for s in section_results),
955	        },
956	    }
957	
958	
959	def write_gaps_sidecar(
960	    output_dir: Path,
961	    document_id: str,
962	    section_results: list[SectionValidationResult],
963	    *,
964	    filename: str = "gaps.json",
965	) -> Path:
966	    """Write the gaps.json sidecar next to report.md.
967	
968	    Returns the written path. Caller is responsible for ensuring
969	    `output_dir` exists.
970	    """
971	    doc = build_gaps_document(document_id, section_results)
972	    path = output_dir / filename
973	    path.write_text(
974	        json.dumps(doc, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),
975	        encoding="utf-8",
976	    )

content.text: Clean naming. Now the remaining files: attribution_origin_guard.py, block_page_chrome_scrub.py, boundary_conditions.py, chrome_furniture_screen.py.

content.content:
1	"""Attribution-origin provenance guard for the faithfulness engine.
2	
3	Root cause (mis-attribution, "correctness is not faithfulness"): a report claim
4	that names an explicit finder / actor ("International Labour Organization and
5	Poland's National Research Institute found that 3.5% of men's jobs...") can be
6	cited to a source that merely RE-REPORTS the finding (e.g. a UN Regional
7	Information Centre article on ``unric.org``) rather than the source that
8	PRODUCED it. Every existing faithfulness gate PASSES because the pipeline
9	verifies TEXT CONTAINMENT / ENTAILMENT, never PROVENANCE ORIGIN: the secondary
10	source's fetched span verbatim restates the attribution clause, so strict_verify
11	(evidence-id-in-pool, span-bounds, numeric-in-span, content-overlap) and the NLI
12	entailment judge all clear it. The one attribution-style guard that exists
13	(M-25a ``extract_trial_names``) is scoped to clinical-trial acronyms only. There
14	is no point in the pipeline that (a) extracts the named finder from an
15	"<ORG> found/reported/estimated Y" sentence and (b) checks whether the CITED
16	SOURCE'S IDENTITY IS that actor (primary) versus merely re-reports it
17	(secondary).
18	
19	This module is an **ADDITIVE, DISCLOSURE-ONLY** leg layered on top of
20	``verify_sentence_provenance`` (mirrors the existing ``overstatement_guard`` /
21	``shell_detector`` / ``_detect_unhedged_superlative`` additive patterns). Per the
22	WEIGHT / WITHHOLD-DISCLOSE / CONSOLIDATE architecture DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3):
23	
24	  * It NEVER drops a sentence, NEVER fails ``is_verified``, NEVER touches
25	    strict_verify / NLI / D8. It ONLY ever APPENDS a ``soft_warning`` disclosure
26	    label ("attribution via a source whose publisher identity does not match the
27	    named finder"). This TIGHTENS faithfulness (surfaces a new provenance-quality
28	    label) without relaxing any existing gate and without a hard corpus drop.
29	  * It is HIGH-PRECISION and FAIL-OPEN on ambiguity: it fires ONLY when the
30	    claim names a recognizable ORGANIZATIONAL finder (org-keyword phrase or a
31	    3-6-letter ALL-CAPS acronym) via an attribution verb AND NONE of the cited
32	    sources' publisher DOMAIN identities match that finder. When a cited source's
33	    publisher identity cannot be determined from its domain (bare gov TLD, a DOI
34	    resolver, an opaque aggregator host), the leg stays INERT — we cannot rule out
35	    that the opaque source IS the actor, so we never flag.
36	
37	Matching is done against the cited source's DOMAIN (its publisher identity),
38	NOT its title/body: a re-reporting article's headline and body naturally restate
39	the primary org's name, so matching those would suppress the exact case we target.
40	
41	Research grounding (2025-2026 frontier gap; this is not a solved problem):
42	  - Wallat et al. (UvA, 2025) "Correctness is not Faithfulness in RAG Attributions"
43	  - "Cited but Not Verified" (arXiv 2605.06635, 2026)
44	  - DeepTRACE (arXiv 2509.04499) — cite accuracy 40-80%, partly attribution to
45	    sources that merely restate.
46	  - CiteGuard (arXiv 2510.17853, 2025) — retrieval-augmented citation validation.
47	"""
48	
49	from __future__ import annotations
50	
51	import os
52	import re
53	
54	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	# Env flag (LAW VI — named, env-gated). Default ON: this is faithfulness-
56	# TIGHTENING (it only ever ADDS a disclosure soft-warning, never a drop). Setting
57	# the flag to a falsy value reverts to byte-identical pre-guard behaviour (no
58	# extra soft-warning emitted).
59	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
60	_TRUE_TOKENS = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on", "enforce", "warn"})
61	
62	
63	def attribution_origin_guard_enabled() -> bool:
64	    """Whether the attribution-origin disclosure leg is active (default ON)."""
65	    return os.getenv("PG_ATTRIBUTION_ORIGIN_GUARD", "1").strip().lower() in _TRUE_TOKENS
66	
67	
68	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	# Finder / actor extraction.
70	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
71	# Attribution verbs — the strong signal that the preceding proper-noun phrase is
72	# being named as the ORIGINATOR of the finding ("<ORG> found/reported/estimated
73	# that ..."). Present + past tense.
74	_ATTRIBUTION_VERB = (
75	    r"(?:found|finds|reported|reports|estimated|estimates|showed|shows"
76	    r"|concluded|concludes|determined|determines|projected|projects"
77	    r"|calculated|calculates|observed|observes|noted|notes|stated|states"
78	    r"|announced|announces|published|publishes|warned|warns|predicted|predicts"
79	    r"|revealed|reveals|documented|documents|identified|identifies"
80	    r"|confirmed|confirms)"
81	)
82	
83	# A single proper-noun token: a Capitalized word (allowing internal
84	# apostrophe / ampersand / period / hyphen — "Poland's", "AT&T", "U.S.").
85	_CAP_TOKEN = r"[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9'’&.\-]*"
86	# Lowercase connectors that legitimately sit INSIDE an organization name
87	# ("Bureau of Labor Statistics", "Centers for Disease Control and Prevention").
88	_CONNECTOR = r"(?:of|for|the|and|&|de|del|la|von|van|und|et|à|da|do)"
89	# A proper-noun run: one cap token followed by any number of connector-or-cap
90	# tokens. Greedy so it captures the whole "<Org> and <Org>" run before the verb.
91	_PROPER_RUN = rf"{_CAP_TOKEN}(?:\s+(?:{_CONNECTOR}|{_CAP_TOKEN}))*"
92	
93	_FINDER_RE = re.compile(rf"\b({_PROPER_RUN})\s+{_ATTRIBUTION_VERB}\b")
94	
95	# Org-type keywords: a proper-noun run that contains one of these is an
96	# INSTITUTION (not a person / place / generic capitalized phrase). Precision
97	# guard — we only treat the run as an organizational finder when it self-declares
98	# as an institution OR carries a standalone ALL-CAPS acronym token.
99	_ORG_KEYWORDS = frozenset({
100	    "organization", "organisation", "organizations", "organisations",
101	    "institute", "institutes", "institution", "institutions",
102	    "university", "universities", "college", "colleges", "school", "schools",
103	    "department", "departments", "agency", "agencies", "ministry", "ministries",
104	    "bureau", "bureaus", "association", "associations", "foundation",
105	    "foundations", "commission", "commissions", "council", "councils",
106	    "laboratory", "laboratories", "office", "offices", "fund", "funds",
107	    "bank", "banks", "center", "centre", "centers", "centres",
108	    "company", "companies", "corporation", "corporations", "committee",
109	    "committees", "society", "societies", "administration", "administrations",
110	    "authority", "authorities", "board", "boards", "consortium", "consortia",
111	    "coalition", "coalitions", "union", "unions", "federation", "federations",
112	    "network", "networks", "observatory", "programme", "program",
113	})
114	
115	# Generic words that appear in many org names — stripped from the DISTINCTIVE-word
116	# signature so matching keys on the org-specific token(s) ("labour"->generic,
117	# "poland"->distinctive). The ACRONYM builder still uses ALL significant words.
118	_GENERIC_ORG_WORDS = frozenset(_ORG_KEYWORDS) | frozenset({
119	    "national", "international", "research", "global", "world", "general",
120	    "federal", "state", "states", "united", "european", "american", "americas",
121	    "statistics", "health", "science", "sciences", "economic", "economics",
122	    "monetary", "labor", "labour", "development", "affairs", "human", "social",
123	    "public", "policy", "data", "regional", "central", "advanced", "applied",
124	    "medical", "clinical", "technology", "technical", "environmental", "energy",
125	    "finance", "financial", "trade", "labour", "education", "population",
126	})
127	
128	# All-caps acronym token (3-6 letters), e.g. "WHO", "ILO", "IMF", "OECD",
129	# "NBER". 2-letter acronyms ("US", "UN", "EU") are excluded — too noisy.
130	_ALLCAPS_ACRONYM_RE = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z]{3,6}\b")
131	
132	# Splitters between co-attributed organizations in a run.
133	_ORG_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"\s+(?:and|&)\s+|\s*[,;/]\s*", re.IGNORECASE)
134	
135	
136	def _significant_words(phrase: str) -> list[str]:
137	    """Capitalized / acronym words in ``phrase`` (drops lowercase connectors)."""
138	    out: list[str] = []
139	    for tok in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9'’&.\-]*", phrase):
140	        low = tok.lower().strip(".'’&-")
141	        if not low:
142	            continue
143	        # Keep a token as significant when it is Capitalized or ALL-CAPS
144	        # (connectors like "of"/"and"/"the" are lowercase -> dropped).
145	        if tok[0].isupper():
146	            out.append(tok)
147	    return out
148	
149	
150	def _acronym_of(phrase: str) -> str:
151	    """First-letter acronym over the significant words of ``phrase`` (lowercased).
152	
153	    "International Labour Organization" -> "ilo"; "World Health Organization" ->
154	    "who"; "Poland's National Research Institute" -> "pnri"; "Bureau of Labor
155	    Statistics" -> "bls" (the connector "of" is skipped).
156	    """
157	    letters = [w[0].lower() for w in _significant_words(phrase) if w[:1].isalpha()]
158	    return "".join(letters)
159	
160	
161	def _distinctive_words(phrase: str) -> set[str]:
162	    """Org-specific content words (>=4 chars, minus generic org words)."""
163	    out: set[str] = set()
164	    for w in _significant_words(phrase):
165	        low = re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", w.lower())
166	        if len(low) >= 4 and low not in _GENERIC_ORG_WORDS:
167	            out.add(low)
168	    return out
169	
170	
171	def _phrase_is_org(phrase: str) -> bool:
172	    """A run qualifies as an ORGANIZATIONAL finder when it self-declares as an
173	    institution (org keyword) OR carries a 3-6-letter ALL-CAPS acronym token."""
174	    lowered = {re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", w.lower()) for w in _significant_words(phrase)}
175	    if lowered & _ORG_KEYWORDS:
176	        return True
177	    return bool(_ALLCAPS_ACRONYM_RE.search(phrase))
178	
179	
180	def _actor_signatures(run: str) -> tuple[set[str], set[str]]:
181	    """Build the (acronyms, distinctive_words) match signature for a finder run.
182	
183	    Splits the run on inter-org connectors ("and"/"&"/","/";") into per-org
184	    pieces AND also keeps the WHOLE-run acronym as an extra candidate, so an
185	    org whose own name contains "and" ("Food and Drug Administration" -> "fda",
186	    "Centers for Disease Control and Prevention") is still matched by its true
187	    acronym even though the split mangled it. Only ORG-qualifying pieces (or the
188	    whole run when it qualifies) contribute; a signature key is used only when it
189	    is discriminating (acronym >=3 chars, distinctive word >=4 chars).
190	    """
191	    acronyms: set[str] = set()
192	    distinctive: set[str] = set()
193	
194	    pieces = [p.strip() for p in _ORG_SPLIT_RE.split(run) if p and p.strip()]
195	    any_org = False
196	    for piece in pieces:
197	        if not _phrase_is_org(piece):
198	            continue
199	        any_org = True
200	        acr = _acronym_of(piece)
201	        if len(acr) >= 3:
202	            acronyms.add(acr)
203	        distinctive |= _distinctive_words(piece)
204	        # Standalone ALL-CAPS acronym tokens are themselves keys ("WHO"->who).
205	        for m in _ALLCAPS_ACRONYM_RE.finditer(piece):
206	            acronyms.add(m.group(0).lower())
207	
208	    # Whole-run acronym rescue for internal-"and" org names.
209	    if _phrase_is_org(run):
210	        any_org = True
211	        whole = _acronym_of(run)
212	        if 3 <= len(whole) <= 8:
213	            acronyms.add(whole)
214	        distinctive |= _distinctive_words(run)
215	
216	    if not any_org:
217	        return set(), set()
218	    return acronyms, distinctive
219	
220	
221	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
222	# Cited-source publisher identity (domain-only).
223	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
224	# TLD / opaque host labels stripped when reducing a domain to its publisher
225	# identity token(s). After stripping, an EMPTY signature means the publisher
226	# cannot be determined from the URL (bare gov TLD, a DOI resolver, an opaque
227	# aggregator) -> the guard stays INERT for that source (fail-open).
228	_HOST_STOP_LABELS = frozenset({
229	    # generic + country TLDs
230	    "com", "org", "net", "int", "edu", "gov", "io", "co", "info", "biz",
231	    "uk", "us", "ca", "au", "eu", "de", "fr", "es", "it", "nl", "se", "no",
232	    "pl", "ru", "cn", "jp", "in", "br", "za", "ch", "be", "at", "dk", "fi",
233	    "ie", "nz", "mx", "kr", "sg", "hk", "tw", "gr", "pt", "cz", "ro", "hu",
234	    # non-identifying subdomain labels
235	    "www", "www2", "www3", "web", "en", "m", "mobile", "amp",
236	    # opaque resolvers / aggregators (publisher identity not in the host)
237	    "doi", "dx", "ncbi", "pubmed", "pmc", "arxiv", "ssrn", "researchgate",
238	    "jstor", "semanticscholar", "biorxiv", "medrxiv", "preprints", "zenodo",
239	    "figshare", "scholar", "sci", "hub",
240	})
241	
242	
243	def _domain_signature(url: str) -> set[str]:
244	    """Publisher-identity token set from a URL's host, or empty if opaque.
245	
246	    "https://unric.org/en/..." -> {"unric"}; "https://ilo.org/..." -> {"ilo"};
247	    "https://www.who.int/..." -> {"who"}; "https://bls.gov/..." -> {"bls"};
248	    "https://gov.pl/..." -> set() (bare gov TLD -> opaque); "https://doi.org/10..."
249	    -> set() (resolver -> opaque).
250	    """
251	    if not url:
252	        return set()
253	    host = re.sub(r"^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9+.\-]*://", "", url.strip())
254	    host = host.split("/", 1)[0].split("?", 1)[0].split("#", 1)[0]
255	    host = host.split("@")[-1].split(":", 1)[0].lower()
256	    if not host:
257	        return set()
258	    labels = [lbl for lbl in host.split(".") if lbl]
259	    sig = {lbl for lbl in labels if lbl not in _HOST_STOP_LABELS and len(lbl) >= 2}
260	    return sig
261	
262	
263	def _actor_matches_domain(
264	    acronyms: set[str], distinctive: set[str], domain_sig: set[str]
265	) -> bool:
266	    """Whether the finder signature is present in a cited source's domain.
267	
268	    Lenient toward a MATCH (precision on the fires we DO make): an acronym key
269	    matches a domain token when either contains the other; a distinctive word
270	    matches when either contains the other. If NO cited source matches, the
271	    disclosure fires.
272	    """
273	    for tok in domain_sig:
274	        for acr in acronyms:
275	            if acr == tok or acr in tok or tok in acr:
276	                return True
277	        for word in distinctive:
278	            if word == tok or word in tok or tok in word:
279	                return True
280	    return False
281	
282	
283	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
284	# Public leg API. Returns a DISCLOSURE reason string (soft-warning) or None.
285	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
286	def attribution_origin_reason(claim: str, cited_source_urls: list[str]) -> str | None:
287	    """Return a DISCLOSURE reason if the claim names an organizational finder that
288	    none of the cited sources' publisher domains match.
289	
290	    Fires only when ALL hold (HIGH-PRECISION, FAIL-OPEN on ambiguity):
291	      - the leg is enabled (PG_ATTRIBUTION_ORIGIN_GUARD, default ON),
292	      - the claim names a recognizable ORGANIZATIONAL finder (org-keyword phrase
293	        or a 3-6-letter ALL-CAPS acronym) immediately before an attribution verb,
294	      - EVERY cited source has a determinable publisher domain (no opaque host —
295	        an opaque host makes us unable to rule out that IT is the actor -> inert),
296	      - and NONE of those domains matches the finder's acronym / distinctive-word
297	        signature.
298	    Otherwise returns None (inert — no false disclosure). DISCLOSURE-only: the
299	    caller APPENDS a soft-warning; this NEVER drops a sentence, NEVER changes a
300	    verdict, NEVER touches strict_verify / NLI / D8 (§-1.3).
301	    """
302	    if not attribution_origin_guard_enabled():
303	        return None
304	    if not claim or not cited_source_urls:
305	        return None
306	
307	    domain_sigs = [_domain_signature(u) for u in cited_source_urls]
308	    # FAIL-OPEN: if any cited source's publisher identity is opaque (empty
309	    # signature — bare TLD / resolver / aggregator), we cannot assert it is NOT
310	    # the finder. Stay inert.
311	    if any(not sig for sig in domain_sigs):
312	        return None
313	
314	    fired_actors: list[str] = []
315	    for m in _FINDER_RE.finditer(claim):
316	        run = m.group(1).strip()
317	        acronyms, distinctive = _actor_signatures(run)
318	        if not acronyms and not distinctive:
319	            continue  # no discriminating signature -> ambiguous -> skip (fail-open)
320	        matched = any(
321	            _actor_matches_domain(acronyms, distinctive, sig) for sig in domain_sigs
322	        )
323	        if not matched:
324	            fired_actors.append(run)
325	
326	    if not fired_actors:
327	        return None
328	
329	    actors = "|".join(dict.fromkeys(a[:80] for a in fired_actors))
330	    domains = ",".join(
331	        dict.fromkeys(sorted(t for sig in domain_sigs for t in sig))
332	    )[:120]
333	    return f"attribution_origin_unverified:actors={actors}:cited_domains={domains}"

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 P8_chrome_leak (#1344) — render-seam block-page / security-check / copyright-footer
2	chrome STRIPPER (presentation-only, §-1.3-safe).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	---------------
6	The drb_72 workforce report.md body rendered a ResearchGate / Cloudflare BLOCK PAGE (bibliography
7	source ``[27]`` = ``ev_146``, ``source_title`` "Just a moment...", tier T7) as two verified-looking
8	body sentences::
9	
10	    ... To continue, complete the security check below.[27] Ray ID: a160331f3c7dd701 Client IP:
11	    2600:1900:0:2101::1100 (c) 2008-2026 ResearchGate GmbH.[27] Our analysis identifies nine ...
12	
13	The block-page text was captured as a corpus source, its span verbatim-copied into the composed
14	prose, and it passed strict_verify through the SELF-CITATION hole (the sentence == the cited span,
15	so the numeric / content-overlap / entailment checks trivially hold).
16	
17	WHY THE EXISTING SCREENS MISSED IT
18	----------------------------------
19	The whole-page render-chrome predicate (``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable``,
20	called at the I-wire-013 render-seam chokepoint via ``sanitize_rendered_report``) catches the FULL
21	block-page text, but is BLIND to each SINGLE sentence once ``split_into_sentences`` fractures the
22	page at compose / render time. Proven against the run data: the joined page returns ``True`` while
23	"To continue, complete the security check below." and "Ray ID: ... (c) 2008-2026 ResearchGate GmbH."
24	each return ``False``. This is the I-wire-013 blind-predicate class re-appearing at SENTENCE
25	granularity, for the specific bot-challenge / copyright-footer vocabulary the containment predicate
26	does not enumerate.
27	
28	POSTURE (binding)
29	-----------------
30	* PRESENTATION-ONLY / faithfulness-NEUTRAL (§-1.3): runs at the render seam over the FULLY-ASSEMBLED
31	  ``report.md`` string, AFTER the frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 /
32	  provenance / span-grounding) and AFTER ``sanitize_rendered_report``. It only WITHHOLDS a block-page
33	  chrome SENTENCE from the rendered prose. It drops NO source (the ``[27]`` bibliography row + the
34	  corpus-credibility disclosure stay untouched), rewrites NO real claim, and touches NO verdict.
35	  It never relaxes the faithfulness engine — it removes non-content chrome, never a real claim.
36	* HIGH-PRECISION (precision-first): every HARD marker is a bot-challenge / security-verification /
37	  Cloudflare phrase that never appears in a real labor / economics / clinical finding OR in POLARIS
38	  disclosure prose. The copyright-footer rule is triple-gated (copyright mark + rights/entity signal
39	  + dominance) so a real claim that merely cites a license in passing is KEPT. Over-stripping a real
40	  finding is the harm this guards against, not a leaked chrome line.
41	* FAIL-SAFE: a sentence is dropped only when it IS block-page chrome; a real finding welded into the
42	  SAME line is preserved (line-scoped sentence partition). Segmentation is an exact character
43	  partition (``"".join(segments) == line``), so a rejoin can never corrupt real prose. Structural
44	  lines (headers, table rows, code fences, rules) are byte-preserved. If dropping would empty a
45	  line, only the line's leading structure prefix (bullet / number) is kept.
46	* DETERMINISTIC + PURE: stdlib only (``os`` / ``re``), no LLM, no network, no shared-module import
47	  (fully isolated). ``snake_case`` (LAW V). LAW VI kill-switch ``PG_BLOCK_PAGE_CHROME_SCRUB``
48	  (default ON); set to an off token to render byte-identically.
49	"""
50	
51	from __future__ import annotations
52	
53	import os
54	import re
55	
56	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57	# LAW VI kill-switch.
58	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	_SCRUB_ENV = "PG_BLOCK_PAGE_CHROME_SCRUB"
60	_OFF_TOKENS = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no"})
61	
62	# I-deepfix-001 B4-render #13 (#1344): a SECOND, independently-gated leg that reuses the shared
63	# render-chrome detector (``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable``) at SENTENCE
64	# granularity. The whole-report ``sanitize_rendered_report`` runs that same detector but per
65	# CITATION UNIT — so a single chrome SENTENCE welded into a real-prose line (a masthead / editor-
66	# affiliation / raw markdown-link / bibliographic-recital / DOI-only / source-internal "[N]" marker /
67	# OCR title fragment) is invisible to it because the unit as a whole is not chrome. Running the
68	# containment detector per partitioned sentence unblinds exactly that glued-mid-prose class, in the
69	# report BODY and in the §8 basket labels (the scrub walks the whole assembled report, and §8 labels
70	# render as ``-`` bullets, not structural lines). This REUSES the proven, precision-first detector
71	# rather than re-enumerating chrome vocabulary here. LAW VI sub-kill-switch
72	# ``PG_BLOCK_PAGE_CHROME_SCRUB_SHARED`` (default ON); OFF reverts to the pre-#13 hard-marker +
73	# copyright-footer behaviour byte-identically. Fail-safe: any import/detector error => this leg is
74	# inert (never drops on a fault), so the module stays isolated in practice.
75	_SHARED_DETECTOR_ENV = "PG_BLOCK_PAGE_CHROME_SCRUB_SHARED"
76	
77	
78	def block_page_chrome_scrub_enabled() -> bool:
79	    """Return True iff ``PG_BLOCK_PAGE_CHROME_SCRUB`` is not an off token (default ON = scrub)."""
80	    raw = os.environ.get(_SCRUB_ENV)
81	    if raw is None or not str(raw).strip():
82	        return True
83	    return str(raw).strip().lower() not in _OFF_TOKENS
84	
85	
86	def shared_render_chrome_leg_enabled() -> bool:
87	    """Return True iff the #13 shared-detector sentence leg is active (default ON). OFF token in
88	    ``PG_BLOCK_PAGE_CHROME_SCRUB_SHARED`` reverts to the hard-marker + copyright-footer-only screen."""
89	    raw = os.environ.get(_SHARED_DETECTOR_ENV)
90	    if raw is None or not str(raw).strip():
91	        return True
92	    return str(raw).strip().lower() not in _OFF_TOKENS
93	
94	
95	# PRECISION ESCAPE (mirrors the copyright triple-gate's "a real claim that cites a license is KEPT"
96	# posture): a sentence carrying a substantive QUANTITATIVE FINDING signal — a percent (digit or
97	# spelled), a magnitude figure (N million/billion/…), or a currency amount — is a real claim that
98	# may merely CITE page-furniture (a CC-license, a publisher) in passing. The shared containment
99	# detector (built for whole-UNIT render-seam decisions) over-fires on such a claim per sentence, so
100	# the escape defers to strict_verify's already-passed verdict. Page-furniture chrome (editor /
101	# masthead / ORCID / bibliographic recital) never carries a quantitative finding, so the escape
102	# never re-admits a real chrome leak. Over-strip of a real finding is the harm this guards against.
103	_SUBSTANTIVE_CLAIM_RE = re.compile(
104	    r"\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:%|percent(?:age)?\b|per\s*cent\b)"
105	    r"|\b(?:one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten|eleven|twelve|thirteen|fourteen|"
106	    r"fifteen|sixteen|seventeen|eighteen|nineteen|twenty|thirty|forty|fifty|sixty|seventy|"
107	    r"eighty|ninety|hundred)\s+(?:percent|per\s*cent)\b"
108	    r"|\b\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?\s*(?:million|billion|trillion|thousand)\b"
109	    r"|[$€£¥]\s?\d",
110	    re.IGNORECASE,
111	)
112	
113	
114	def _looks_like_substantive_quant_claim(sentence: str) -> bool:
115	    """True iff the sentence carries a substantive quantitative finding signal (percent / magnitude /
116	    currency) — a real claim the precision escape keeps even if it cites chrome in passing. PURE."""
117	    return bool(_SUBSTANTIVE_CLAIM_RE.search(sentence))
118	
119	
120	def _is_shared_render_chrome(sentence: str) -> bool:
121	    """True iff the shared render-chrome detector flags this SINGLE sentence as page-furniture
122	    chrome AND the sentence is not a substantive quantitative claim (precision escape). Fail-safe:
123	    returns False (never a drop) on ANY import/detection error, so a fault in the shared module can
124	    only ever LEAK chrome, never over-strip a real finding. Gated by the caller under
125	    ``shared_render_chrome_leg_enabled()``."""
126	    if _looks_like_substantive_quant_claim(sentence):
127	        return False  # a real quantitative finding — keep it even if it cites a license/publisher
128	    try:
129	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
130	            is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable,
131	        )
132	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover — weighted_enrichment is stable in-tree
133	        return False
134	    try:
135	        return bool(is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(sentence))
136	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover — detector is pure in-tree
137	        return False
138	
139	
140	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
141	# HARD block-page / bot-challenge / security-verification markers. Each is a challenge-PAGE-specific
142	# phrase (Cloudflare / DataDome / PerimeterX / hCaptcha / ResearchGate interstitial) that never
143	# appears in a real finding OR in POLARIS disclosure/audit prose. A sentence containing ANY hard
144	# marker is block-page chrome and is withheld unconditionally. Lower-cased for a case-insensitive
145	# substring test.
146	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147	_HARD_MARKERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
148	    "complete the security check",
149	    "security check required",
150	    "checking your browser before",
151	    "checking if the site connection is secure",
152	    "detected unusual activity from your network",
153	    "unusual traffic from your",
154	    "performing security verification",
155	    "verifying you are not a bot",
156	    "verify you are a human",
157	    "verify you are human",
158	    "are you a robot",
159	    "enable javascript and cookies to continue",
160	    "please enable cookies and reload",
161	    "this process is automatic. your browser will redirect",
162	    "ddos protection by",
163	    "why do i have to complete a captcha",
164	    "captcha challenge",
165	    "please complete the captcha",
166	    "access to this page has been denied",
167	    "ray id:",                      # Cloudflare error / challenge footer "Ray ID: <hex>"
168	    "cf-ray",                       # Cloudflare response-header / footer token
169	    "cf_chl_",                      # Cloudflare challenge cookie/query token
170	)
171	
172	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	# Copyright-footer SOFT rule — triple-gated so a real claim that cites a license is KEPT.
174	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175	# (a) a copyright MARK adjacent to a year (single year or a "2008-2026" range).
176	_COPYRIGHT_MARK_YEAR_RE = re.compile(
177	    r"(?:©|\(c\)|copyright\s*(?:©|\(c\))?)\s*"
178	    r"\d{4}(?:\s*[-‐-―/]\s*\d{2,4})?",
179	    re.IGNORECASE,
180	)
181	# (b) a rights / legal-entity signal that co-occurs in a footer (never in a running claim).
182	_RIGHTS_ENTITY_RE = re.compile(
183	    r"all\s+rights\s+reserved"
184	    r"|\b(?:gmbh|inc\.?|llc|ltd\.?|b\.?v\.?|s\.?a\.?|s\.?l\.?|plc|pty|s\.?r\.?l\.?)\b"
185	    r"|\bcorp(?:oration)?\b|\bincorporated\b|\bpublish(?:ing|ers)\b",
186	    re.IGNORECASE,
187	)
188	# (c) dominance guard: tokens that BELONG to a footer (not real content). After removing these plus
189	# the mark/year and the entity signal, a residue of <= _FOOTER_RESIDUE_CONTENT_FLOOR content words
190	# means the sentence is DOMINATED by the footer (a real claim carries far more content).
191	_FOOTER_RESIDUE_CONTENT_FLOOR = 4
192	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]{3,}")
193	_FOOTER_FILLER_WORDS = frozenset(
194	    {"copyright", "all", "rights", "reserved", "the", "and", "for", "inc", "ltd", "llc", "corp",
195	     "gmbh", "plc", "pty", "publishing", "publishers", "incorporated", "corporation"}
196	)
197	
198	
199	def _is_dominant_copyright_footer(sentence: str) -> bool:
200	    """True iff ``sentence`` is a DOMINANT copyright footer: a copyright mark next to a year AND a
201	    rights/entity signal AND (after removing the mark/year and footer-filler words) a residue of at
202	    most ``_FOOTER_RESIDUE_CONTENT_FLOOR`` real content words. Triple-gated: a real claim that merely
203	    mentions a copyright/publisher in passing carries real content and is KEPT (precision-first)."""
204	    if not _COPYRIGHT_MARK_YEAR_RE.search(sentence):
205	        return False
206	    if not _RIGHTS_ENTITY_RE.search(sentence):
207	        return False
208	    residue = _COPYRIGHT_MARK_YEAR_RE.sub(" ", sentence)
209	    content = [w for w in _WORD_RE.findall(residue) if w.lower() not in _FOOTER_FILLER_WORDS]
210	    return len(content) <= _FOOTER_RESIDUE_CONTENT_FLOOR
211	
212	
213	def is_block_page_chrome_sentence(sentence: str) -> bool:
214	    """True iff a SINGLE sentence unit is block-page / security-check / copyright-footer chrome:
215	    it contains a HARD bot-challenge marker, OR it is a DOMINANT copyright footer, OR — when the
216	    #13 shared leg is enabled — the shared render-chrome detector flags it as glued page-furniture
217	    (masthead / editor-affiliation / raw markdown-link / bibliographic-recital / DOI-only / OCR
218	    title fragment). High-precision: a real labor / economics / clinical finding trips none of the
219	    paths (precision-first per §-1.3)."""
220	    if not sentence or not sentence.strip():
221	        return False
222	    low = sentence.lower()
223	    for marker in _HARD_MARKERS:
224	        if marker in low:
225	            return True
226	    if _is_dominant_copyright_footer(sentence):
227	        return True
228	    if shared_render_chrome_leg_enabled() and _is_shared_render_chrome(sentence):
229	        return True
230	    return False
231	
232	
233	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
234	# Sentence partition — an EXACT character partition of a prose line (``"".join(segments) == line``)
235	# so a rejoin of the kept segments can never corrupt real prose. A period is a boundary only when it
236	# is NOT a decimal point (``(?<!\d)`` guards "12.3" / an IPv4 octet), may carry trailing ``[N]``
237	# citation tokens, and is followed by whitespace + a sentence-opening character (or end-of-line).
238	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
239	_SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
240	    r"(?<!\d)[.!?](?:\s*\[\d+\])*(?=\s+[A-Z(\[\"'“‘]|\s*$)"
241	)
242	
243	
244	def _partition_sentences(line: str) -> list[str]:
245	    """Partition ``line`` into sentence segments, each carrying its trailing inter-sentence
246	    whitespace, such that ``"".join(result) == line`` exactly."""
247	    segments: list[str] = []
248	    pos = 0
249	    for match in _SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE.finditer(line):
250	        end = match.end()
251	        j = end
252	        while j < len(line) and line[j].isspace():
253	            j += 1
254	        segments.append(line[pos:j])
255	        pos = j
256	    if pos < len(line):
257	        segments.append(line[pos:])
258	    return segments
259	
260	
261	_STRUCTURAL_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^(\s*(?:[-*+]\s+|>\s+|\d+[.)]\s+)*)")
262	_FENCE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:```|~~~)")
263	_RULE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:-{3,}|\*{3,}|_{3,})\s*$")
264	
265	
266	def _is_structural_line(line: str) -> bool:
267	    """True iff ``line`` is a markdown STRUCTURE line that must be byte-preserved (a heading, a table
268	    row, a code fence, or a horizontal rule). Blank lines are handled by the caller."""
269	    stripped = line.lstrip()
270	    if not stripped:
271	        return False
272	    if stripped.startswith("#"):
273	        return True
274	    if stripped.startswith("|"):
275	        return True
276	    if _FENCE_RE.match(line):
277	        return True
278	    if _RULE_RE.match(line):
279	        return True
280	    return False
281	
282	
283	def _leading_structure(line: str) -> str:
284	    """Return the line's leading whitespace + list/quote marker prefix (e.g. ``"- "``, ``"  1. "``),
285	    so a fully-chrome line keeps its bullet skeleton rather than a dangling stub."""
286	    m = _STRUCTURAL_PREFIX_RE.match(line)
287	    return m.group(1) if m else ""
288	
289	
290	def _scrub_line(line: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
291	    """Scrub block-page chrome sentences from a single prose ``line``. Returns ``(new_line,
292	    dropped_count)``. FAIL-SAFE: a line with no flagged sentence returns byte-identically; a rejoin
293	    only concatenates KEPT segment slices (each already carrying its own single trailing space), so
294	    real prose is preserved with normal single spacing."""
295	    segments = _partition_sentences(line)
296	    if not segments:
297	        return line, 0
298	    flagged = [i for i, seg in enumerate(segments) if is_block_page_chrome_sentence(seg)]
299	    if not flagged:
300	        return line, 0
301	    kept = [seg for i, seg in enumerate(segments) if i not in set(flagged)]
302	    if not any(seg.strip() for seg in kept):
303	        # Whole line was block-page chrome — keep only the structural prefix (bullet / number) so a
304	        # list item is not left dangling; the chrome text is removed.
305	        return _leading_structure(line).rstrip(), len(flagged)
306	    new_line = "".join(kept).rstrip()
307	    return new_line, len(flagged)
308	
309	
310	def scrub_block_page_chrome(report_md: str, *, enabled: bool | None = None) -> tuple[str, int]:
311	    """Strip block-page / security-check / copyright-footer chrome sentences from a fully-assembled
312	    ``report.md`` string. Line-scoped (structural lines byte-preserved) and presentation-only.
313	
314	    Returns ``(scrubbed_report_md, dropped_sentence_count)``. When the scrub is disabled, or the
315	    input is empty, or nothing is flagged, the input is returned byte-identically (``dropped == 0``).
316	    """
317	    if enabled is None:
318	        enabled = block_page_chrome_scrub_enabled()
319	    if not report_md or not enabled:
320	        return report_md, 0
321	    out_lines: list[str] = []
322	    dropped = 0
323	    for line in report_md.split("\n"):
324	        if not line.strip() or _is_structural_line(line):
325	            out_lines.append(line)
326	            continue
327	        new_line, count = _scrub_line(line)
328	        dropped += count
329	        out_lines.append(new_line)
330	    if not dropped:
331	        return report_md, 0
332	    return "\n".join(out_lines), dropped

1	"""B2 (I-deepfix-001 #1344) — per-section boundary-conditions / counter-evidence line.
2	
3	DeepTRACE one-sided #1 · DRB-II analysis · Telus clinical balance. The existing both-sides render
4	fires ONLY when a refuter CLUSTER exists (``refuter_cluster_ids``), which consolidation rarely
5	produces — so a headline claim renders with no opposing caveat or boundary even when the weighted
6	corpus already holds a LOWER-WEIGHT source that qualifies or bounds it.
7	
8	THE FIX (this module, pure): for a section's headline baskets, synthesize ONE rendered
9	"Boundary conditions / counter-evidence" line drawn from LOWER-WEIGHT baskets that qualify or bound
10	the headline — a low-weight source that dissents or narrows the claim STAYS and is surfaced AT ITS
11	WEIGHT (WEIGHT-IN, not filter-out). The line quotes the lower-weight basket's OWN span-verified
12	member text (faithful-by-quotation — the same span already passed isolated verification), attributed
13	to its source; it NEVER fabricates opposition and fires even without a refuter cluster.
14	
15	§-1.3 DNA — surfaces opposition ALREADY PRESENT in the weighted corpus; adds nothing, drops nothing,
16	invents nothing. A qualifying basket is one that (a) shares >=2 content words with the headline
17	(same topic), (b) carries strictly LOWER ``weight_mass`` than the headline, and (c) either is
18	referenced as a refuter OR its claim carries a boundary/qualifier marker ("only", "except",
19	"in patients with", "when", threshold wording). Faithfulness is untouched: the line is a marker-less
20	disclosure appended AFTER strict_verify, quoting an already-verified span; it is never a new claim
21	fed to the faithfulness engine. PURE / offline. LAW VI kill-switch. snake_case.
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	import re
27	from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional
28	
29	_ENV_FLAG = "PG_SECTION_BOUNDARY_CONDITIONS"
30	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
31	_MIN_OVERLAP_ENV = "PG_SECTION_BOUNDARY_MIN_OVERLAP"
32	_DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP = 2
33	
34	# Boundary / qualifier markers — a claim carrying one is a candidate to BOUND a headline. Overridable
35	# via config (comma-separated PG_SECTION_BOUNDARY_MARKERS) so it is not a hard-coded constant (LAW VI).
36	_DEFAULT_MARKERS = (
37	    "only", "except", "unless", "however", "but not", "limited to", "restricted to",
38	    "in patients with", "in adults with", "when ", "if ", "provided that", "conditional on",
39	    "no significant", "did not", "failed to", "not associated", "no difference", "no benefit",
40	    "at doses", "above ", "below ", "at least", "greater than", "less than", "subgroup",
41	    "baseline", "threshold", "contraindicated", "caution",
42	)
43	
44	_CONTENT_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9\-]{2,}")
45	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
46	    "the", "and", "for", "that", "this", "with", "from", "have", "has", "had", "was", "were",
47	    "are", "been", "will", "would", "could", "should", "their", "there", "these", "those",
48	    "which", "while", "when", "where", "what", "who", "whom", "into", "than", "then", "such",
49	    "also", "not", "but", "its", "our", "your", "his", "her", "they", "them", "some", "more",
50	    "most", "over", "under", "between", "among", "per", "via", "about", "each", "any", "all",
51	    "may", "can", "does", "did", "only",
52	})
53	
54	
55	def boundary_conditions_enabled() -> bool:
56	    """B2 kill-switch. Default ON; OFF => no boundary line synthesized (byte-identical revert)."""
57	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
58	
59	
60	def _min_overlap() -> int:
61	    try:
62	        return max(1, int(os.environ.get(_MIN_OVERLAP_ENV, str(_DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP)).strip()))
63	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
64	        return _DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP
65	
66	
67	def _markers() -> tuple:
68	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SECTION_BOUNDARY_MARKERS", "").strip()
69	    if raw:
70	        return tuple(m.strip().lower() for m in raw.split(",") if m.strip())
71	    return _DEFAULT_MARKERS
72	
73	
74	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
75	    return {
76	        w.lower() for w in _CONTENT_WORD_RE.findall(text or "")
77	        if w.lower() not in _STOPWORDS
78	    }
79	
80	
81	def _basket_field(basket: Any, name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
82	    if isinstance(basket, dict):
83	        return basket.get(name, default)
84	    return getattr(basket, name, default)
85	
86	
87	def _basket_weight(basket: Any) -> float:
88	    try:
89	        return float(_basket_field(basket, "weight_mass", 0.0) or 0.0)
90	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
91	        return 0.0
92	
93	
94	def _basket_claim_text(basket: Any) -> str:
95	    txt = str(_basket_field(basket, "claim_text", "") or "").strip()
96	    if txt:
97	        return txt
98	    subj = str(_basket_field(basket, "subject", "") or "").strip()
99	    pred = str(_basket_field(basket, "predicate", "") or "").strip()
100	    return f"{subj} {pred}".strip()
101	
102	
103	def _supports_members(basket: Any) -> list:
104	    out = []
105	    for m in _basket_field(basket, "supporting_members", None) or []:
106	        verdict = str(
107	            (m.get("span_verdict") if isinstance(m, dict) else getattr(m, "span_verdict", "")) or ""
108	        ).upper()
109	        if verdict == "SUPPORTS":
110	            out.append(m)
111	    return out
112	
113	
114	def _member_quote(member: Any) -> str:
115	    return str(
116	        (member.get("direct_quote") if isinstance(member, dict)
117	         else getattr(member, "direct_quote", "")) or ""
118	    ).strip()
119	
120	
121	def _member_source_label(member: Any) -> str:
122	    url = str(
123	        (member.get("source_url") if isinstance(member, dict)
124	         else getattr(member, "source_url", "")) or ""
125	    ).strip()
126	    eid = str(
127	        (member.get("evidence_id") if isinstance(member, dict)
128	         else getattr(member, "evidence_id", "")) or ""
129	    ).strip()
130	    tier = str(
131	        (member.get("source_tier") if isinstance(member, dict)
132	         else getattr(member, "source_tier", "")) or ""
133	    ).strip()
134	    label = url or eid or "source"
135	    return f"{label}" + (f" (tier {tier})" if tier else "")
136	
137	
138	def _carries_boundary_marker(text: str) -> bool:
139	    low = (text or "").lower()
140	    return any(m in low for m in _markers())
141	
142	
143	def find_qualifying_lower_weight_basket(
144	    headline_basket: Any,
145	    candidate_baskets: Iterable[Any],
146	) -> Optional[Any]:
147	    """The best LOWER-WEIGHT basket that qualifies/bounds ``headline_basket`` — or None. PURE.
148	
149	    A candidate qualifies iff it shares >= min-overlap content words with the headline, has strictly
150	    lower ``weight_mass``, carries a span-verified (SUPPORTS) member, and either is referenced by the
151	    headline's ``refuter_cluster_ids`` OR its claim carries a boundary/qualifier marker. Ties break by
152	    (max content overlap, then lowest weight) for determinism. Never returns the headline itself.
153	    """
154	    headline_words = _content_words(_basket_claim_text(headline_basket))
155	    if not headline_words:
156	        return None
157	    headline_weight = _basket_weight(headline_basket)
158	    headline_ccid = str(_basket_field(headline_basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
159	    refuter_ids = {str(c) for c in (_basket_field(headline_basket, "refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ())}
160	    min_ov = _min_overlap()
161	
162	    best = None
163	    best_key: tuple = (-1, 0.0)
164	    for cand in candidate_baskets or ():
165	        ccid = str(_basket_field(cand, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
166	        if ccid and ccid == headline_ccid:
167	            continue
168	        if _basket_weight(cand) >= headline_weight:
169	            continue  # must be strictly LOWER weight
170	        supports = _supports_members(cand)
171	        if not supports:
172	            continue  # can only surface a span-verified (faithful) quote
173	        claim = _basket_claim_text(cand)
174	        overlap = len(headline_words & _content_words(claim))
175	        if overlap < min_ov:
176	            continue
177	        is_refuter = bool(ccid and ccid in refuter_ids)
178	        if not is_refuter and not _carries_boundary_marker(claim):
179	            continue
180	        key = (overlap, -_basket_weight(cand))  # more overlap, then lower weight, wins
181	        if key > best_key:
182	            best_key = key
183	            best = cand
184	    return best
185	
186	
187	def _boundary_quote_hygiene_enabled() -> bool:
188	    """``PG_BOUNDARY_QUOTE_HYGIENE`` kill-switch (default ON, LAW VI). OFF => the boundary quote is emitted
189	    unscreened (byte-identical to legacy). ON => a truncated / glued / render-chrome candidate quote is
190	    SKIPPED (the section falls through to the next candidate or emits nothing) so a broken 'graduatio...'
191	    fragment never renders as counter-evidence."""
192	    return os.getenv("PG_BOUNDARY_QUOTE_HYGIENE", "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
193	
194	
195	def _boundary_quote_hygiene_v2_enabled() -> bool:
196	    """N1-FIX-2 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2) ``PG_BOUNDARY_QUOTE_HYGIENE_V2`` kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF —
197	    only ``1/true/on/yes`` enables. ON adds two hygiene rules: (a) `_quote_is_unrenderable` also rejects
198	    a markdown-link / URL-fragment quote (the cbsnews "ws.com/team/…[Add CBS News on Google](https://…"
199	    leak); (b) `synthesize_boundary_line` uses the ``" on {subject}"`` suffix ONLY when the headline
200	    subject carries >=2 content words (kills "on however:" / "on entry-:" / "on mid-:"). OFF =>
201	    byte-identical (neither rule fires)."""
202	    return os.getenv("PG_BOUNDARY_QUOTE_HYGIENE_V2", "").strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "on", "yes")
203	
204	
205	# N1-FIX-2: a markdown link (``[text](url)``) and a bare domain-path URL fragment (the first
206	# whitespace token of a leaked chrome quote, e.g. ``ws.com/team/megan-cerullo/``) — both are render
207	# chrome, never legitimate counter-evidence prose. Consulted ONLY under the V2 flag.
208	_MARKDOWN_LINK_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^\]]*\]\([^)]*\)")
209	_URL_FRAGMENT_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9.\-]*\.[A-Za-z]{2,}(?:/|$)")
210	
211	
212	def _quote_is_unrenderable(quote: str) -> bool:
213	    """I-deepfix-001 (#1369) STEP 4: True iff a boundary candidate quote is render-chrome OR a
214	    truncated/glued fragment that reads as broken (the 'graduatio...' mid-word cut). Conservative — only
215	    rejects clear artifacts; a complete verbatim sentence is never rejected. Over-rejection is safe (it
216	    only withholds an anti-signal line, never a fact). Pure; never raises."""
217	    q = (quote or "").strip()
218	    if not q:
219	        return True
220	    # N1-FIX-2 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): reject a markdown-link / URL-fragment quote (render chrome). Gated
221	    # on the V2 flag so V2-OFF is byte-identical to the #1369 STEP-4 predicate. The cbsnews leak
222	    # ("ws.com/team/megan-cerullo/) … [Add CBS News on Google](https://www.goog") ends on a non-alpha
223	    # token so the mid-word check below never fired — its FIRST whitespace token is a bare domain-path.
224	    if _boundary_quote_hygiene_v2_enabled():
225	        if _MARKDOWN_LINK_RE.search(q):
226	            return True
227	        first_tok = q.split()[0] if q.split() else ""
228	        if "://" in first_tok or _URL_FRAGMENT_RE.match(first_tok):
229	            return True
230	    # An embedded blank line signals a GLUED multi-fragment span (the 'graduatio' class).
231	    if "\n\n" in q or "\n \n" in q:
232	        return True
233	    try:
234	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
235	            is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable,
236	        )
237	        if is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(q):
238	            return True
239	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — screen is advisory; never break the render on a probe fault
240	        pass
241	    # Ends MID-WORD: no sentence-terminal punctuation AND the final token is a bare lowercase word fragment
242	    # (>=6 letters) — the 'graduatio' cut. Conservative: a quote ending in punctuation or a short word passes.
243	    if q[-1] not in ".!?\"')]}%":
244	        toks = q.split()
245	        last = toks[-1] if toks else ""
246	        if last.isalpha() and last.islower() and len(last) >= 6:
247	            return True
248	    return False
249	
250	
251	def synthesize_boundary_line(
252	    headline_baskets: Iterable[Any],
253	    candidate_baskets: Iterable[Any],
254	) -> str:
255	    """Synthesize ONE per-section boundary-conditions / counter-evidence line, or "".
256	
257	    For the section's headline baskets (highest weight first), find the best qualifying lower-weight
258	    basket and quote its OWN span-verified member text, attributed to its source at its weight. Fires
259	    even without a refuter cluster. Returns "" when no qualifying lower-weight basket exists (caller
260	    appends nothing => byte-identical). Never fabricates — the quote is an already-verified span.
261	    """
262	    headlines = sorted(
263	        (b for b in (headline_baskets or [])),
264	        key=lambda b: -_basket_weight(b),
265	    )
266	    candidates = list(candidate_baskets or [])
267	    for headline in headlines:
268	        qualifier = find_qualifying_lower_weight_basket(headline, candidates)
269	        if qualifier is None:
270	            continue
271	        supports = _supports_members(qualifier)
272	        if not supports:
273	            continue
274	        member = supports[0]
275	        quote = _member_quote(member)
276	        if not quote:
277	            continue
278	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) STEP 4 anti-signal: skip a truncated / glued / chrome fragment (the
279	        # 'graduatio...' class) so it never renders as counter-evidence. Skipping falls through to the next
280	        # candidate; if none qualify the section emits nothing (byte-identical to no-qualifier).
281	        # N1-FIX-2: OR the V2 flag in so the V2 markdown/URL-fragment rules fire even if STEP-4 is off; when
282	        # BOTH flags are off neither predicate is consulted => byte-identical.
283	        if (_boundary_quote_hygiene_enabled() or _boundary_quote_hygiene_v2_enabled()) and _quote_is_unrenderable(quote):
284	            continue
285	        source = _member_source_label(member)
286	        subject = str(_basket_field(headline, "subject", "") or "").strip()
287	        # N1-FIX-2: the " on {subject}" suffix reads as chrome when the subject is a single garbage token
288	        # ("however", "entry-", "mid-"). Under V2, require the subject to carry >=2 content words; else
289	        # render the bare label. V2 OFF => legacy behaviour (any non-empty subject) => byte-identical.
290	        if _boundary_quote_hygiene_v2_enabled():
291	            topic = f" on {subject}" if len(_content_words(subject)) >= 2 else ""
292	        else:
293	            topic = f" on {subject}" if subject else ""
294	        return (
295	            f"\n\n**Boundary conditions / counter-evidence{topic}:** a lower-weight source qualifies "
296	            f"or bounds the headline above — \"{quote}\" ({source}). This opposing/limiting evidence "
297	            "is surfaced at its weight; weigh it against the headline."
298	        )
299	    return ""

1	"""I-wire-014 (#1334) FIX-B/C — shared deterministic page-furniture screen.
2	
3	The benchmarked-SAFE winner (``extended_deterministic`` whole-unit-collapse) on the

5	content_preserved_rate = 1.0. The decision is **whole-unit-collapse-ONLY**: furniture
6	tokens are stripped ONLY to TEST whether a unit is furniture-DOMINANT; if the residue is

64	    r"\bQR-Code\b",
65	    r"Literaturnachweis",
66	    r"Inhaltsverzeichnis",
67	    r"nur vorhanden,?\s+wenn\s+Javascript\s+eingeschaltet(?:\s+ist)?",
68	    r"Javascript\s+eingeschaltet",
69	]
70	_JOURNAL_HTML_RE = re.compile("|".join(_JOURNAL_HTML_RULES), re.IGNORECASE)
71	
72	_AFFIL_RULES = [
73	    r"Conflict of Interest Disclosure:[^.]*\.",
74	    r"\(Liquidator\)",
75	]
76	_AFFIL_RE = re.compile("|".join(_AFFIL_RULES), re.IGNORECASE)
77	
78	_WHAT_YOULL_LEARN = "What you" + "’" + "ll learn:"
79	_PAYWALL_RULES = [
80	    r"Member-only story",
81	    re.escape(_WHAT_YOULL_LEARN) + r"\s*(?:-\s*[^-]*)*",
82	    r"\bFeature Story\b(?:\s+By\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)?"
83	    r"(?:\s+Last update\s+[A-Z][a-z]+\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{4})?",
84	]
85	_PAYWALL_RE = re.compile("|".join(_PAYWALL_RULES), re.IGNORECASE)
86	
87	_COOKIE_RULES = [
88	    r"The content references language or the region that you are in[^.]*\.?",
89	    r"region that you are in",
90	    r"In order to better serve you and keep this site secure, please complete this challenge\.?",
91	    r"please complete this challenge",
92	    r"accept all cookies",
93	]
94	_COOKIE_RE = re.compile("|".join(_COOKIE_RULES), re.IGNORECASE)
95	
96	# I-deepfix-002 (#1363) FIX-1 — author/date byline furniture welded to the front of a fetched body
97	# (drb_72 "August 30, 2023 **[By Jim Jones]** ..." [8]; "_Written by Jim McGwin, College of Business_"
98	# [14]). Obeys the whole-unit-collapse over-strip guard (is_furniture_dominant: suppress ONLY when the
99	# post-strip residue has < 4 real words) — a pure byline LINE collapses; a byline prefix welded to a real
100	# multi-clause claim keeps its >=4-word residue and is returned UNCHANGED. "By <Name>" is bounded to <=3
101	# capitalized tokens so "...found that, by March 2024, employment..." is never matched.
102	_BYLINE_RULES = [
103	    r"(?:January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December)"
104	    r"\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{4}\s*\**\s*\[?\s*[Bb]y\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){0,2}\s*\]?(?:\([^)\s]*\))?\s*\**",
105	    r"_?\b(?:Written|Posted|Reviewed|Edited|Authored|Reported|Compiled)\s+[Bb]y\s+"
106	    r"[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){0,2}(?:,\s+[A-Z][^_\n]{0,40})?_?",
107	]
108	_BYLINE_RE = re.compile("|".join(_BYLINE_RULES))
109	
110	_FURNITURE_RES = [_JOURNAL_HTML_RE, _AFFIL_RE, _PAYWALL_RE, _COOKIE_RE, _BYLINE_RE]
111	
112	# A residue is "near-empty" if it has < this many alphabetic words of length>=2 (no real clause).
113	_NEAR_EMPTY_WORD_FLOOR = 4
114	
115	# Self-contained whole-line furniture: a LINE that, after furniture strip, is near-empty. Used by
116	# clean_fetch_body (whole-LINE removal only — never an inline partial strip of a welded fragment).
117	
118	
119	def _alpha_word_count(s: str) -> int:
120	    return len(re.findall(r"[^\W\d_]{2,}", s, re.UNICODE))
121	
122	
123	def _strip_furniture(text: str) -> str:
124	    s = text or ""
125	    for rx in _FURNITURE_RES:
126	        s = rx.sub(" ", s)
127	    s = re.sub(r"[ \t]{2,}", " ", s)
128	    s = re.sub(r"\s*[-*|]\s*(?=[-*|])", " ", s)
129	    return s.strip(" -*|\t")
130	
131	
132	def is_furniture_dominant(text: str) -> bool:
133	    """True iff ``text`` is page-furniture-DOMINANT (the benchmarked whole-unit-collapse decision):
134	    a furniture token was removed AND the residue is near-empty (< _NEAR_EMPTY_WORD_FLOOR real
135	    words). A real claim with a welded fragment keeps its residue and returns False (preserved by
136	    construction). PURE."""
137	    base = (text or "").strip()
138	    if not base:
139	        return False
140	    residue = _strip_furniture(base)
141	    return residue != base and _alpha_word_count(residue) < _NEAR_EMPTY_WORD_FLOOR
142	
143	
144	def is_self_contained_furniture_line(line: str) -> bool:
145	    """True iff a WHOLE LINE is furniture (for clean_fetch_body whole-line removal). Same
146	    whole-unit-collapse test applied per line; never an inline partial strip. PURE."""
147	    return is_furniture_dominant(line)
